name: Automatic Voter Registration Update | Fight For Reform
subject: Amy Klobuchar just announced!!
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=24170%2E4449941%2Eo5S2ob) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=24170%2E4449941%2Eo5S2ob) We emailed Thursday.
We emailed yesterday.
Now, we’re emailing again – that’s how critical this is.

[**Team, Amy Klobuchar just introduced an AMAZING new bill to support Automatic Voter Registration – it would destroy DECADES of Republican voter suppression efforts!!!**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73809?t=3&akid=24170%2E4449941%2Eo5S2ob)

[**We’re racing to support Sen. Klobuchar and pressure Congress to pass this bill, but it comes at a cost – and we’re running out of resources. Unless we make up our $74,583 shortage by 9 AM tomorrow, we’ll have to give up. Can we count on you to rush in $10 ASAP? >>**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73809?t=4&akid=24170%2E4449941%2Eo5S2ob) | [DONATE $10 NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73809?t=5&akid=24170%2E4449941%2Eo5S2ob)

We can’t let this bill fail – **and we especially can’t let Republican voter suppression win.**

[**So we’re asking you personally: Before 9 AM tomorrow, can you chip in just $10? >>**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/73809?t=7&akid=24170%2E4449941%2Eo5S2ob)

| | | Hi, it’s Jasmine Clark, and if I’m elected, I would be the first woman with a STEM PhD EVER to serve in Congress. Think about that: In the entire history of this country, no woman scientist has ever had a vote in our nation’s legislature. That is not acceptable. It is time to change that. To do it, I need your help. Republicans would rather elect extremist yes-men who are backed by corporate PACs and far-right groups — they’re terrified of a woman scientist holding them accountable. But I’m powered by grassroots support — people like you who believe facts still matter. **[We have an important fundraising deadline coming up, so I need your help. Please chip in now to help me make history and send a scientist to Congress.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130172751/616865796/-12840034?refcode=260425em-stem-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NjYwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MWQ3M2VmZS0wNDQxLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FchfJSip9HgpV1dcBmZKTH4xAbJOtr1SXGEfMOIy__M=)**

Randy grew up in California’s Central Valley, where families are living with some of the worst air pollution in America. He knows what it looks like when politicians put corporate polluters first, because he’s seen the impact on his own community.
And he’s running to stop them for good.
This primary will determine what kind of Democrat advances, and whether we send a true climate champion forward.
That’s why I’m asking:

# [The Men Stopping a Modern Day Slave Trade at the Arizona Border Offered Idaho a Blueprint That Works... but the Idaho's Sheriffs Association Went to Boise and Said "No Thanks"](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=195484784&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1NDg0Nzg0LCJpYXQiOjE3NzcxNjc0NDcsImV4cCI6MTc3OTc1OTQ0NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Y3V-nqHtLiEc0-5jBn5GnfpOnlRey2xGhKbRxy3YBIY)
### Two days at the Arizona-Mexico border with a room full of sheriffs from across the country who all flew in to learn from a model that works.
| | [Brian Lenney](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---
| Apr 26
---
| [](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=195484784&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1NDg0Nzg0LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3NzE2NzQ0NywiZXhwIjoxNzc5NzU5NDQ3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjExNDgyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.hqeQ8-Z-HauR6CCxUn-0QfZnUsJ8G6f3mjA3mHAJD_M&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/brianlenney/p/the-men-stopping-a-modern-day-slave?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

I just got back from two days at the Arizona-Mexico border with some of the most experienced law enforcement people in the country.
[The Federation for American Immigration Reform](https://substack.com/redirect/aed6091b-9fa4-40d2-87d7-d8cc350517a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) put the trip together. I bought my own plane ticket and went with a few other Idaho legislators. Sheriffs and law enforcement officers from across the country were there with us, all for the same reason: _somebody had to actually go see this rather than read about it in a press release._
Our shepherds for the trip were [Sheriff Mark Dannels](https://substack.com/redirect/1cff8c58-d0ec-444b-90b7-2ac6daae28c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Cochise County and his team, including Detective Cody Essary, who walked us through the desert personally and showed us what their surveillance network captures every single day. Sheriff Mark Lamb, formerly of Pinal County and now working with FAIR on border security full time, was there too, as well as [Art Del Cueto](https://substack.com/redirect/afbfc78e-0518-4d35-8c48-f3108241d14a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (who spent decades working the border). Between these men you're talking about decades of combined law enforcement experience and thousands of hours working this specific problem from ground level.
Every single one of them used the same words.
**Modern day slavery.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/50de0b06-ae98-4620-a889-2721bcbea651?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Walking along the Arizona/Mexico border
That's the operational reality as described by a 42-year law enforcement veteran who chairs border security for the National Sheriffs Association and sits on the Department of Homeland Security Advisory Council. Sheriff Dannels has personally briefed RFK Jr., Dr. Phil, Tom Homan. When he used those words it carried the weight of someone who's been watching this happen from 83 miles of the most trafficked border terrain in the country for over a decade.
I filmed what gets left behind.
The debris field out there tells the story better than any press release ever could. Physical evidence of human beings moved through that landscape like freight, because to the cartels running this operation, that's the accurate description of what they're doing.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/02ededcc-fa08-4650-836e-9efa79cbcccb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Cody told us how cartels charge $10,000 to $30,000 crossing fee for every single person who makes the trek.
He didn't get that number from a government report. He got it from the people themselves, in the field, in real time. He's worked that desert long enough to have seen this end every way it possibly can. He's been at scenes where people didn't make it out, where evidence of what they paid and what they still owed was scattered around them in the heat. When Cody describes what these crossings actually cost and what the debt does to people afterward, he's pulling from conversations he's personally had with human beings in the worst moments of their lives.
Here's what that debt actually means in practice:
_**The cartel controls every inch of the Mexican side of the border and nobody ... not ONE PERSON crosses without authorization. **_
Can't pay upfront? You cross on credit and that balance follows you into the interior. Now you're here illegally and owe $15,000 to an organization that knows where your family lives back home, and surfacing to any authority risks everything.
What happens next is pedictable...
_**You do whatever the cartel needs done.**_
Sex trafficking. Prostitution. Running drugs. Labor. Whatever they need that week, that's your life now because the cartel owns you and everyone you love. Don't comply? They know your mother's address back home. Your kids' school. Noncompliance isn't a negotiation, it's a death sentence, and sometimes they make an example out of the people closest to you first just to make sure you understand how serious they are.
And the debt never quite clears either, because something new always gets tacked on. These guys have watched this play out enough times that they can finish the story before you ask him how it ends.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/644b8f05-ebd4-498f-a068-1b7ea6ec701c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Sunset at the Arizona border
They walked us through the mechanics of the crossings too.
Cartel scouts sit on nearby mountains (pictured above) with binoculars guiding movement by radio. The crossers get outfitted in cartel-supplied camouflage with carpet shoes designed to wipe their tracks, and in some cases they drag blankets to erase the trail completely.
The [Cochise County SABRE program](https://substack.com/redirect/1cff8c58-d0ec-444b-90b7-2ac6daae28c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (Southeastern Arizona Border Regional Enforcement) runs over 1,000 hidden cameras throughout that desert, solar-powered and built into fake rocks so smugglers can't locate and destroy them. Dannels started this in 2017 with game cameras and rancher partnerships. Since then SABRE has detected over 116,000 illegal crossings and put roughly 437 drug smugglers in custody. All with only six deputies running the whole operation, fixing their own equipment in a garage because sending cameras to the manufacturer means weeks offline they simply can't absorb.
People who make it through get ferried to cartel stash houses in Phoenix and moved north into the American interior. Whatever the local network needs that week, 25 to 30 percent of everything they earn goes back south to the cartel. Sometimes the business owner genuinely has no idea because the manager is the cartel's person. In a lot of destination towns there's a restaurant or a storefront functioning as a cash collection point where workers hand over their cut.
The children are the hardest part to sit with.
They've documented around 5,000 cases of children being drugged and cycled through fake family units specifically because families move through processing more easily. They get recycled back into Mexico and inserted into a new fake family for another run. The cartels have no regard for human life and children in this system are a rentable asset, nothing more.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/86cbe4dd-89dc-41d2-b4e0-c4ee4f7cd131?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Years of building the political and legal case against enforcement created the operating environment the cartels needed.
The court filings, the sanctuary policies, the sustained campaign to make federal immigration enforcement as difficult as possible at the local level, all of it fed a system the cartels were happy to exploit.
Biden's autopen halted wall construction within an hour of taking office.
What followed was a dark and deliberate unwinding of everything that made that border functional (e.g. surveillance equipment going dark, processing centers absorbing the agents who should have been in the field), and Dannels standing there with six deputies and a camera network built from donated dollars because the federal government had functionally abandoned that desert.
So when you see a construction bid come in mysteriously low, undercutting every legitimate competitor, or produce on a shelf priced below what it honestly costs to grow, that gap has to come from somewhere.
_**It comes from someone with a cartel debt working with zero ability to complain to anyone about anything, ever. So when a business association lobbies against border enforcement and packages it as concern for workforce needs, understand what pipeline they 're actually running defense for.**_
Arizona has been trying to fight this for fifteen years.
[SB 1070](https://substack.com/redirect/83819160-0ceb-41f2-b3f2-ffdaaa4e9df5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) passed in 2010.
Governor Brewer signed it and Obama's DOJ filed suit almost immediately, taking it all the way to the Supreme Court. By 2012 three of the four main provisions were gone. One survived, a single requirement that officers check immigration status of people they've already lawfully arrested, out of everything the law originally contained.
Then in November 2024, Arizona voters passed [Proposition 314](https://substack.com/redirect/e9defa5c-0e79-4b68-a71a-20861315a046?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) directly at the ballot, going around a governor who'd vetoed every enforcement measure her legislature sent her. More carefully drafted this time, written around people caught in the act of crossing rather than anyone present in the state (it's contingent on a parallel Texas case surviving federal court and that fight isn't resolved). Fifteen years of work, and people dressing cartel protection up as civil rights advocacy have litigated most of it into nothing.
Just this month, Idaho had a chance to look at that history and do something with it.
But it didn't happen and this wasn't the first time.
I carried the harboring bills on the Senate side. [House Bill 764](https://substack.com/redirect/bc54a8da-9a82-4c6f-8735-1d1fb8bc8158?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) this session, along with nearly every other immigration enforcement bill in 2026, was mine or a bill I was directly involved in pushing.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1f12064a-79cd-45f3-b7dd-1df87ec2bb69?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
What these bills would have done is give Idaho law enforcement the same basic authority Arizona built its entire enforcement model around, making it a state crime to knowingly assist someone unlawfully present in the United States.
But the House Judiciary Committee killed HB 764 without a floor vote. [In 2025, our almost identical bill died in that same committee.](https://substack.com/redirect/1e662e1c-46a9-4e49-a1f2-3ce6e4bab4c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) The 2026 session wrapped up this month and every single immigration enforcement bill either failed or never got a hearing. Every one in a Republican supermajority, in one of the reddest states in the country.
The Idaho Sheriffs Association lobbied against these bills. ISA went into the capitol and argued against giving their own members tools that Sheriff Dannels has spent fifteen years refining, tools that drew sheriffs and law enforcement from across the country to Arizona to study firsthand.
That needs a straight answer...
[](https://substack.com/redirect/740d9270-e874-4bea-ad6c-cc549499c126?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
What exactly is ISA protecting here?
Dannels built SABRE with game cameras and rancher handshakes and it became a nationally recognized enforcement model that law enforcement from around the country fly in specifically to study. Lamb's years running anti-smuggling operations in Pinal County gave him a ground-level view of this pipeline that nobody sitting in a statehouse committee room is ever going to get from a briefing document. We stood in that desert alongside sheriffs and LEOs who flew in from across the country, and the reason every single one of them made the trip was sitting right in front of us the whole time.
_Idaho 's sheriffs association spent this session making sure their own members couldn't follow that lead. While I was carrying these bills in the Senate, ISA was in the building arguing the other direction._
That's worth saying clearly because coverage tends to miss it: morale at the border is genuinely high right now. Sheriff Dannels and everyone we interacted with said it directly.
_**The agents feel like they 're finally being allowed to do the job they signed up for.**_
The cartels still own the Mexican side of that fence though. A cartel scout sat on a mountain watching us through binoculars, same way he watched when Trump visited during the 2024 campaign. The machine kept running at lower volume, and cartels have a patience for this work that outlasts any administration. They're counting on the fact that legislatures don't have that same patience.
Idaho just burned another session.
Sixty miles of terrain in Cochise County alone sits without a wall. Steel posts intended for Trump's original build are stacked out in that desert right now. The window is open. Someone in our state needs to find the spine to do something permanent with it before the next cycle closes it again.
Anyone making the case for continued legislative inaction needs to explain, with some specificity, how their position differs from anyone who's ever looked at a slave trade and decided the economics were worth defending.
Still waiting on that answer from ISA.

clean_body: Two days at the Arizona-Mexico border with a room full of sheriffs from across the country who all flew in to learn from a model that works. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Two days at the Arizona-Mexico border with a room full of sheriffs from across the country who all flew in to learn from a model that works. | | | Apr 26 | | | | | | | | | | | | I just got back from two days at the Arizona-Mexico border with some of the most experienced law enforcement people in the country. put the trip together. I bought my own plane ticket and went with a few other Idaho legislators. Sheriffs and law enforcement officers from across the country were there with us, all for the same reason: somebody had to actually go see this rather than read about it in a press release. Our shepherds for the trip were of Cochise County and his team, including Detective Cody Essary, who walked us through the desert personally and showed us what their surveillance network captures every single day. Sheriff Mark Lamb, formerly of Pinal County and now working with FAIR on border security full time, was there too, as well as (who spent decades working the border). Between these men you're talking about decades of combined law enforcement experience and thousands of hours working this specific problem from ground level. Every single one of them used the same words. Modern day slavery. | | ---|---|--- Walking along the Arizona/Mexico border That's the operational reality as described by a 42-year law enforcement veteran who chairs border security for the National Sheriffs Association and sits on the Department of Homeland Security Advisory Council. Sheriff Dannels has personally briefed RFK Jr., Dr. Phil, Tom Homan. When he used those words it carried the weight of someone who's been watching this happen from 83 miles of the most trafficked border terrain in the country for over a decade. I filmed what gets left behind. The debris field out there tells the story better than any press release ever could. Physical evidence of human beings moved through that landscape like freight, because to the cartels running this operation, that's the accurate description of what they're doing. Cody told us how cartels charge $10,000 to $30,000 crossing fee for every single person who makes the trek. He didn't get that number from a government report. He got it from the people themselves, in the field, in real time. He's worked that desert long enough to have seen this end every way it possibly can. He's been at scenes where people didn't make it out, where evidence of what they paid and what they still owed was scattered around them in the heat. When Cody describes what these crossings actually cost and what the debt does to people afterward, he's pulling from conversations he's personally had with human beings in the worst moments of their lives. Here's what that debt actually means in practice: The cartel controls every inch of the Mexican side of the border and nobody ... not ONE PERSON crosses without authorization. Can't pay upfront? You cross on credit and that balance follows you into the interior. Now you're here illegally and owe $15,000 to an organization that knows where your family lives back home, and surfacing to any authority risks everything. What happens next is pedictable... You do whatever the cartel needs done. Sex trafficking. Prostitution. Running drugs. Labor. Whatever they need that week, that's your life now because the cartel owns you and everyone you love. Don't comply? They know your mother's address back home. Your kids' school. Noncompliance isn't a negotiation, it's a death sentence, and sometimes they make an example out of the people closest to you first just to make sure you understand how serious they are. And the debt never quite clears either, because something new always gets tacked on. These guys have watched this play out enough times that they can finish the story before you ask him how it ends. | | ---|---|--- Sunset at the Arizona border They walked us through the mechanics of the crossings too. Cartel scouts sit on nearby mountains (pictured above) with binoculars guiding movement by radio. The crossers get outfitted in cartel-supplied camouflage with carpet shoes designed to wipe their tracks, and in some cases they drag blankets to erase the trail completely. The (Southeastern Arizona Border Regional Enforcement) runs over 1,000 hidden cameras throughout that desert, solar-powered and built into fake rocks so smugglers can't locate and destroy them. Dannels started this in 2017 with game cameras and rancher partnerships. Since then SABRE has detected over 116,000 illegal crossings and put roughly 437 drug smugglers in custody. All with only six deputies running the whole operation, fixing their own equipment in a garage because sending cameras to the manufacturer means weeks offline they simply can't absorb. People who make it through get ferried to cartel stash houses in Phoenix and moved north into the American interior. Whatever the local network needs that week, 25 to 30 percent of everything they earn goes back south to the cartel. Sometimes the business owner genuinely has no idea because the manager is the cartel's person. In a lot of destination towns there's a restaurant or a storefront functioning as a cash collection point where workers hand over their cut. The children are the hardest part to sit with. They've documented around 5,000 cases of children being drugged and cycled through fake family units specifically because families move through processing more easily. They get recycled back into Mexico and inserted into a new fake family for another run. The cartels have no regard for human life and children in this system are a rentable asset, nothing more. Years of building the political and legal case against enforcement created the operating environment the cartels needed. The court filings, the sanctuary policies, the sustained campaign to make federal immigration enforcement as difficult as possible at the local level, all of it fed a system the cartels were happy to exploit. Biden's autopen halted wall construction within an hour of taking office. What followed was a dark and deliberate unwinding of everything that made that border functional (e.g. surveillance equipment going dark, processing centers absorbing the agents who should have been in the field), and Dannels standing there with six deputies and a camera network built from donated dollars because the federal government had functionally abandoned that desert. So when you see a construction bid come in mysteriously low, undercutting every legitimate competitor, or produce on a shelf priced below what it honestly costs to grow, that gap has to come from somewhere. It comes from someone with a cartel debt working with zero ability to complain to anyone about anything, ever. So when a business association lobbies against border enforcement and packages it as concern for workforce needs, understand what pipeline they 're actually running defense for. Arizona has been trying to fight this for fifteen years. passed in 2010. Governor Brewer signed it and Obama's DOJ filed suit almost immediately, taking it all the way to the Supreme Court. By 2012 three of the four main provisions were gone. One survived, a single requirement that officers check immigration status of people they've already lawfully arrested, out of everything the law originally contained. Then in November 2024, Arizona voters passed directly at the ballot, going around a governor who'd vetoed every enforcement measure her legislature sent her. More carefully drafted this time, written around people caught in the act of crossing rather than anyone present in the state (it's contingent on a parallel Texas case surviving federal court and that fight isn't resolved). Fifteen years of work, and people dressing cartel protection up as civil rights advocacy have litigated most of it into nothing. Just this month, Idaho had a chance to look at that history and do something with it. But it didn't happen and this wasn't the first time. I carried the harboring bills on the Senate side. this session, along with nearly every other immigration enforcement bill in 2026, was mine or a bill I was directly involved in pushing. | | ---|---|--- What these bills would have done is give Idaho law enforcement the same basic authority Arizona built its entire enforcement model around, making it a state crime to knowingly assist someone unlawfully present in the United States. But the House Judiciary Committee killed HB 764 without a floor vote. The 2026 session wrapped up this month and every single immigration enforcement bill either failed or never got a hearing. Every one in a Republican supermajority, in one of the reddest states in the country. The Idaho Sheriffs Association lobbied against these bills. ISA went into the capitol and argued against giving their own members tools that Sheriff Dannels has spent fifteen years refining, tools that drew sheriffs and law enforcement from across the country to Arizona to study firsthand. That needs a straight answer... What exactly is ISA protecting here? Dannels built SABRE with game cameras and rancher handshakes and it became a nationally recognized enforcement model that law enforcement from around the country fly in specifically to study. Lamb's years running anti-smuggling operations in Pinal County gave him a ground-level view of this pipeline that nobody sitting in a statehouse committee room is ever going to get from a briefing document. We stood in that desert alongside sheriffs and LEOs who flew in from across the country, and the reason every single one of them made the trip was sitting right in front of us the whole time. Idaho 's sheriffs association spent this session making sure their own members couldn't follow that lead. While I was carrying these bills in the Senate, ISA was in the building arguing the other direction. That's worth saying clearly because coverage tends to miss it: morale at the border is genuinely high right now. Sheriff Dannels and everyone we interacted with said it directly. The agents feel like they 're finally being allowed to do the job they signed up for. The cartels still own the Mexican side of that fence though. A cartel scout sat on a mountain watching us through binoculars, same way he watched when Trump visited during the 2024 campaign. The machine kept running at lower volume, and cartels have a patience for this work that outlasts any administration. They're counting on the fact that legislatures don't have that same patience. Idaho just burned another session. Sixty miles of terrain in Cochise County alone sits without a wall. Steel posts intended for Trump's original build are stacked out in that desert right now. The window is open. Someone in our state needs to find the spine to do something permanent with it before the next cycle closes it again. Anyone making the case for continued legislative inaction needs to explain, with some specificity, how their position differs from anyone who's ever looked at a slave trade and decided the economics were worth defending. Still waiting on that answer from ISA. | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Brian Lenney ? ?

| We’ve run our Voter Protection Team before. But we’ve never run our Voter Protection Team in a year quite like this one. Donald Trump is openly talking about having Republicans _“take over”_ voting — and right-wing officials are floating the idea of deploying ICE to patrol the polls. That’s not normal. Friend, I have no words. This is bad. I won't beat around the bush: Is saving this reliably red Senate seat worth it to you? Is it worth donating just $10 or even $5 right this very moment? We've relied on Texas to help us flip the Senate, the House, and the White House countless times over the last 50 years, and my stomach is in knots seeing the Democrat U.S. Senate nominee's first quarter fundraising numbers. Inaction today spells certain doom for our glorious Texas. _**_I 'm pleading with you to wake up and stop pretending like someone else is going to step in and make sure we don't lose the crown jewel of Republican states for the first time in 38 years. _**_ **[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zo9dbam21lme1m4f6skvr08pfd1pimomj88f7lug_rp22sh2s8i6epr7chie9ob4clh2bs)[SAVE TEXAS](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z8f7f8bk1tn5oa44prlg5kcbmqndr3ffrg5f8h3o_rp22sh2s8i6epr7chie9ob4clh2bs)[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z4frerll0ubcou4bokohamoem89k703bmuc9qmfg_rp22sh2s8i6epr7chie9ob4clh2bs)[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z4hdr3dvbl7jpv52nvketbsmnk1ge2bf6ahe4fng_rp22sh2s8i6epr7chie9ob4clh2bs)[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zi4fucon11g0l571k5gnoeg2uu0tbfsbp748hci8_rp22sh2s8i6epr7chie9ob4clh2bs)[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zgv87r7cfvl6oe9454cvogjknj5soufck7v2j3jo_rp22sh2s8i6epr7chie9ob4clh2bs)** God bless you,Ted Cruz

name: Hillsdale College via American Action News
subject: They knew freedom was worth fighting for
body:
In the spring of 1775, a handful of farmers and tradesmen stood their ground against the greatest military power on earth.

This sponsored message from American Action News is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zs7ncmsjc8g3ua9i8l3q9kj9er0j3se4i7dq1itg_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g68oj4cjh2bs)
---
| | Dear Friend,

In the spring of 1775, a handful of farmers and tradesmen stood their ground against the greatest military power on earth.

They didn't know how the story would end.

But they knew that freedom was worth fighting for.

The "shot heard 'round the world" set in motion a chain of events that led to the greatest experiment in self-government the world has ever known.

That's why I'm excited to share something special with you.

**The White House recently asked Hillsdale College to assist the Salute to America 250 Task Force in producing a new free video series, [_The Story of America_](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z6boufcbi6u4cuhe83utqi2pmb9jura4okbvj21g_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g68oj4cjh2bs)** --a sweeping, episode-by-episode journey through our nation's founding in honor of its 250th anniversary.

As we approach the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, this series invites you to learn about the principles, courage, sacrifice, and faith that made America possible--and _why this story still matters today_.

Filmed in the historic Indian Treaty Room in the Eisenhower Executive Office Building in Washington, D.C., each episode brings a pivotal chapter of the American story to life.

**Here 's a glimpse of what you can access on your journey at no cost:****
**

* **The Declaration of Independence** -- _Larry P. Arnn, President, Hillsdale College_
How the Declaration of Independence defines who we are--and what it still asks of us

* **The Formation of the Army** -- _Pete Hegseth, Secretary of War_
The moment citizen-patriots took up arms and answered liberty's call against overwhelming odds

* **The King 's Call to Arms** -- _U.S. Senator Mike Lee_
When imperial authority collided with colonial defiance--and compromise was no longer possible

* **The Culper Spy Ring** -- _Brian Kilmeade, Co-Author, George Washington 's Secret Six_
The remarkable story of ordinary Americans who risked everything to outsmart the British and save the Revolution

* **Story of the American Soldier** -- _Michael Knowles, Author and Podcaster_
A tribute to the forgotten men whose sacrifice secured American independence

* **The U.S. Marine Corps Birthday** -- _Vice President J.D. Vance_
The enduring legacy of courage, service, and sacrifice that defines the U.S. Marine Corps

**You can receive instant access to this free video series right now using this secure link: **

**[https://info.hillsdale.edu/story-of-america-free-access](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z9rtfvhp4sfp4s3128n2mj8lnjar28cp8alu6flo_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g68oj4cjh2bs)**

**As we approach our nation 's 250th anniversary, now is the time to rediscover the spirit of 1776.**

And it's my hope that, by remembering the great deeds of the past, we can inspire Americans today to equally great deeds.

Enjoy your free video series!

Bill Gray '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

**P.S.** New episodes will be released throughout the year as we approach America's 250th anniversary--sign up now so you don't miss a single one!

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/story-of-america-free-access**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z6tfh06kml3m9cchlubdk1k2ejq2e9armfka1nmo_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g68oj4cjh2bs)
---
| |
---

Pop Acta Media | American Action News
---

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:
I have no words for what I’m about to show you

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z94q5ec6e2881bvurtq3520ttq4hhornvbarsq6o_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g62o70cjh2bs)

---
Friend, I have no words. This is bad. I won't beat around the bush: Is saving this reliably red Senate seat worth it to you? Is it worth donating just $10 or even $5 right this very moment? We've relied on Texas to help us flip the Senate, the House, and the White House countless times over the last 50 years, and my stomach is in knots seeing the Democrat U.S. Senate nominee's first quarter fundraising numbers. Inaction today spells certain doom for our glorious Texas. _**_I 'm pleading with you to wake up and stop pretending like someone else is going to step in and make sure we don't lose the crown jewel of Republican states for the first time in 38 years. _**_ **[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zru42mnanoes72u2p746v14d0hoipbeluvckhef0_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g62o70cjh2bs)[SAVE TEXAS](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zjjbd4tnd8g0v17r0u2g592t5vlutii9pj7s6ji0_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g62o70cjh2bs)[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z0ukv5afocgujlc7aj7siovesie9bbf75383t9n8_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g62o70cjh2bs)[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zl0i73is2ta311ndimns08rm95vvrm8aojgf4908_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g62o70cjh2bs)[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z3eii01mr60q9j2akaujrveirc2jakf81o9i5s4o_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g62o70cjh2bs)[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zf74h94miq6bp4pkgoe8mbshcat2t2dr67k5vrfg_rp22sh2s8i6epr7c9g62o70cjh2bs)** God bless you,Ted Cruz

body:
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001OK9FNmxCSwdRoYkTePWvOPCOAAc-lImBOrS11IAg3uFxokzg0GLX1_lldULR7GULQjXh0MJy08UoLO5FSaa9ro5-rGka7cuo09JluZ516WPs3s2AbDP6hmK2jR8OstnD9jMr3OtTg_jEXvFiPoWBX9nKzo_iLDMES2MhtHtIPn7nzZTKA9F4E9GV3q25sTlo3W80estwl6rcEGZ7xjGmEqzz_vFOZhbfNABfe8B2g4I=&c=9itxr1Id1EmVFOdWMNVSRMKdF6HJ4KgIO5yDfJC0WbSWMjl0-MGLjQ==&ch=F11dxDAHLrYKSujQkrHzVJKWyLdvnMh4FL3KiW96cdaLeN2Jgt1tZw==)

|
Peter,
Yesterday, Democrats scored a massive victory in Virginia.
Virginia voters just dealt a significant blow to MAGA's gerrymandering in Texas and other red states. In fact, it gives Democrats an edge over MAGA for the upcoming midterms:
---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001OK9FNmxCSwdRoYkTePWvOPCOAAc-lImBOrS11IAg3uFxokzg0GLX1_lldULR7GULQjXh0MJy08UoLO5FSaa9ro5-rGka7cuo09JluZ516WPs3s2AbDP6hmK2jR8OstnD9jMr3OtTg_jEXvFiPoWBX9nKzo_iLDMES2MhtHtIPn7nzZTKA9F4E9GV3q25sTlo3W80estwl6rcEGZ7xjGmEqTJQlypyFmfGgzQY8IDtuA=&c=9itxr1Id1EmVFOdWMNVSRMKdF6HJ4KgIO5yDfJC0WbSWMjl0-MGLjQ==&ch=F11dxDAHLrYKSujQkrHzVJKWyLdvnMh4FL3KiW96cdaLeN2Jgt1tZw==)
---

But while all eyes were on Virginia, another battle was already brewing.
On May 19, Georgia holds a state Supreme Court election.
This election is another crucial front in the battle against Trump and MAGA's election rigging schemes. If Democrats win this race, it will set them up to completely flip the GA Supreme Court just months before the 2028 election.
You remember 2020. Georgia was ground zero for Trump's election rigging plot. Fake electors. Pressure campaigns. Direct attempts to manipulate the vote.
Georgia's Supreme Court is the last line of defense against the voting restrictions, rigged maps, and legal challenges MAGA will throw at the state in the 2028 presidential election.

---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001OK9FNmxCSwdRoYkTePWvOPCOAAc-lImBOrS11IAg3uFxokzg0GLX1_lldULR7GULQjXh0MJy08UoLO5FSaa9ro5-rGka7cuo09JluZ516WPs3s2AbDP6hmK2jR8OstnD9jMr3OtTg_jEXvFiPoWBX9nKzo_iLDMES2MhtHtIPn7nzZTKA9F4E9GV3q25sTlo3W80estwl6rcEGZ7xjGmEs6V1nfyyQMtREFzlo6QL48=&c=9itxr1Id1EmVFOdWMNVSRMKdF6HJ4KgIO5yDfJC0WbSWMjl0-MGLjQ==&ch=F11dxDAHLrYKSujQkrHzVJKWyLdvnMh4FL3KiW96cdaLeN2Jgt1tZw==)
---

Unlike Virginia, this election won't have the benefit of national attention. It's a spring election. It's nonpartisan. And most voters don't know this election is even happening--let alone who to vote for.
This race will be decided by which side is able to reach more voters. And right now, that's the problem.
There is a funding gap on our side, and if we don't close it, we could lose.
That's why Defend Our Courts is rallying Democrats to come to Georgia's aid. With the support of over 100,000 Democrats nationwide, we have already helped dems win massive victories in the Wisconsin and Pennsylvania Supreme Court races.
Now we're taking our winning formula to Georgia where Dems have an opportunity to deal another blow to Trump's election rigging schemes.
But time is running out. This election is in 23 days, and we have to raise enough money to reach more voters than MAGA before the clock runs out. That's why we're asking you today:
[VA was a massive win for Dems. But we can't let Georgia go unnoticed. With less than a month to go, we need to ramp up NOW. Can we count on you to rush a contribution of $50, $25, or whatever you can spare to help us reach the voters who will decide this election?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001OK9FNmxCSwdRoYkTePWvOPCOAAc-lImBOrS11IAg3uFxokzg0GLX1_lldULR7GUL5eqx71GZFvFqRRysZP4rXpZU88i4NzhGdaeehZwnOfTL2RZBtHAqIXkckfYOERfTDIbN9VPGYjForXNlJDzKlF5MTumP554wgTzDTBwkYzIRvbYGwaEqt72y7n4wmeMMoDf-RQISLpqYq9PmLsJr7qj1wPO18hKLwsqwMr9hx-r-6sgvt8mDjK65fCfQz12R&c=9itxr1Id1EmVFOdWMNVSRMKdF6HJ4KgIO5yDfJC0WbSWMjl0-MGLjQ==&ch=F11dxDAHLrYKSujQkrHzVJKWyLdvnMh4FL3KiW96cdaLeN2Jgt1tZw==)

name: The Flyover
subject: Trump Rushed from Press Dinner, UFO Whistleblower Dead, and Chain Letters
body:
President Trump was evacuated Saturday night from the White House Correspondents' Association dinner at the Washington Hilton after at least four shots erupted inside the building. [Watch the moment here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1q7DAUBOCnkbs1-rvSulBxYdklxYZAipRC9jlaK8iWkWQbv31wXiHtDHwD8_W0TxdimF_vbkJTfbIz7iVirZhtzeu05LWMCD6naclYCoR5RsspVxcqL1xZcCEeVjZgPCJH0aBhWmvWXanmzWjYQEFJoTvor1z86yh45Slo4QaGMPgmmFOjkitGqZZdK1nvemASZA9SdppIihBqyu13CnMd0ccjbZjbIU1NNGOtSyHiP-F3wu_7vrdHWuva49mfiavDSMR9I-KmP-MDPx5s42-Eq0rETZUmG1j2EGMo6tx6TS7EX7v-6ZDN8J8AAAD__4XsdCE).

# Sunday, April 26, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1986, a reactor at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant in Soviet Ukraine exploded during a late-night safety test, triggering the worst nuclear disaster in history. See [some rare Chernobyl photos here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGupDAQRE9jMiO78WIIHGxCNptuiAzdDF4ZG7XNoLn9an7wL_Djkqrqvb-P-eFDDOn5xx_k6pbnRHeJVCvxXPk6Tr7KTrhxPk6mUjCkRDMo6KUyEvoZfYjv2TToNiKgriGnrbV6HJSFZndous4TqkGB3X4BDah9T51Co9QwKNsE92lTBnqtlDVja_TiF9QGzYLGjFYYRRhq5vZfDqnutMV3fhG3az6a6PZazyK63wImAdN93y3TVYnLJxcwnWGtF1MRMLFnkueeay5Sj0Mv15045eUdZbrWSJ4lhuJLJZbfiEbA1LDD8w4xhtJ__jwPH-LXfv2RtJeD_wEAAP__jSCAvg). In today's _Quick Hits_ , we're highlighting [one of the oldest libraries](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFutDAQhJ8GOiNj9sxd4eJv6O5vU6LFXg5Hxkb2coS3j4jyBulGGmnm0_fxHJ_og4-v_7iS4TmNkY4SiJnyyHlft7yXhdyc07plKsX5GGlUUmkhQSg9OvThHKF2ZiZS1NVk2r7v28dd9qpejEQrOyBUN2wlwF1Pnaabxu6GqMFC7c21JkHpVsoeHg20E06uBQeTA3j0FUhynlNuPpOPvNAczvSm3Ni01sEszFupun-VGio1HMfRTJO9ukoNdg-8Z6rUgJm9DVf6fesELyQo2gUj-_gShVM-RZpF-ppTdkWs5Dy9MYjgp4z5rLNx2-FD8EVfUK8VffiB4D-Zexv1HQAA__9Ng4PQ) in the world, Merton College Library at Oxford University. We have photos and details below. In case you missed it, this week on****_The Flyover Podcast_ , host Ayla Brown unpacked a new study raising eyebrows about the news you see every day. Researchers took a deep dive into major platforms like Google, Apple News, and Yahoo, and what they found could change how you think about your feed. Are these aggregators really neutral, or is there something else going on? [Listen here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzFuwyAUANDT4C3W5_vH4IEhUmWpQ7p2RCZ8x1TYWIAT5fZVeoVe4Env-2qvU4hhu39NK5s6J7vxs0SulbOt-Vj3fJSF_ZzTumcuxYdtY4uA_QnohL31U4gvS403MzNy17CRSik5aFDYLOYGUpO_YcfaS-5BD3h27Jw6n0FCJ5tg3hoQ9hJA0dCSdJPzkjw5TzQoQcA-1JTbnxS2uvAcX-nBub2ltYlmqXUvorsIHAWOr3TUo3UscBw-KTwQ1O4uohur6D5Q6yYbvz9DjKH0b_m-TiH-SfVf_YfB3wAAAP__uaBsqQ). If the news has you feeling weighed down and overwhelmed recently, you're not alone... but it doesn't have to stay that way. That's why we read _[The Pour Over](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkM2KwyAURp_G7Br0xtFm4WKgdNfZzlJMvCYOGoM_LX37IWWgDzDbey4fh_N90zfjg9-WLxNRVZf0ho8SsFbMuuYW99zKitblFPeMpVi_baiBgjhRfgKhrfHhqXlnlUMEHDpUTErJxjOV0K1qsOA-xMwAx2mW1kqD4KRjZ8moEWbqvDrWKAfBKJV87DmbzGQZt3yynI-ScIrW15T7n-S3uqILz3TH3M8pdkGtte6FDJ8ErgSuxS9b21fM2NcV99Ty6zXl5eDDtdWoS2p5RjJc3lsExEEiWt8iGS7vCn-kYj7uXIPosrL7w4fgizjklmh8eMnUfxW8K_gNAAD__14TiIA)_. It's one of the sources we trust to deliver the facts while keeping us grounded in peace.

## Trump Evacuated From Press Dinner
President Trump was evacuated Saturday night from the White House Correspondents' Association dinner at the Washington Hilton after a gunman opened fire near a security checkpoint just outside the ballroom. [Watch the moment here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1q7DAUBOCnkbs1-rvSulBxYdklxYZAipRC9jlaK8iWkWQbv31wXiHtDHwD8_W0TxdimF_vbkJTfbIz7iVirZhtzeu05LWMCD6naclYCoR5RsspVxcqL1xZcCEeVjZgPCJH0aBhWmvWXanmzWjYQEFJoTvor1z86yh45Slo4QaGMPgmmFOjkitGqZZdK1nvemASZA9SdppIihBqyu13CnMd0ccjbZjbIU1NNGOtSyHiP-F3wu_7vrdHWuva49mfiavDSMR9I-KmP-MDPx5s42-Eq0rETZUmG1j2EGMo6tx6TS7EX7v-6ZDN8J8AAAD__4XsdCE). The Secret Service rushed into the ballroom as guests dove under tables. First Lady Melania Trump, Vice President JD Vance, and Cabinet officials [were also evacuated](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDFuhDAQRU8DnZExXgyFizR0mzYlMsywTGRsZA-gvX3EXiH1k77e-z_P8enIU3h9uw0tL3EMeGWPzJhGTse2pyOvCEuK254wZ6AQcFRStUJqodoRHPn3qEuwC6LCpkRbG2PqvpNGlat9dI-mV9D0bdcuTSd7UKbtoWseBmHGuiR7r0mt2lpKo_tK15OboNagJ9C6N4WWCMQxVb-RAq-4-Hc8MVVz3EpvV-Y9F81XoYZCDdd1VfOU74qbF2rwdKI4dnCMuVDDJ0rg6ebDMYK4VmIUazwyijmmhHmPATBwFp_WJDLORyJ-Cwoz3aRQQ5ks7Bd5T7m9BV-bI_8R4n-9eFr1FwAA___OU4pd). Trump and Vance were unhurt. Authorities identified the suspect as [31-year-old Cole Allen](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO6yAQRb8Gd0R4TMxLQfEaumy7pYVhiGeFwQKcKH-_Ir-w3YyudHXP-b4vd0uR0uPL7qhbyEvCV43YGpallXM_ylk39KHk_ShYq6eUcAEBMxeSw7x4S_G9yMHrgAg4DahHpdR4-ycUDJt2bhLjpKwDuV5X5Wcl3RpUsKDk5GccSPc2IWEehVDydpHjalc_Si9XL-VNMSnQU8vl8pMptQ1DfOcnlovL-xD11tpR2fSfgWFg0vvItfWIgenFDIyQ_b4yMGflnY-BeZxpt4nbxl8bNeRbPityl0vBeuTkMbXKP7CFU_8oEHpuK3c5IrcxYuLdCnc2UsglkWVghqL98aIYqc59-GO3FD9D25_sPjX8BgAA___UR5Ck) of Torrance, California, a tutoring center teacher armed with a shotgun, handgun, and multiple knives. A Secret Service agent was struck at close range but was saved by a bulletproof vest. See security footage of the moment [here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGuozAQRb8Gd4nGw2QMhYuVVnTZaqUtkcFD8MpgZDuJ8vdPvF947b3Subrn3328uxDD_vjjNrF1SeMu7xKlVsljzc_tyM-yil9y2o4spfiw7zIiIF-ALsijdyF-RlLeLiIorRKrjTG678CgWm0nMs2sRfc0TT0COQCYGVvPYvgmKtiTBoSsAQz1V9KTm7wmT5Mn6k1DID7UlK__U9jrKkv8pJfk65w2Fe1a61Ga9leDQ4NDzc-6ljQHF8_-DAmyuPg77S76v-elBocjlVoaHLRm4o5v0LJpO0ZsVbb-eIcYQ-Fz-rG58I1S9Ud-Xha_AgAA___eW3ga). Trump called Allen a "lone wolf whackjob" and said the shooting wasn't believed to be tied to the Iran war. The dinner has been rescheduled within 30 days.
## US Pulls Plug on Iran Talks
President Trump on Saturday canceled a trip by envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for ceasefire talks with Iran, posting that he had "all the cards" and Tehran could call him if it wanted to negotiate. [See the post here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFu9CAQhJ_GdHeCZX_2p6CIFLm7VJFSWtisz0TYWIDvdG8fOa-Qdkb6RvN93Yabjylu9w-_smtzHjZ-1sStcRlaOda9HHXhMJe87oVrDXHbeAAJ5iLxAmYIPqbXgCK4mRlYC3aKiJT9LwnE4vSoZq1gJov_jJ9sGL1GO6HSIxmSRkR30iSCUVIS2iuq0Y9BYcAxIFrqUHKILZfrd45bW3hOr_zgcp3yKpJbWttrp9866DvoWznaUvMUfTr7M0RZ2Kf3vPkUPs9LHfR7rq120Ctl0BgCrY1CkpZIFBf2Z0wpVnNO31cff1Gi_cnPw8FPAAAA__-IOHfG). Trump cited "tremendous infighting and confusion" [inside Iran's leadership](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL9u8yAUR58Gb1iAsbEHhnz6ZKlDunToaPHnOqbBYAGJlbevyCt0_ulenXO-r8tVOe_C7VPtIMsalwBn9lAKpKWkx36kR97ArinuR4KcrQsBFkbYgAnHbFiscv618MbKFYBB14CkQgg6jUSwZpN6nYTotDG90WQ0YMee9oqrodd67UbaOFm_Ec4GSojgU8upVtpSbrm2nE8CcQLWlZjan-hC2WD1r_iE1Jq4N15upRwZdRfEZsTm8zzbPYe6ITZDwI-M2Fyl6hiTt4jNbzFsVDDgM4bwjK-MS3IHLhEf6u5yUQGvMWGXVMBF-Xs9VwlfLox-lDCibo7GWdT9_1fjwZm_IB1NkvY4nfcuDxX7tivn35jlT22fkv0GAAD__7gVkG4), where new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since taking power. He later said Iran sent a "much better" proposal within 10 minutes. The cancellation came just hours after Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi left Islamabad, [telling reporters](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOozAQhp8GukG28eFQuEhxSFfk2i3REA_BG2OjsQnK26_IK2z9az5933zdxhv64OPjP65ky5zGSEcOVArxWHhfN97zQm7mtG5MOTsfI41KqA6EBtWNDn14j7p2diZS1NZkpTFG9hdhVL3YjrSQUutWOzQOpSS86KlDVPhHqtbU3p40oVUnhTC6b7SccHJSOz05rXtTaUHOl8TNd_KxLDSHd3oRN_e01sEupWy5aq-VGio1HMfRrDmeW6UGirDnSg1n1DkmDq5SwycMMr4zLAR3jHcK5OC55yUSw-HLM80zFPYbzInBM0YoGJ4ZDoIFXwQYApTPMbsTjQzXq5L_sP9bs3Xb4UPwuTvlHyv68JEtv_rwy6qfAAAA__81wpHV) he had "yet to see if the U.S. is truly serious about diplomacy." Brent crude closed near [$105 a barrel](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2OqzAQxfGngY7IHhwcChe3octtt0S2Zwiz8geynUR5-xX7Ctsf_Y70_7qvd8uB0-O_jWTaltdE7xqoNSprK894lGfdCbeS41GoVuSUaAUB0yDUANOKlsNnVT2ajQho7MlIrbWcb0JDv5urJSk1eLg6BxP4zV_diDC6m5J-1qJnc2pCwSSF0Gq-KOmsQ6lQOVRq1p0ShNxyuXxnTm2nLXzyi8rF59gHs7d21G7818HSwdKKRU4P8jnlyL6eow4Wn2PMyO3TweIKpTb48kQaMoe-GDzeHALX6fx6RMvh125_CvIy8BMAAP__W4Z2jg) Friday, up roughly 17% on the week, as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut and U.S. and Iranian forces continue tit-for-tat ship seizures.
## Pentagon Floats Booting Spain from NATO
A leaked Pentagon email reportedly outlines [options for punishing NATO allies](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAYhJ9GOwywLCwFxTV2e-2VBuVX_wuCgd_19u0v3itcN8lkJt_M13N4eoyYlk-_gaM5DwnOGoEIykDl2PZy1BXCXPK2F6g1YEowSC4144pJPQSP8T2oNrgZQMKtBSeMMcI-uJHt6qz1WlsRhBaPux01GG7Gh1ICNJ_8qFt0VxtXUgvOjbKdEqMfg1BBjUEpaxrFISDl0n1nTLTCHN_5BaWb8tZGtxLttbl9NLJvZH-eZ_f2a86X28j-mtPI3hfCKcIl4WeKR8UXsB0S-SUnBpvHyOaYPVVWj7pDCpgWxu_8JoTiultpi21xYT8xRqz6glqu1B8E_eu5l5O_AQAA__-AuoPG) that declined to support U.S. operations in the Iran war, including suspending Spain from the alliance and reviewing Washington's backing for Britain's claim to the Falkland Islands. The memo, prepared by top Pentagon policy adviser Elbridge Colby, expressed frustration that allies refused access and overflight rights for the Iran campaign. Spain blocked U.S. forces from using [jointly operated bases](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O5SAMRp-GdETE8eanoNgm3d12y4gEkzAigIDc6L79iHmFaSzLsuxzvv-v9aWss_74py6SxYTV05MdlUJpLem-YrrzSdqkcMVEOWvrPa0gYOACOQyrVtZ9Vmy0NERAfUOyG8exmycxQnPKHnphhMFpown1NvT7ANP-x-hpViBwaqys1wTC0Akx4txit6lNd6hx04jzyFCQtiWk9itYX04y7hPelNo9XI2TZykxs_4vg4XB8jxPS3cKVaMuMFiuD6-TSAyW-orBIrD2tUTyRR3B8z34bDWlzPOdI3lt_cFzVNbzKs-9KqFJUsfHOmfzULGOS1n3g1F-ld1bwncAAAD__zENhA0) at Rota and Moron. Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez brushed off the report, [telling reporters](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSqzAMRb8GOmeMcHAoXLyGLq_dklGwAG2M7bFNMvn7He8vbCOp0D1z5n7d5zuyY7_9x4NMWcPs6Z0dlUJpLuk8YjrzTnZN4YiJcrbsPc0gYRBSCRhmi-w-s2qtWYmA-pZMp7XuxpvU0O6m6wd7Q9RLbxeEcRkH0qBvPQ7Xxa5wbdlUmlQwdFJqNV5U98CH7ZRVD6vUqBslyXIJ6fId2JedVvcJL0qXJRytM3spMTf9vwamBqY1JOLNx-B4-dSPBqbKb2CSqt51nFlE8gW34AUdyE54LEHkiOzF-RQruqdDbwXnurLghL6BqU3Gxjc7x3moWlvN_mqUP3X3MvATAAD__ztWg6o) his government works "off official documents," not informal communications. NATO's founding treaty contains no mechanism for suspending members, but Argentine President Javier Milei welcomed the potential Falklands review as a step toward sovereignty.

**Whatever happened to chain letters?** Chain letters are older than the internet, older than email, and nearly as old as modern mass mail itself. The recognizably modern chain letter took off in the United States in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, when cheap postage and improved delivery networks made it easy to copy a letter and send it along to friends. The formula was simple and strangely compelling. Copy this message. Mail it to several people. Good fortune would follow if you did. Bad luck might follow if you did not. The phenomenon exploded during the Great Depression with the famous "Send-a-Dime" letters of 1935. One widely circulated version, [called the Prosperity Club,](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO5CAQRL8GZ1jQcMYOCC5xNpdeaOHpZswdBguYsebvV94NN9yopFKp9PT-3pabCzGkxx-3k20-L4nOGqk1Kksrz_0oz7oR-pL3o1CtGFKiBQQMXGgOw4IuxPeiO7SeCEh1ZKUxRk6jMNBtVirjEVZhfgnjB-UdaASpxgHVOtKoumCvN6FhkEIYPfVarm5FqVGvqPVkmBaEoeXS_8shtY18fOcXlf6e9y7arbWjMvWbwcxgPs-zfxD9rzEf14DBHIMnBrN3WLlLyFuhhPV7w-UkBIMZhLyiUkLuOIad-H1zIfEvLVxOStSuWDzOEGOow0X42F2In0TtRxpfFj4CAAD__w2zheg) asked recipients to mail a dime to the first name on a list, cross that name off, add their own at the bottom, and send copies to five friends. If the chain held, the math promised 15,625 dimes -- $1,562.50, a small fortune at the time. The craze became so intense that the Denver post office was handling tens of thousands of such letters a day at its peak, with single-day counts reaching 100,000 before postal inspectors cracked down. The math, of course, could never work: five recipients going just ten levels deep would already involve nearly ten million people. When email spread in the 1990s, the chain letter adapted almost instantly. Forwarding email costs nothing and takes seconds, allowing the old format to scale dramatically. Inboxes filled with digital descendants: inspirational forwards, fake virus warnings, the endlessly recirculated plea for get-well cards to Craig Shergold (a real British boy who had long since recovered), and the infamous hoax promising that Bill Gates would pay you to forward an email. Some were harmless. Others had a more practical purpose. Many early chain emails collected long strings of forwarded addresses in the header, which made them valuable tools for spammers building mailing lists. Today, the classic postal chain letter is nearly extinct, and chain emails faded as spam filters, threaded inboxes, and the shift to social platforms quietly strangled them. But the underlying mechanism: copy this message and send it to others, is still alive and well in social media repost chains, TikTok and Facebook viral challenges, and messaging app hoaxes. _Did you ever receive or send chain letters? Let us know your thoughts by replying to this email._

The Secret Service rushed into the ballroom as guests dove under tables. First Lady Melania Trump, Vice President JD Vance, and Cabinet officials . Trump and Vance were unhurt. Authorities identified the suspect as of Torrance, California, a tutoring center teacher armed with a shotgun, handgun, and multiple knives. A Secret Service agent was struck at close range but was saved by a bulletproof vest. See security footage of the moment . Trump called Allen a "lone wolf whackjob" and said the shooting wasn't believed to be tied to the Iran war. The dinner has been rescheduled within 30 days. US Pulls Plug on Iran Talks President Trump on Saturday canceled a trip by envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for ceasefire talks with Iran, posting that he had "all the cards" and Tehran could call him if it wanted to negotiate. . Trump cited "tremendous infighting and confusion" , where new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since taking power. He later said Iran sent a "much better" proposal within 10 minutes. The cancellation came just hours after Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi left Islamabad, he had "yet to see if the U.S. is truly serious about diplomacy." Brent crude closed near Friday, up roughly 17% on the week, as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut and U.S. and Iranian forces continue tit-for-tat ship seizures. Pentagon Floats Booting Spain from NATO A leaked Pentagon email reportedly outlines that declined to support U.S. operations in the Iran war, including suspending Spain from the alliance and reviewing Washington's backing for Britain's claim to the Falkland Islands. The memo, prepared by top Pentagon policy adviser Elbridge Colby, expressed frustration that allies refused access and overflight rights for the Iran campaign. Spain blocked U.S. forces from using at Rota and Moron. Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez brushed off the report, his government works "off official documents," not informal communications. NATO's founding treaty contains no mechanism for suspending members, but Argentine President Javier Milei welcomed the potential Falklands review as a step toward sovereignty. Plus, you get an exclusive 20% off if you use the discount code () | Whatever happened to chain letters? Chain letters are older than the internet, older than email, and nearly as old as modern mass mail itself. The recognizably modern chain letter took off in the United States in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, when cheap postage and improved delivery networks made it easy to copy a letter and send it along to friends. The formula was simple and strangely compelling. Copy this message. Mail it to several people. Good fortune would follow if you did. Bad luck might follow if you did not. The phenomenon exploded during the Great Depression with the famous "Send-a-Dime" letters of 1935. One widely circulated version, asked recipients to mail a dime to the first name on a list, cross that name off, add their own at the bottom, and send copies to five friends. If the chain held, the math promised 15,625 dimes -- $1,562.50, a small fortune at the time. The craze became so intense that the Denver post office was handling tens of thousands of such letters a day at its peak, with single-day counts reaching 100,000 before postal inspectors cracked down. The math, of course, could never work: five recipients going just ten levels deep would already involve nearly ten million people. When email spread in the 1990s, the chain letter adapted almost instantly. Forwarding email costs nothing and takes seconds, allowing the old format to scale dramatically. Inboxes filled with digital descendants: inspirational forwards, fake virus warnings, the endlessly recirculated plea for get-well cards to Craig Shergold (a real British boy who had long since recovered), and the infamous hoax promising that Bill Gates would pay you to forward an email. Some were harmless. Others had a more practical purpose. Many early chain emails collected long strings of forwarded addresses in the header, which made them valuable tools for spammers building mailing lists. Today, the classic postal chain letter is nearly extinct, and chain emails faded as spam filters, threaded inboxes, and the shift to social platforms quietly strangled them. But the underlying mechanism: copy this message and send it to others, is still alive and well in social media repost chains, TikTok and Facebook viral challenges, and messaging app hoaxes. Did you ever receive or send chain letters? Let us know your thoughts by replying to this email. | Dads of daughters will understand... | Are you team iPhone or team Android? 1. 2. 3. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Most retirees miss out on bigger Social Security checks—leaving billions unclaimed. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Most retirees miss out on bigger Social Security checks—leaving billions unclaimed. name: Jim Jordan
subject: Hand to God, I’ll never stoop to that low
body:
After all you’ve done for our Party, I salute you!
---
| | | | | |
---
| | | | | [View in browser](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NTh9AAwAAAAAAEUbCXCDHhFvChu6lMihhRezVQqL-It9E62fqkf6BUKVHpsyddfR1skN1xhXUN_af3M2eNojpPejunbuXEULqPwq1uR4hTBbAOrXARWY "View in Browser")
---
_**A Special Message from our Sponsor**_ _Please note that views expressed in this message do not necessarily reflect those of_ _Parler_ _and do not constitute an endorsement or recommendation by_ _Parler_ _._
---
| Jim Jordan After all you’ve done for our Party, I salute you, Friend! | | Friend,
---
I’m sick of seeing text messages that accuse you of abandoning the GOP after all you’ve done for us.
---
All because you didn’t give $15 before some random deadline, that’s supposed to mean you **_“betrayed”_** the Republican Party and became a Democrat?
---
So all the times you stood in line to vote, you knocked on doors, you put up a yard sign, you made phone calls, you filled out surveys, you sacrificed your time and money – suddenly, all of that means nothing?
---
**_Give me a break! That’s just insulting._**
---
_Hand to God, I will never stoop to that low._
---
Truth be told, I try not to write too many emails and texts as it is. I’m busy working in Washington to do the job you asked me to do.
---
Honestly, fundraising just isn’t Priority #1.
---
At the end of 2024, I transferred $2.5 MILLION _**away**_ from my own campaign to help conservatives in tough races who _truly_ needed help.
---
**You know what I care about most?** The _ONLY_ thing that WINS elections – **_VOTES_**.
---
_That means registering voters, verifying every Trump voter’s profile, and constantly getting LIVE feedback from voters like you so that the President and I _always_ have the best, most accurate data in the country._
---
That’s how we won in 2024. And it’s my plan to win AGAIN in 2026.
---
[**Please complete the brief questionnaire to verify that YOU are among the highest propensity voters we can count on in the midterms – and then I’ll officially mark you as “verified” in our GOP Voter file for the month.**](https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4NTh9AAwAAAAAAEUbCXCEoiTNFRe1LVNrkdLA599B5wRFFcsfuQevjhLEjlhLulAEmNsqQdMMmp4ZBYboHgNR7lx0gLwF65VMivcKzJR7AM-wxl80uBlo)

name: Ashley Moody
subject: Some extremely alarming news to share
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Moody for Florida
body:
Three points. That's within the margin of error. In other words, this race is effectively tied. Let that sink in.

| | | | [](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkMvK2zAQRp9G3snoZjleaFEI3qWLUujSKNLYEdXFjOSkefvi0D7Av_04HM7Mr9tysyGGvH23CQy2dcG61JIA_jSEBPFto8UU8pbhVRfBhKZMUaFPjnfeeOctdx0YPo4jnyalRfcww12KQTHNnWQSRq4uTA-OMyamcfDSd8GcKqaE5kyyYervINVgrZjUxTI7KqKYjYCtR9iPewzO5mbr77Wgg96V1EXzaG2vRH4jYiZiruAOhP4VMoI_CSLmVIp_07UgXWPB4C0R8xqeQD9H0ZBXwFxofRRsy073shM54ynCkDcirw0PIEIfLS3NEXn98XOm_8P_7a7kBrkReYVkQ-QdGr-_Qoyh6pEotp3rp7h9_b9PI_4GAAD__wrvihQ)
---
Friend, this is not a drill... **A shocking new poll just dropped showing Deep State conspirator Alexander Vindman within just three points of flipping Florida in our must-win Senate special election.** [**Three points. That's within the margin of error. In other words, this race is effectively tied. Let that sink in.**](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkMvK2zAQRp9G3snoZjleaFEI3qWLUujSKNLYEdXFjOSkefvi0D7Av_04HM7Mr9tysyGGvH23CQy2dcG61JIA_jSEBPFto8UU8pbhVRfBhKZMUaFPjnfeeOctdx0YPo4jnyalRfcww12KQTHNnWQSRq4uTA-OMyamcfDSd8GcKqaE5kyyYervINVgrZjUxTI7KqKYjYCtR9iPewzO5mbr77Wgg96V1EXzaG2vRH4jYiZiruAOhP4VMoI_CSLmVIp_07UgXWPB4C0R8xqeQD9H0ZBXwFxofRRsy073shM54ynCkDcirw0PIEIfLS3NEXn98XOm_8P_7a7kBrkReYVkQ-QdGr-_Qoyh6pEotp3rp7h9_b9PI_4GAAD__wrvihQ) The man who abused his position at the highest levels of our government, who drove the Trump impeachment from inside the National Security Council, who wasted millions of taxpayer dollars trying to bring down a duly elected president — that man is now knocking on the door of the United States Senate. In Florida. In President Trump's home state. Vindman has made no secret of what drives him. He's not running out of some deep love of Florida — he just moved here! This is a man vehemently opposed to everything we stand for, singularly obsessed with destroying the America First Agenda, and determined to make every Trump supporter pay the price. _**This is not ambition. This is cold, hard revenge – and now he has the money to make it real.**_ **Vindman has raised over $8 million** — radical left-wing cash pouring in from the most extreme Trump-hating mega-donors in the country. Every dollar is fuel for his revenge campaign. And here's what makes this even more alarming: Democrats don't just have a candidate — they have momentum. In the last month alone, Democrats flipped three Florida seats. In a major upset, they flipped a Trump +11 district that includes Mar-a-Lago. **National forecasters have quietly downgraded Florida from "Safe Republican,"** and in the last nine special elections nationwide, Democrats ran an average of 17 points higher than Kamala Harris. _**They smell political blood.**_ Make no mistake. This race for Marco Rubio's former Senate seat is a special election – and special elections are unpredictable by nature. The normal rules don't apply. Turnout is volatile, outside money pours in fast, and a motivated opposition can flip a race that looks safe on paper. Democrats know this better than anyone. **This is exactly why both Chuck Schumer and Hakeem Jeffries are going ALL IN on Florida right now.** If Vindman flips this seat, Democrats take back the Senate majority — and nothing stands between them and another impeachment, endless obstruction, and a complete dismantling of everything we've built. [**A "Senator Vindman" is not a hypothetical. It is a live threat. Right now. Today.**](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkMvK2zAQRp9G3snoZjleaFEI3qWLUujSKNLYEdXFjOSkefvi0D7Av_04HM7Mr9tysyGGvH23CQy2dcG61JIA_jSEBPFto8UU8pbhVRfBhKZMUaFPjnfeeOctdx0YPo4jnyalRfcww12KQTHNnWQSRq4uTA-OMyamcfDSd8GcKqaE5kyyYervINVgrZjUxTI7KqKYjYCtR9iPewzO5mbr77Wgg96V1EXzaG2vRH4jYiZiruAOhP4VMoI_CSLmVIp_07UgXWPB4C0R8xqeQD9H0ZBXwFxofRRsy073shM54ynCkDcirw0PIEIfLS3NEXn98XOm_8P_7a7kBrkReYVkQ-QdGr-_Qoyh6pEotp3rp7h9_b9PI_4GAAD__wrvihQ)
---
[PROTECT THE MAJORITY](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkMvK2zAQRp9G3snoZjleaFEI3qWLUujSKNLYEdXFjOSkefvi0D7Av_04HM7Mr9tysyGGvH23CQy2dcG61JIA_jSEBPFto8UU8pbhVRfBhKZMUaFPjnfeeOctdx0YPo4jnyalRfcww12KQTHNnWQSRq4uTA-OMyamcfDSd8GcKqaE5kyyYervINVgrZjUxTI7KqKYjYCtR9iPewzO5mbr77Wgg96V1EXzaG2vRH4jYiZiruAOhP4VMoI_CSLmVIp_07UgXWPB4C0R8xqeQD9H0ZBXwFxofRRsy073shM54ynCkDcirw0PIEIfLS3NEXn98XOm_8P_7a7kBrkReYVkQ-QdGr-_Qoyh6pEotp3rp7h9_b9PI_4GAAD__wrvihQ)
---
With a deep sense of humility and responsibility, I am standing squarely in his way, backed by President Trump’s complete and total endorsement. In Florida. In President Trump's home state. Vindman has made no secret of what drives him. He's not running out of some deep love of Florida — he just moved here!
