Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | First Invеstmеnt Updаtеs brоught tо уоu bу Іnception Media, LLC. We would like to emphasize that we do not offer tailored financial advice. This email does not serve as financial guidance, and you bear full responsibility for your investment decisions. # Sunday, April 26, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1899, actor [Guinn "Big Boy" Williams](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO6yAQRb_G7mzBgE1SULzGXV67pYVhYs8uhggmcfL3q-QXtr26Okc6X5f54ihSWv-7HS0_r3lOeNSIzFhm2ndai2PKKbojFHflevce6wwCxk7oDsY5OIqvWbfBLiKcZYtWGmNASHU6t5vV0iwLaFQDqEGYRWrpnRFSBw0GULZk3zChYZRSKHHqjT6flPcqhGEYtFeNFhiIc-l5Q8anq9f4yg8svc97G-3GfKuN-tfA1MB0HEfPdXM5RUrY57I2MG0uhSXnnwYmTFwI6_tJMZLba7feKaXuu7TFhttnraNptFh3R_Ej4b-leVj4DQAA__8I73ji) was born in Decatur. His most notable roles were in western films _Dodge City_ and _Santa Fe Trail_. Williams was also a skilled polo player. A [historic home](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2uozAQBeCnMZ2RsR1-Chfb0GXbLZEZD2EUg7P2JCRvf0Ve4TajkY706ej8u05XT5H221-_oeP3kqYdjxKRGfNE20a37JnSHv0Rsl-4PAGwTFrpViordTsFT_Ez2Sq4WYWhqdA1Xddp1Zh-qFYX5sV2ujNmMUO7zAFQAfp-8WDb1vS-Indiyuq2aZRRfd3ZoTcAJoTL5WLBCKswEKdc84qMb1-W-EkvzDWkrYpuZX4UYf4IPQo9HsdR32EOZyj0eNpCj8qe_3m-gGSEVZZPYdykh_9PyljkSoVTJpDxOc8J7nJNG0pY_Q4YY8pFZiwUcAcUeqyyC4-DYqTSdsKq2-Ypfivx74Z8Of0TAAD__5AtiRQ) in Lubbock has a new owner. Learn more in _Et Cetera_. In case you missed it, this week on****_The Flyover Podcast_ , host Ayla Brown unpacked a new study raising eyebrows about the news you see every day. Researchers took a deep dive into major platforms like Google, Apple News, and Yahoo--and what they found could change how you think about your feed. Are these aggregators really neutral, or is there something else going on? [Listen here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykz0tuwyAQANDT4F0sGLDBCxaRKktdpNsuLQzjZCQcIpj8bl-1V-gFnvS-T8spUKbr-Svs6Pm1leWKz5aRGetC-07nGpjKNYdnqmHjdo8R2wISxoM0BxiXFCi_F9Mlv8o0qQ69staCVNpN3cVHtY6AQWltNz04m8IU4qTWwckBYUsd-V9MGhiVklq63prJ6Rh1SsMwmKiFkZiIS-35goyv0Lb8Lg-sfSx7l_2F-daEPgqYBczvcud7v6KAefo09ABpb-tR6JmF_gDnuurT7Uk5UxutMPK8B8p_Ev_v__DwEwAA__-EQW1I). Today's sponsor, [FinanceBuzz](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkE1uwyAUhE-Dd7Ge3wMbL1hUirxLu6nU7hDhJ0HFcQQkTm5fue0Nupz5pBnNfBz0wcQUL6dXM3tVH2HRF7-W5Gv1Wcd5jqdsalwuyawum1DLzVpfNAL2O-A77LUzMT01b5w6ghu7xqtuGAaEjuTYnFUA3gt5HIMU5L2TwQokYYF6SVYa2US1hQHHvuuAQLYDHyVZS84JIbglxsG7WJfc1rOv_mFKSM_l7nNrl7lJ6lzrtTB6YTgxnNZ1bZ_5UfPXhhlOJgRtGU1LCNskx2iPAkgw7Df0a0CHf7rcjoz275_TW5OVu64xpVj6gXE4zSamn876v6fuCr8DAAD__xnVetQ), is sharing simple, expert-backed ways to cut everyday expenses and free up more money in your monthly budget without changing your lifestyle.

## Fifth Circuit Clears Texas Immigration Law
Texas can now enforce [Senate Bill 4](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2PhCAQhn8NdhgE_CoorrHba680fAxKgmJgXHf__cWtrr9mMsWTmfd9fh7zQ4cY9uVbb6Dw5dO8w1UiIEKew7aFJWsMaY_6cll7LKe1UGbOeEeZpLybnQ7xPcvKKcPc2FSgmr7vOWvEMFarGrwxxopWMu_BjdBB54zwfhzZaJqWVUHdx5jkXdMwwYa6l-MgrBXOtW0rrSCSgQuYco0rILx08fGdnpBrm7YqqhXxKER8ET4RPl3XVX8gzMGcO9QpL4RP9w_CJybv_R4fhv5pSKO-aDFU0hZXakO2Z0Bq05mRJk_1cYCOhebz1kX4VGXljivEGErXE8mWTYf4iYT_E_lU_DcAAP__meqImw), the 2023 law allowing state police to arrest people suspected of crossing the border illegally, after a federal appeals court lifted the block on Friday. The [Fifth U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u5CAQhE-DMyzAjH8Cgk2cvU03tNrQ9iC1jQXtx5vbr2aCvcDGpfpUX_35Wr4gUjz333Cg458tLSfWQsiMeYnHEfcMHNNJUEOGjcvtPZbFKNNLZaXplwCRXottgltVmHSDTg_DYJTuxql5Otg22JQJaLq-9-sIYEGvRodpDKjXoYnuDVPW9FqrTo3tYKex874L4fF4WN8JqzBETrnlJzL-QNnolb4xtz4dDbkn81VE90uYWZi51tpmvBlzeefCzIQ7kDDz_u6cB54szHwXCdeFQEX6dGeWQJRqkR--xHNL2aP86J8sIWcsLAmq_GduhZmb7MJVI1Es_SCs2g-I9JnF_3fmtzN_AwAA___5Ros3) ruled 10-7 that the plaintiffs, including immigrant advocacy groups Las Americas Immigrant Advocacy Center and American Gateways, plus El Paso County, lacked legal standing to challenge the law. The court vacated the preliminary injunction without ruling on the underlying constitutional questions. SB4 creates a state misdemeanor for illegal border crossings and allows magistrate judges to order those arrested to return to Mexico. The law briefly took effect for about nine hours in 2024 before courts blocked it again. With the injunction now lifted, [state enforcement can begin](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAUhJ_G7rAwYDAFxW3c5bZbWpifBO2xieAkJG-_cqrtt5ti9I2--bqsF5sgHdf_dg8GXzGvR2gVAmIoa9r3dC0WUz7ANl9sxPpwLtSVUSYJFYTJ1dsE71X03mzU67EPZlRKMTryWfc3IyLjs5603TbvJY-T5NYLTZVyVEth-2ROGBVMjiPldB6U0DN3jns_TZNwvBM0-IS5DHgLGF62RnjnZyiDy3sP5oZ4rx3_17GlY0trbYj5JU6Ns9Cx5YwdW1x-FCSQIlayQXbfpG6CQMBKPlQSjpiLC-SXNgHb-mL8vSWAVKXqBL3uNsFnG__22NOwnwAAAP__F-6CMQ) while remaining legal challenges continue.
## Corpus Christi Braces for Water Emergency
Corpus Christi's city council [will vote](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGunTAQBNCvgW6vjG0uULhIQ_fSpkR-9gKrGButDVz-PiK_8OoZjUbnz9f0ZSlQXH7bDU35zGmKeOWApSBPtG20sC2UYrCXZzuXfDiHeZJCvkFokO_JWwr3pGtvvoUfmhpN03WdFI3qh3o1EpUX6B0OGqWWvteq7dpGf8-qF8LONZlnTGj5bhqhRP_q9NAr55T3bdtqpyot0FNJ_CorFvzYPIc7ncgvl7Y6mLWUPVfqVyXHSo7Xdb3-MuVyPnElRz5ipLiA57uSo0u8Hxnc-lQIHJUbAlqPnGFn3C0jzInBQsATA6SIcNmCDLghLxjdDTZ6WNORcU3Bw5HtgsCYC5N7qHLNxu8XhUD53VVaLJul8P9s-RnxaeS_AAAA___LMJW1) on Tuesday on a plan that would require a water usage cut of at least 25% as a historic drought has drained the city's main reservoirs to just 8% of capacity. City staff said [a Stage 1 emergency](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGupDAQRE9jMiNjGwyBg03I_qYbosZuoCUDI7sZZm6_mrnCzyp4qiq9fz_TD1CiY_0LO3p-Led04F0SMmOeaN9pzcB0HgnumGHhcoWAZdJKd1JZqbspAqX3ZKvoZxWHpkLfOOe0akw_VJvXKoJC19p5MLD0brbQ965rFfRxMBgr8p8yZXXXNMqovnZ26E0IJsa2bW0wwiqMxGeueUPGF5Qlvc8n5jqce5X8xvwowvwRehR6vO-7_kKcab4OrM-8Cj1-NoQelf3kRujxy8hw5sdVZNgyFSZ5A2OWIVOhIsOVGSjteLAMFxehxyr7-LgpJSqdE1atO1D63uDfyXt6_T8AAP__z66EXw) could arrive as early as September without significant rainfall. That threshold is triggered when water supply is projected to fall short of demand within 180 days. Industrial users, including ExxonMobil, Valero, and Occidental, now consume roughly 60% of the city's water supply, a consequence of years of efforts to attract refineries and petrochemical facilities to [Corpus Christi Bay](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2O5CAQQOHT4AwLA_7pgGATZ7PphhaGsl1aDK2iuj2-_Wom2AvMBZ6evj8fy4fHhHn_7U9w_LmVJcNVEzADLXieuJNnLDn5K5LfuL5CgLpopQeprNTDEj2me7FNdKuKj64B143jqFVnpkdzuHX1YNbYqWBNP6298SEEHzewcVWD7Rp0XzFl9dB1yqipHe1jMiGYGPu-t8EIqyAiF2r5AIZPX7d0lzdQG8rZJHcwP6swv4SehZ6v62r_vrgttAs9Qwbabwn5jVTyCZmFnv_PG6HnUOj5qjIchJVRfvfl5RlIwgm0Qw63DIQVqySoTBi-PGpDLj4vTAnrMAqr9tNj-j7inzm-nf4XAAD__1zsip0). The average residential customer uses about 7,000 gallons per month; the proposed cap of 5,250 gallons would affect [roughly 30% of households](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbGu4yAQRb8Gd0SAsR0XFNu4e9tuaRFmHI8WQwST8PL3K6_Sbbnl6GiudO799bV-eYqU7j_9gY6_t7wmbDUiM5aVjoPuxTPlFH2D4jeuzxCwrkaZUSorzbiCp_hebQfupmDWHTo9TZNRur_O3e6u_jbqeZ7H3m52mybUkwe86S2Mwwx67sidYcqaUWvVq-tlsvO1D6EHGIbBhl5YhUCcy4V3ZPz2dYvv_MJyCfnootuZH1X0P4RZhFlaa5ffhSq_TizMcvoIs8QcfJSfg5J852eRCem-33LZcwZhlpDL41ll2M9_EmaB3BLnlv5hsnnGIn0CGYjfMqIHLFV6gIK1fnDByoXC2V-Vj8yYmHyUg1Jyo4S1Kw4ejWKkOk7CqvvhKf614v_b4uXMnwAAAP__lsukIg). City Council member [Carolyn Vaughn said](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGu5CAMhk9DOiLiQBgKim3SvW23jHjgJNaSMALymLn9KnOF7SzZ_j77__O1fDmKdG6_3YG2vta0nNhKxFoxL3QctGVXKZ3RtZDdWsvlPZYFBExcSA7TEhzF9yK7YL9FMEOHdtBagxjGh-l2q0xYjXTfCpUWBrQ3gMZIPajVgVSiI3vDhIRpGMQoHr2W5jF6P4aglJJ-ZFJgoJpyX3es-HJlje_0g7n36eii3Wt9Fjb-YjAzmFtrvY_k_8KerlLTeU8xmO-3GMyffQbz7WQwC3nXA4PZp_y8Cvd7plKJR3QBc-ENadv5nhqviWcMl0fub-7x6bqKmV8FuTso8C2nRufGfaZCt6XLNjwbxUhl0kyK7XAUP2fX_wv7x8K_AAAA__95BpUC), _"Everybody's going to have to make sacrifices."_
## Iran War Impacts Texas Farmers
Texas farmers are [seeing rising costs](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbGO3DAMRL_G7rSQaFmyCxdp3F3alAZXpHYJyNZC1p3u_j7wpUnqdMQQ84CH-fW2vaEkOR4_ceelfsa8HdzOxLVy2WTf5VGwSj4SNioY6_keAp8baHBKWwVuI5T0tdmelrum2fS8GO89aDNMc_9cPJF3xhs3knM4BrCG7tFG43Rk8KGX5YJpC84YPejp5u08DSEMROM42jB0VjNJzeVWn1z5E8-YvvIHl1vIe5-WZ62vsxt-dLB2sLbWbi0iXs8OVixVQuIO1surg_WVk1QJ1ynHKcTqr-Sb_k8guxwPlaOSgodqWFTI74mOqu6sWi4nq5iL-lOMWHYuVw8mr2a2fhiMUzTerbJxJoXBWUUuTg7mAIShLwu9mqQkp_Od1Y8dJX171f9b42OB3wEAAP__37OfIg) due to Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which choked off a key shipping lane for fertilizer. Bank of America estimates some fertilizers have already risen 30% to 40% since the Strait closed, with urea costs [jumping 47%](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O4yAUBeCnsTtH_BlwQbGNu2y7pcXCJb4aCBHciZN5-pHnFaY-R590zr_rdvWY8X776ws4eqW63eHoGYigbVgK3ponrPfsj9h8ov4ZAvRNMKEnpiaht-gxvzc1RvefxYWP4LgxRjAu7TLuLmnPwHptrJp1iBBtXKxUkkeTtNRpRHdiTAnNOZPMXoxarAxBxjjPswpyUAwiUm0X2oHg5XvK7_qEdgm1jNntRI8-yD-DWAex0g475nxmg1j3WuAcNIh1tlIwzacEjTDjF7Qp1E59wvLwgabkWzlq-4B2tsfm4uPAnLFrMyh2Kx5_0JF-d9PTie8AAAD__y32fRo) since late February. Panhandle cotton growers and Rio Grande Valley produce farmers already operate on razor-thin margins, and now they're staring down [40% increases on nitrogen products](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO2zAQRL-G6mhQS0oUCxVp1F3alMLecmkvQkkGybPO-frA_oXrBhhg5uH9-Vg_ULLs19-48dy-07HufNbMrXFZZdvkWrDJsWc8Y8HU6hcR1xUMjNo4DeMaUfJzdV2cP00Mfcdz770H09spdLfZD9abmEygxBA4hpFc70ZKiXqDFjqZX2PGwdj3xprp4l2YLJGNcRgGR1Y5w1HaUS7txo2_sab8PB5cLnRsXZ5vrd2rsr8ULAqW8zwvfx9f_CoVLFiaUGYFy3bs_FSwMB37sb1S4tIkyz8u-l6EuGopuOsTiybeW8Gs33c6Ydm4VAULjEGjDSmy8frTMGnHZtJT8qCZYjRTH0ecQlfmeD8lZ6mjV85cN5T85m0_s_yY4X8AAAD__xY-kVg). In addition, farm diesel prices have climbed 46% since late February. [Analysts expect food prices to follow](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL_GZEamMWYIHFxCtpdeiHrsZmitwcjuXXbv608zwf3ARiWVnp5K9edteUNOfDx-405evta8HHTVRCJUFt53fhQUzkfCKxZcpX6EQHUBA04bq8EtETl9L7aJ_m7i1DXku3EcwXT9bWo2b9c7BXN3k8XbZCLA3a3oEGI3xBiRGvZPmbHgus705taOdrr1IfQxDsNgQ6-sociSSysbCX1hXdN3_qTShrw3yW8iZ1X9LwWzgvm6rnbLH1XyEbaSDw6JnqCC-cyJhUNVML80WvCdFMxY5En9r1csO5VnBtL15HfSKxXhxH-p6JCrVM0FD31h0QBgHAy2PbezKT6eF6fE1Y3KmseOnF4r5Wfffnr4FwAA___i55Go) as higher input costs work their way through the supply chain.

clean_body: Texas can now enforce Senate Bill 4, the 2023 law allowing state police to arrest people suspected of crossing the border illegally, after a federal appeals court lifted the block on Friday. | | | | | | Sunday, April 26, 2026 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1899, actor was born in Decatur. His most notable roles were in western films Dodge City and Santa Fe Trail. Williams was also a skilled polo player. A in Lubbock has a new owner. Learn more in Et Cetera. In case you missed it, this week on**The Flyover Podcast , host Ayla Brown unpacked a new study raising eyebrows about the news you see every day. Researchers took a deep dive into major platforms like Google, Apple News, and Yahoo--and what they found could change how you think about your feed. Are these aggregators really neutral, or is there something else going on? . Today's sponsor, , is sharing simple, expert-backed ways to cut everyday expenses and free up more money in your monthly budget without changing your lifestyle. Amarillo ⛅ 86°/53° | Austin ⛅ 91°/71° | Beaumont ⛅ 86°/72° | Brownsville ⛅ 90°/77° | Corpus Christi 🌤️ 89°/75° | Dallas 🌤️ 91°/72° | El Paso 🌤️ 86°/67° | Fort Worth 🌤️ 90°/71° | Houston 🌤️ 88°/74° | Lubbock 🌤️ 91°/60° | San Antonio ⛅ 90°/71° | Tyler 🌤️ 88°/71° Take **The Flyover with you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog--your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Fifth Circuit Clears Texas Immigration Law Texas can now enforce , the 2023 law allowing state police to arrest people suspected of crossing the border illegally, after a federal appeals court lifted the block on Friday. The ruled 10-7 that the plaintiffs, including immigrant advocacy groups Las Americas Immigrant Advocacy Center and American Gateways, plus El Paso County, lacked legal standing to challenge the law. The court vacated the preliminary injunction without ruling on the underlying constitutional questions. SB4 creates a state misdemeanor for illegal border crossings and allows magistrate judges to order those arrested to return to Mexico. The law briefly took effect for about nine hours in 2024 before courts blocked it again. With the injunction now lifted, while remaining legal challenges continue. Corpus Christi Braces for Water Emergency Corpus Christi's city council on Tuesday on a plan that would require a water usage cut of at least 25% as a historic drought has drained the city's main reservoirs to just 8% of capacity. City staff said could arrive as early as September without significant rainfall. That threshold is triggered when water supply is projected to fall short of demand within 180 days. Industrial users, including ExxonMobil, Valero, and Occidental, now consume roughly 60% of the city's water supply, a consequence of years of efforts to attract refineries and petrochemical facilities to . The average residential customer uses about 7,000 gallons per month; the proposed cap of 5,250 gallons would affect . City Council member , "Everybody's going to have to make sacrifices." Iran War Impacts Texas Farmers Texas farmers are due to Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which choked off a key shipping lane for fertilizer. Bank of America estimates some fertilizers have already risen 30% to 40% since the Strait closed, with urea costs since late February. Panhandle cotton growers and Rio Grande Valley produce farmers already operate on razor-thin margins, and now they're staring down . In addition, farm diesel prices have climbed 46% since late February. as higher input costs work their way through the supply chain. | Flying together with our sponsor Retirement should be about your bucket list , not your grocery list. Finding extra room in your monthly budget doesn't require a total lifestyle overhaul--just a few strategic moves. : with you. | ➤ Austin: The city announced on Friday that police officers will cooperate with ICE "when operationally feasible," a week after Gov. Greg Abbott's office threatened to pull $2.5 million in public safety grants. () ➤ Houston: Voters are juggling three separate election cycles, with early voting continuing through April 28. () ➤ West Texas: Veterans from WWII, the Korean War, and Vietnam returned home Saturday after a trip to Washington, D.C., to visit their memorials, thanks to Permian Basin Honor Flight. () ➤ Dallas: Republicans filed a lawsuit to require precinct-based voting for the primary runoff Election Day, after a switch sent over 12,000 voters to wrong polling locations in March. (-) ➤ San Antonio: Police detained three suspects in connection with a fatal shooting on the city's East Side on Friday that left one man dead. () ➤ Tyler: The University of Texas cut the ribbon on Friday on its new $308 million, 248,000-square-foot School of Medicine building. body:
Hometown water supply in danger
[View in Web Browser](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.com/click/e3256692/VaHR0cHM6Ly9vZGIubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3Yvc0ZVS043NTNxMg/SZW1haWxfZnJlZXRoaW5rZXJkYWlseV9jb20yNjA0MjY/LbmNsQjc2dXFObg/qP2hpZHM9bmM1cW11MXhEaVdCa21scw/gae38jw/s4hb4360713)

**FBI's Urgent Warning Sounds the Alarm** America's enemies have found a way to strike without firing a single shot. Hackers are attacking city water systems, overriding safety protocols to weaponize our utilities against us and positioning themselves to strike when the signal comes. National security experts warn China isn't alone. Iran, Russia and North Korea are preparing coordinated attacks. Overflowing, damaging and poisoning the lifegiving systems we trust to keep us safe. **Like it or not, your local tap water may be our country's weakest link.** Deteriorating infrastructure. Systems years beyond their expiration dates. Negligent corporations. And God-knows-what in the water we drink. We're up against arsenic, plastics, chlorine, forever chemicals, hormone-altering drug residue and a host of other contaminants. Listen, I'm not trying to be the messenger of doom... _**But protecting the water supply in your home is more important than ever.**_ If you don't take steps to be prepared, you and your family could end up vulnerable. Forced to choose between drinking poisoned, toxic or dirty water, or going without water altogether. It sounds harsh... But the truth is that even good people with good intentions can make serious mistakes. Mistakes like...
* **Getting filters that claim to purify your water, but don't prove it.**
* If a company doesn't have certifications from a third-party lab, show lab tests or reveal how many gallons were tested... that's a bad sign. After a couple gallons, most water filters on the market stop working for scary contaminants and start passing them into your water.
* **Getting bottled water with a 2-year shelf life and risking plastic leaching into your supply.**
* Not to mention all the money you'll waste, and the work required to carry cases of water. Plus with bottled water, you never really know what's been filtered out.
* **Or simply using a pitcher in the fridge... with slapshot filtration power and limited capacity.**
* Most pitchers hold a couple cups of water at a time. Not nearly enough to drink, cook and clean in an emergency. You'll run out in no time, just when your life depends on it.

"Finally, I don't have to keep refilling my pitcher all day. I'm very happy with my water filter. Not only does our water now taste good (it tasted dirty from the tap) but the large capacity tank means I'm not refilling small pitchers repeatedly during the day."_

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: Am I putting the cart before the horse?
body:
I don’t know what to think of this, Peter...

| | | | I guess I shouldn't put the cart before the horse.

But I've been sending emails exactly like this one for weeks and haven't had any luck.
I thought support from MAGA Patriots was at an all-time high, but I fear I was wrong.
So, I'm choosing to cut to the chase and pray for a different outcome.
My name is Marsha Blackburn. I'm a top ally of Donald Trump and a fierce supporter of the America First Agenda. I’m sure you think this message is just another drop in the bucket, but I'm pleading with you to hear me out.
Here's the deal: Democrats across the country have never been hungrier for revenge after their miserable defeat in 2024. They’re riding high after their recent victories in New Jersey and Virginia, and I fear that this momentum will carry them to victory this year in 2026.
We're TOAST if conservatives stay on the sidelines! George Soros is writing blank checks to any Democrat who asks, and if we don't go on the offensive, they'll turn every state into a battleground overnight.
So, I'm going to ask you what I know is no small favor: Will you pledge just a few bucks before deleting this message to help?
[](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.-FmxFmxVhVPbxhzbw)[These messages aren’t cheap to send, so I'm praying you've made it this far and will be willing to pitch in even just $5.](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.) | [ CHIP IN HERE >> ](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.)
---
Every single dollar matters in battleground races where corrupt Democrats are prepared to spend, say, and do anything to win. If Republicans are going to stay in the majority and continue to implement President Trump’s agenda, we must act like it. Not tomorrow, next week, or next year. Now.
[ CHIP IN HERE >> ](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.-LnEg)
---
God bless you. Thank you for your time today.

Marsha Blackburn
Pro-Trump Republican

| |
---

Brentwood, TN 37024

| |

Early voting in Georgia starts tomorrow!

**This is about more than just an election—it’s about Georgia’s future.**
The decisions we make now will shape the direction of our state for years to come, **and I’m asking you to step up.**
Reach out to your friends, your family, your coworkers. Send a quick note or text. Tell them the primary election is on May 19, why it matters, and why you’re voting for me. Those personal conversations make all the difference in elections like this.
We’ve built real momentum across the state, but we need to carry it through the finish line.
[And if you’re able, I’m asking for your financial support in these final days. Every dollar we raise right now goes directly toward turning out voters when it matters most.](https://BradForGeorgia.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cb0b5d8f4cac8238170f7992f&id=e98b504763&e=6036811227)[Please consider making a contribution today.](https://BradForGeorgia.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=cb0b5d8f4cac8238170f7992f&id=5bdf30830a&e=6036811227)
Thank you for everything you’ve done—and for what I know you’ll do in these final days.
Onward.

Peter, we need your attention now:
🔵 Georgia will decide who has Senate control, and experts are now calling it a _"**Lean Democrat** "_ race.
🔴 North Carolina is a Republican held seat, but Democrats are working hard to **_FLIP_** the state.
🔴 Ohio is now a **_TOSS_** UP state – which means Democrats have a real shot at taking back the Senate.
🔴...And Nebraska is **_losing status as a GOP stronghold!_**

name: Haley Stevens
subject: I'm fighting for folks like you, null
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
I’ve been on the road, and here's what I've been hearing:

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=2841%2E49239%2EoI8ZbR)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17524?t=2&akid=2841%2E49239%2EoI8ZbR)
---
Hi Null,

I’ve been on the road, visiting communities all across Michigan – and everywhere I go, I hear the same things:
🩺 People are anxious about affording health care.
💰 Grocery prices are too damn high.
👷 Working Michiganders want real action to create and protect good-paying jobs.
🗳️ And many are deeply concerned about our civil rights, democracy, and basic freedoms.
These aren’t political talking points – they’re real concerns from real people. And that’s exactly why I’m running for U.S. Senate in Michigan. Because when I see a problem, I get to work:
✅ I helped save 200,000 Michigan jobs as part of President Obama’s Auto Rescue Task Force.
✅ I led the charge to bring semiconductor manufacturing back to Michigan.
✅ I introduced the No Tariffs on Groceries Act to stop reckless food tariffs that are raising grocery prices.
✅ And I filed articles of impeachment against RFK Jr. for endangering public health.
I was named one of the most effective lawmakers in Congress – because I pride myself on showing up, listening, and delivering for working families.
Now I’m bringing that same grit to this battleground Senate race. With polls showing I’m the Democrat best positioned to defeat MAGA Mike Rogers, **a Republican super PAC announced it's spending $45 MILLION to try to buy this seat.**
[**So I’m asking, null: Will you chip in to my campaign so we can keep up with this surge in GOP spending, flip the Senate, and provide real change for Michiganders?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17525?t=3&akid=2841%2E49239%2EoI8ZbR)
_Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue._ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17545?t=4&akid=2841%2E49239%2EoI8ZbR)
---

---
| | [Donate Another Amount](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17525?t=7&akid=2841%2E49239%2EoI8ZbR)
---
Thank you for standing with me.

With grit and gratitude,

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17529?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=8&akid=2841%2E49239%2EoI8ZbR) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17524?t=9&akid=2841%2E49239%2EoI8ZbR)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

name: Democrats.org
subject: This isn’t AI
body:
---
Derek, ** There’s something we want to get off our chests... We know that your inbox is probably flooded with emails from other organizations and candidates. And we know that the constant stream of messages asking for money can be a lot. **But we want you to know that a real person wrote this email. name: VoteVets
subject: A minute for the wins
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
Tracking everything we can and should be fighting back against takes an incredible amount of time and effort.
#
Peter, it’s heavy right now.
There are 10,000 things to be angry about every day. Tracking everything we can and should be fighting back against takes an incredible amount of time and effort. That’s what we’re good at. Doing the work, getting things done.
Something we’re **_not_** very good at is communicating out that work to you — the people who make it possible. So, today, it’s just that:
Earlier this week, Virginia greenlit a plan for redistricting that could put us over the top in the fight for control of the House. The process isn’t over yet, but the people have spoken. And when those maps are passed, it is VoteVets backed candidates who will win those new seats.
In Iowa, we’re backing Josh Turek for US Senate and just made another significant investment in the race. That brings our total to more than **_$4 million_** in Iowa. So, when Josh puts us over the top in the Senate this November, pat yourself on the back. You made it possible.
And in New Jersey, we just put $200,000 behind Rebecca Bennett’s campaign for Congress. That $200,000 is part of a broader $10 million campaign to help VoteVets candidates win their primaries and take back the House.
**VoteVets is quite literally paving the road to Democratic majorities in Congress.**
That’s what our supporters are doing. Seriously.
And today, we’re asking you to join them.
If any of that sounds worth a donation, please: [**Contribute $3 or anything you can afford to help VoteVets effecively end Trump’s presidency by flipping Congress this November.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-apr26.8SLh3X&refcode=em260426-1-60&t=1&akid=35106%2E3013755%2Ej_A79D)

subject: Fight back against Jamie Raskin’s attack in court
body:
[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=4fd6178781bf4eb999309fe0f579de67&_e=lpzxYd8fn8Gzijtx5E5bWw73R8--O5M2l29z_ODLxYadq7wT4Wh7GbhafhdSXhKqb46PO0ukCzgw4J1JYicMTfMllSFuZoPQEkLlLPQNrjnB690qrJ0cMca0_eVIkpAw3NYgAcQzVjdPBHzXogZdethral91Bm-uFf8GIVJfMGKR6OKkGWJxihecyobKpClR6j1wMdLFUISUNBAnM79sLcuKVaLW_bnLIYvnbS6x0D27snOJ-bK1cwys3FOhDABAvObIViMrGWLqFOxzqE5lHezEv56HN2IJfSL_x-MyluLGbGC1y8zXQxSu72WLFhxFgol5G2w_Fsl6Cp3nnOChCkdUhxqh8Td3FGJ7mMHWo35beBqllbkbUQtXw5l-r6_ZS_SX1BdsB0AYyk6iJV7QnMOLM7fa8UGdyUF9jLb7QnsDY1dAsU2QP2l9mj0MenpV6pIsYwlDftOFKqi6otxfdowNCQopJih6vM5RzJgWCwGErGZFkDREWt-lPsLCZYVstoPJVqfE0yRORMK7sbcuFViHLcvV20Gb6r-8PDKBA68ZsMMsS7OZ3n4gyWzUUgYwSZefMENIb6St6G00vkOeiNWn1yWRz4HkiegRLsrMDxaeB9UIh9umDbd1hgSmciKI)

Peter,

No one likes asking for help. Most of us have worked hard all our lives to be self-sufficient and provide for our families.

But for our January 6 patriots still waiting on pardons -- good individuals who answered the call to peacefully protest a stolen election -- the last five-plus years have been nothing short of a nightmare.

Luckily, **the Trump Department of Justice has rightly asked the court to vacate the charges and convictions against the unpardoned Proud Boys and Oath Keepers leaders** and dismiss the cases with prejudice.

After years of weaponized prosecutions under the Biden regime, this is the right step toward real justice.

**But a new threat has emerged -- not from the DOJ, but from the radical left in Congress.**

Congressman Jamie Raskin -- one of the most partisan Democrats in Washington -- filed an amicus brief in three separate appeals in the D.C. Circuit to defend the original jury verdicts.

**Our side 's attorneys had to drop everything and work around the clock all weekend to draft a strong opposition brief! **

The filing deadline is extremely tight -- early this week.

These patriots have been completely financially drained after more than five years of brutal legal battles, imprisonment, and persecution. Many are veterans who served our country honorably, only to be treated like enemies of the state by their own government. They simply do not have the resources to fund this critical fight on their own while still dealing with other lingering issues.

**Stand in the Gap has stepped in to raise emergency funds so their legal team can file a powerful response -- on time and with strength.**

That is why we need your help today, Peter.

Our goal is to raise at least $120,000 this week to defend the 12 individuals involved.

clean_body: Peter, No one likes asking for help. Most of us have worked hard all our lives to be self-sufficient and provide for our families. But for our January 6 patriots still waiting on pardons -- good individuals who answered the call to peacefully protest a stolen election -- the last five-plus years have been nothing short of a nightmare. Luckily, the Trump Department of Justice has rightly asked the court to vacate the charges and convictions against the unpardoned Proud Boys and Oath Keepers leaders and dismiss the cases with prejudice. After years of weaponized prosecutions under the Biden regime, this is the right step toward real justice. But a new threat has emerged -- not from the DOJ, but from the radical left in Congress. Congressman Jamie Raskin -- one of the most partisan Democrats in Washington -- filed an amicus brief in three separate appeals in the D.C. Circuit to defend the original jury verdicts. Our side 's attorneys had to drop everything and work around the clock all weekend to draft a strong opposition brief! The filing deadline is extremely tight -- early this week. These patriots have been completely financially drained after more than five years of brutal legal battles, imprisonment, and persecution. Many are veterans who served our country honorably, only to be treated like enemies of the state by their own government. They simply do not have the resources to fund this critical fight on their own while still dealing with other lingering issues. Stand in the Gap has stepped in to raise emergency funds so their legal team can file a powerful response -- on time and with strength. That is why we need your help today, Peter. Our goal is to raise at least $120,000 this week to defend the 12 individuals involved. Every dollar you give helps protect the rule of law, push back against leftist interference, and stand in the gap for these American patriots. Or keep reading to learn exactly who these individuals are -- many of them veterans -- why they were unfairly targeted, and why this fight matters to every conservative who believes in fairness and justice. These were the patriots that the Biden DOJ pursued the hardest with politically charged trials. Stewart Rhodes (Veteran), founder and leader of the Oath Keepers, was convicted of seditious conspiracy and sentenced to 18 years in prison. Kelly Meggs was convicted of seditious conspiracy and sentenced to 12 years. Kenneth Harrelson (Veteran) was convicted of related felony charges and sentenced to 4 years. Jessica Watkins (Veteran) was convicted of related felony charges and sentenced to 8.5 years. Roberto Minuta, Edward Vallejo (Veteran), Joseph Hackett, and David Moerschel were all convicted of seditious conspiracy after their own months-long trial and received substantial prison sentences with federal terrorism enhancements. Ethan Nordean was convicted of seditious conspiracy and sentenced to 18 years. Joseph Biggs (Veteran) was convicted of seditious conspiracy and sentenced to 17 years. Zachary Rehl (Veteran) was convicted of seditious conspiracy and sentenced to 15 years. Dominic Pezzola (Veteran) was convicted of serious felony charges in the same case and sentenced to 10 years. These individuals should never have been targeted with inflated seditious conspiracy charges in the first place. Now, the Trump administration is finally moving to correct this injustice and vacate the convictions. But Raskin is fighting tooth and nail to stop it. He is demanding the court appoint a special lawyer (an independent amicus) to oppose the DOJ and defend the old jury verdicts -- essentially trying to keep the deep state 's narrative alive and continue punishing these patriots. This need is especially urgent. These patriots are broke and still fighting on multiple fronts. They cannot afford to pay lawyers to defend against this latest leftist attack themselves. Even if you're unable to give financially right now, your prayers for these patriots, for wisdom in the courtroom, and for protection against political persecution are always powerful and deeply appreciated. Thank you for your time, consideration, and continued support of conservative values and American justice. In solidarity, Shane Jenkins Founder and Executive Director of Stand in the Gap P.S. This is exactly the kind of emergency Stand in the Gap was built to handle. Even after President Trump commuted their sentences, many of our brothers and sisters are still struggling to rebuild their lives. They endured prison, lost jobs, homes, friends, and family. They were publicly defamed and smeared by the mainstream media and the radical left as "insurrectionists" and "terrorists." In many ways, that 's when the hardest part began -- financial ruin, ongoing stigma, and now fresh attacks from radicals like Jamie Raskin.** After everything our fellow patriots have endured, they shouldn't have to face this alone. The courtroom fight continues with a razor-sharp deadline. Without your rapid support, these individuals won't have the legal resources they need to push back effectively. Peter,

For too long, the medical establishment got away with mutilating children in the name of "gender-affirming care."

They gave puberty blockers to 13-year-olds, performed double mastectomies on 15-year-olds. And when the complications started -- when the regret set in -- the medical establishment walked away. _**No consequences. No accountability. Just billions in profit.**_

[_**The Chloe Cole Act changes that.**_](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/jkr8zEwZOy_2NBu5pefqBiNEKJqHOzf4_I0bqQo1qnTvogyXGK8Tl8NIypptE-ePKIkItezO6cbhrkY3T1MHEvz0d1G0V0Mv1Dr9M5fA4th03mJyuiD82HZ7NUlD-E0yBihQZLjQa-XQD-qyKsXrQo6h8Id_rqRrVKFn1A7Fu6mQGRkl-wABb1p-4v3632M87vhYYwKPs-0pr41ang2mIGcSauknge5fzHtdujvtkJRUlHrfgZ_HSXdBgqeLsz6719ykK3R_HMSHi87mXOt6Yebwbdu4fZcYa55CkO89UFrm8mZzXtgyBZNe4pAiSJYvcj-zCRnPOO50bzMGtOxEzaxHG-vDbP7IXtIt8mBT-BGeg6BTawu2a1gOn43GW-Zf6ABexbuwRfv5cI9pPMEHx7_bZSMfBZ-MAIxIAL3ee34CfW7mVZmA11ErXt_FAWhRJQ_9vcFEoEoo6hxHnMR0Lz4BDaCmkng6wXvqKTwEk7gIT0pQuMnXYpwf_NHRyrD8zJU18NshPblVMzULKAcU77ZtQJW5yoI5u9oSzHqSoFeJ_E80bTKVqkD2Tr3k6f2Xyf4It7TVvtOQuNrfBOyGaPLzVmAxDtq_NV3WL4iNwrCCYtDw3UZvVlJcIvri6qkKr4_0kBELWM-yg24NVAsLwV5aOsuDacGCPXWcROB3GfngVp0IDoF1Ua1anHhXk4A2xptn/YgR42ay_FbyeSh9KSv8_6CjrejbsKFld/20)

This bill gives victims (and their parents) of this medical malpractice the legal power to sue the doctors, hospitals, and clinics responsible for these **irreversible harms. **

| | [](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/ugGyUF_hK5ZthHjNs0PnXM7nRhojyD82JZgy3pswLTBS0N73XqgpcVM5_nx7NerzFiJcLx5n1-AN7jbn6ggVb-G8-wYiptd8iIXfTooXxyUj4XWB2nUPP3UO8zcOOcKtmIiph628FUOYKANO1Axc2ldzVgCq5X91_65K1R0tcZHvaUe7nySALvc1PLC9cO_Ot-3mDFz-0Z30uuVpmR3IQIawK890XgFMYWsREnxBbgLPmYivqZ9nmBNoHR3TgTuRZCfYngAwxm--qktvGMSTjyhUslQ0lmTdf07KRLBKyTRgrxzhL_bE49XVnu3JhNuQu0f1gPj78yDjRSIt2fcx_Rwj9ep9g5VSGzctiHPk2UpwUTWBHFSsWjw1pb5i-wx8MAT_fBIClPNm1_noVHjzHNdIS8cb7ggnkHg3vAZzT3IyQCJ4E41dxshPMWVLaLX34kEIx-3xcAal49uzB9hEE8hA4Jmo_5xcETWAU1sJHrO5qQlvdwzhArGudegba9cNOWcsLMQOOMEGg6_Qb1tqc6ranwLuax7ygDK99p2ZVPn8_-_i-otCNLsPfufJNMzdr4hjj-faNlkGVIWMDJq0VmHe1RPiFN56qmfBe2m5sZ5mxqcHfrRtEQ32RCpWnofnkmidAloP0qGBrDFhWa0tkLVK7bkG0hVzVHONyvNsj8TfFePRkFVRk0syfsgXGQITVPFa/09_6Vo7vRFnfoWp--yJv96k2XtH_AGmF/20)

It extends the statute of limitations, so victims have time to seek justice. It codifies [_**President Trump's Executive Order 14187**_](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/EyUE5tvmXAVVtIdjq_tJKBnqycnthSN-TsOMQpe3U--YEeo9xqr3btsl9ybyD62la3RaxNRjXUiEpgUygycyyzLkevMfxVfwp3BzBEpxFJBGUd9UXluLRhrPU-0X7OQkFNruQGFCfP_c_myBZu3f0Krgj5pXBXB08qZ9AYj5U4Q66M_72cpUCjR5Eo3OYqpDC42GS01I7wx3LMVI-8V1ZZwf8LwRQShPV0GF-wmFltiSbHSoGqRFAHmb8-ix1Nh5vqVv7j8E82L2kJjRvrICdMn8vAIp6cg-aptg2MMgGQFqHH9hYUOqpB77gfPMLsiCOB-bk519fhNTwTGSab991mpGtcuPuS8SgzR88-cr2dakpNFvOKZ327zU3PZawX-yEGxSpVb9JJ8luj0Sh-mD_ggxwuMyzxR-KYWo0DYOL9vrTNeTx0BKMF3i431TaYmIOIXiRKQEITGyZDae3VS2D8ZM1njyU5xtg8s4rpbdxgBwkJbzfAjajnGKofEbAik27n6H59Y08Jtp2nTNFpv49uyqewqaCNSCTfPgHtVno7Yrp1Y8s-G0YJ905RFqSTiO68lRUp5eMxEF1miKm4RuHY6RwtwJ53CES6nxhLvvg0MBdmi-za6dtiuJgrgfF-BOSHhgntOTXTgf1dGvYh14wkDNuOoQfIhys7yF6ullfnl5e89Kpgvw9LE9IzG6QrCyMeYP/3p6AscX9-NNkHv57GrPbCa1u3M42toMn/20) protecting children from chemical and surgical mutilation. And it sends a clear message to the gender industrial complex: _**You will be held accountable.**_

Heritage Action is leading the charge to pass this bill and end the mutilation of America's children. **But we need _YOUR_ signature to help mobilize grassroots support, pressure Congress, and get this across the finish line.**

name: Fair Fight Action
subject: The next few days can make all the difference for our fight in Florida
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FAIR FIGHT ACTION
body:
Floridians are saying no to rigged voting maps. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130196542/616954473/1793776944?refcode=emm_ffa_20260426_T1_FLsplit_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNTA1MWE2Mi03YzQxLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EKKZTgazpbHvyZe278UjhsuAA4Qgy-BnvebSBXdfIgU=&emci=0a78195c-7c41-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=05051a62-7c41-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=1037277)

| | Here’s the thing about momentum: once you lose it, it can be almost impossible to get it back. And right now, **if we press our advantage in the next few days, we can significantly reduce the momentum behind a new attempt to gerrymander Florida’s congressional voting maps.** State lawmakers will begin a special session this week and Governor Ron DeSantis is urging them to pass an illegal redistricting plan that would tilt more U.S. House seats in favor of the GOP — possibly enough to decide control of the chamber. But with the support of grassroots activists like you, we’ve been supporting the No Partisan Maps coalition on the ground in Florida — to build and demonstrate widespread opposition to new rigged maps. We can turn the tide here. We can show lawmakers that their constituents don’t want them signing off on a blatant gerrymander. And we can stop this scheme in its tracks. clean_body: Floridians are saying no to rigged voting maps. | | | Here’s the thing about momentum: once you lose it, it can be almost impossible to get it back. And right now, if we press our advantage in the next few days, we can significantly reduce the momentum behind a new attempt to gerrymander Florida’s congressional voting maps. State lawmakers will begin a special session this week and Governor Ron DeSantis is urging them to pass an illegal redistricting plan that would tilt more U.S. House seats in favor of the GOP — possibly enough to decide control of the chamber. But with the support of grassroots activists like you, we’ve been supporting the No Partisan Maps coalition on the ground in Florida — to build and demonstrate widespread opposition to new rigged maps. | 2026 is a make or break election year for democracy.
All of the talking heads are talking about the House and Senate elections, and yes, those will be consequential. But here's the truth most political pundits and election analysts are missing:
The fate of Democracy will also hinge on often overlooked and underappreciated state Supreme Courts races.
As MAGA witnessed the formation of a massive blue wave in 2025, they started working on desperate countermeasures, launching an all-out blitz of anti-democratic election rigging efforts at the state level--rewriting elections laws to make it harder for Democrats to vote and gerrymandering maps.
The constitutionality of their actions will ultimately be decided by state Supreme Courts. That means whoever controls these courts will control the fate of our Democracy:

| 32 states have state Supreme Court elections this year. And thanks to a massive grassroots effort, Wisconsin was able to pull out a victory. But the threat is still very real: if MAGA loyalists capture these courts, they'll green-light voter suppression and rigged maps as "constitutional".
That's why Defend Our Courts is stepping up once again.
The national party is going to be stretched thin. With dozens of competitive House and Senate races, state court elections will risk being ignored. We can't let that happen, not when the entire guardrail of democracy is on the line.
[The more desperate MAGA becomes, the stronger we'll need to be in every state. We need to continue our momentum and we can't do it alone. clean_body: | | | | If you believe you've received this email in error, you may unsubscribe below. | | | | | == | 2026 is a make or break election year for democracy. All of the talking heads are talking about the House and Senate elections, and yes, those will be consequential. But here's the truth most political pundits and election analysts are missing: The fate of Democracy will also hinge on often overlooked and underappreciated state Supreme Courts races. As MAGA witnessed the formation of a massive blue wave in 2025, they started working on desperate countermeasures, launching an all-out blitz of anti-democratic election rigging efforts at the state level--rewriting elections laws to make it harder for Democrats to vote and gerrymandering maps. The constitutionality of their actions will ultimately be decided by state Supreme Courts. That means whoever controls these courts will control the fate of our Democracy: | == | 32 states have state Supreme Court elections this year. And thanks to a massive grassroots effort, Wisconsin was able to pull out a victory. But the threat is still very real: if MAGA loyalists capture these courts, they'll green-light voter suppression and rigged maps as "constitutional". That's why Defend Our Courts is stepping up once again. The national party is going to be stretched thin. With dozens of competitive House and Senate races, state court elections will risk being ignored. We can't let that happen, not when the entire guardrail of democracy is on the line. == | | | | | == | == | == | == Whatever you can give truly makes a difference. Because if we lose the courts, we lose everything else. -- Defend our Courts | | == | Defend Our Courts | P.O. Box 45569 | Philadelphia, PA 19149 US | | | | | |

**My biggest regret is that I didn’t do this sooner.**

_[**I’m running for governor because we have a rare opportunity to end the MAGA monopoly on statewide power and take Georgia in a better direction. clean_body: | | | | = Friend, my journey to becoming a Democrat has not been easy. People who have known me for years ask if I’ve lost my mind, and I tell them no -- I found my heart. As a Republican, loving my neighbor came with asterisk marks. But now, I wake up every morning as a proud Democrat with a better set of tools to serve the people of Georgia. My biggest regret is that I didn’t do this sooner. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = = My wife Brooke and I have three sons, and we’ve raised them with a family motto: It’s never the wrong thing to do the right thing. That motto gave me the courage to stand up to Donald Trump when he tried to overturn the 2020 election. It also led me to stop making excuses, admit I was wrong, and leave the party I’d known to run for governor as a proud Democrat. I’m in this race to show Georgians across the state that I’m fighting for them no matter who I must go against -- but I can’t do it without your support. = The election is just a few weeks away, and we’re running out of time to make our message heard, so I hope I can count on your urgent support. Thank you, Geoff = | Duncan for Governor P.O. Box 699 Ringgold, GA 30736 United States | Paid for by Duncan for Governor | If you'd like to unsubscribe, you can do so .

While we work to fulfill the mandate, the Left is already pouring _**_millions_**_ into a desperate attempt to seize the Senate. They want to erase our progress and paralyze the President.
I refuse to let that happen. Not on my watch.
Our April End-of-Month Goal is the _**_o_**** _nly_****_way_**_ to measure how high our firewall is. It’s the signal that tells the world we won't back down.

I didn’t run for Senate to sit quietly on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and a proud Alabamian—and I came to fight for the values that make America strong.

_**Faith. Family. Freedom.**_

The Left is doing everything they can to tear it all down. But I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for the people who feel forgotten, overlooked, and ignored.

From securing the border to protecting our kids and defending our way of life—I’m in this for the long haul.

**This fight isn’t easy, but it’s worth it. And I’m not going anywhere.**

Will you stand with me?

name: Micaya Davis
subject: Lead pipes are why I trust my cousin Jamie
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jamie Davis for U
body:
I've watched him work hard for other people his whole life. That's who he is.
| | | | | [](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001H64PGASrpbjnOrCbasZv1VJnN2NEREwsa6L_h_hfEJ14c_BFv-kzlOlhIdX4c406xoBv8Mmkf-XQCFCbsu4Oxs1549KZVr-8vxujQbe0stxAqAByNFPDWx6NeRU1GMKUmLX2f-41dXayEFE1CgZvVvrEEPfqO2VOVAmc9Q8Tg2vw5Id0ITCvRPlGYzVtdBGKEz7VcrobUNQmpJ5-Myzd9A==&c=zZ8Db8E0N_hHSNf2W1OcqE30kbojemDJGwLFQEqxgjxQRF0usnwlIg==&ch=Ue5G8gy-914mi3zzO4GtlYISpVBqw7SpKBzOp3Y1Qlm1RROc7SJRbQ==)
---
Peter,
My name is Micaya Davis, and Jamie Davis, the Democrat running for U.S. Senate, is my cousin.
I've known him my whole life, and I wanted to take a minute to tell you why I trust him -- and why I believe he's going to be Louisiana's next U.S. Senator.
I've watched Jamie work hard his entire life. Not just for himself or our family, but for other people.
Jamie is one heck of a farmer. He came home and built our family farm from 700 acres to over 3,200 acres today. He kept that land in our family -- the same land our grandfather worked as a sharecropper but could never own.
But Jamie's service to our community reaches well beyond the farm.
When lead contamination was discovered in the pipes of the water system affecting schools in St. Joseph, Jamie didn't sit around and mope about the problem. He got to work.
He advocated directly with Governor John Bel Edwards and General Honore. He fought until he secured state funding to replace a 90-year-old water system. Ground was broken in March 2017. By March 2018, the new system was complete.
That's who Jamie is. When there's a crisis, he shows up. When people need help, he fights for them.
It hasn't always been easy for Jamie. It hasn't been without setbacks. But he keeps showing up. He keeps doing the work. And he keeps fighting for people.
That's the kind of humility and tenacity we need in the U.S. Senate.
Washington is full of career politicians who've never had to work with their hands, who've never had to struggle, who've never had to come back from failure and keep going.
Jamie's different. He's lived the same struggles as the people he'll represent. And when things get hard -- and they will get hard -- I know he won't quit.
Because he never has.
That's why I'm supporting my cousin. That's why I believe in him. And that's why I'm asking you to join me in helping him make history as Louisiana's first Black U.S. Senator since Reconstruction.
[Will you chip in $25, $10, or $5 to help Jamie win this race?](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001H64PGASrpbjnOrCbasZv1VJnN2NEREwsa6L_h_hfEJ14c_BFv-kzlOlhIdX4c4067YwtjrDCKVhEPyi5EnufUsIPCrMrFO1fsOSG99iE_SlQZr0S73mSeuIHDOiSD7t1X0An6zUBKEAil21WtA78sR6o1wcrjr4bLp6rr7S731AWY8oz7vSXfWUWaslr3T1rELcX5z0dGJbw7a8aGS5-OlZvXTxYMWOyTM3vVcDvdNQ=&c=zZ8Db8E0N_hHSNf2W1OcqE30kbojemDJGwLFQEqxgjxQRF0usnwlIg==&ch=Ue5G8gy-914mi3zzO4GtlYISpVBqw7SpKBzOp3Y1Qlm1RROc7SJRbQ==)
---
| If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
---

clean_body: I've watched him work hard for other people his whole life. That's who he is. | | | | | == Peter, My name is Micaya Davis, and Jamie Davis, the Democrat running for U.S. Senate, is my cousin. I've known him my whole life, and I wanted to take a minute to tell you why I trust him -- and why I believe he's going to be Louisiana's next U.S. Senator. I've watched Jamie work hard his entire life. Not just for himself or our family, but for other people. Jamie is one heck of a farmer. He came home and built our family farm from 700 acres to over 3,200 acres today. He kept that land in our family -- the same land our grandfather worked as a sharecropper but could never own. But Jamie's service to our community reaches well beyond the farm. When lead contamination was discovered in the pipes of the water system affecting schools in St. Joseph, Jamie didn't sit around and mope about the problem. He got to work. He advocated directly with Governor John Bel Edwards and General Honore. He fought until he secured state funding to replace a 90-year-old water system. Ground was broken in March 2017. By March 2018, the new system was complete. That's who Jamie is. When there's a crisis, he shows up. When people need help, he fights for them. It hasn't always been easy for Jamie. It hasn't been without setbacks. But he keeps showing up. He keeps doing the work. And he keeps fighting for people. That's the kind of humility and tenacity we need in the U.S. Senate. Washington is full of career politicians who've never had to work with their hands, who've never had to struggle, who've never had to come back from failure and keep going. Jamie's different. He's lived the same struggles as the people he'll represent. And when things get hard -- and they will get hard -- I know he won't quit. Because he never has. That's why I'm supporting my cousin. That's why I believe in him. And that's why I'm asking you to join me in helping him make history as Louisiana's first Black U.S. Senator since Reconstruction. == | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | == | Jamie has been fighting for people his whole life. Now it's our turn to fight for him. | Micaya Davis Proud Cousin and Proud Supporter of Jamie Davis for US Senate | | == | | | | == | | == | == == == == Mail checks to: Jamie Davis for U.S. Senate PO Box 55 Ferriday, LA 71334 | Paid for by Jamie Davis for U.S. Senate | | | | Jamie for Louisiana | PO Box 55 | Ferriday, LA 71334 US |

name: James Talarico HQ
subject: John Cornyn thinks only billionaires and corporations deserve tax breaks.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter —
Recently, James called for a suspension of the federal gas and diesel taxes to provide people with relief at the pump.
In response, John Cornyn accused James of trying to “explode the deficit.”
**[James was asked about Senator Cornyn’s response on CNN, and he did not hesitate to call him out on it on his hypocrisy and failure to deliver for working Texans. Watch it for yourself here and share it to make sure Texans know who is actually fighting for their families.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/2241?t=1&akid=597%2E1347274%2Ej2gZKU)
[](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/2241?t=2&akid=597%2E1347274%2Ej2gZKU)
John Cornyn wasn’t worried about the deficit when he gave $1 trillion in tax cuts to the top 1%.
He wasn’t worried about the deficit when he started spending $1 billion a day on this new disastrous war in Iran.
How is there always enough money for new wars and tax cuts for billionaires — but never enough money to lower the cost of health care, child care, groceries, gas, or utilities for working Texans?
Working Texans need immediate relief — right now. And then they need a Senator who will prioritize long-term solutions to make life more affordable in this country.
Team Talarico
👋 An important request before you scroll away...
Our April fundraising deadline is coming up fast, and James needs your help. Our fundraising has taken a hit this month, and we need A LOT more donations to ensure we don’t fall short.
**[If you care about taking back Texas' U.S. Senate seat, please consider contributing any amount today. Our average ActBlue donation is around $29 right now, but whatever you can spare will make a difference. Thank you.](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q2.t_pReT&refcode=em260426-1-ak&t=3&akid=597%2E1347274%2Ej2gZKU)**
[DONATE](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q2.t_pReT&refcode=em260426-1-ak&t=4&akid=597%2E1347274%2Ej2gZKU)

Paid for by Talarico for Texas
Talarico for Texas
PO Box 14508
Austin, TX 78761
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update your contact information, [**you can submit this form.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/signup/update-info/?t=5&akid=597%2E1347274%2Ej2gZKU) Looking for Team Talarico merch? [**Check out our online store!**](https://store.jamestalarico.com?utm_source=em_footer&utm_campaign=em_footer) This is a grassroots movement to take back Texas for working people. James is grateful to have you in our movement. We try to send only the most important updates and opportunities to participate via email, but if you want to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.jamestalarico.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=7&akid=597%2E1347274%2Ej2gZKU).

subject: The dark history behind “equity” and “social justice”
body:
Marxism began with promises of a perfect world—and ended in fear, famine, and death.

| _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/u/aKE4ZAWMCI8EBr3a0EW11BWHokx5WohowQHYiMB73oSupH8Rl-O_nUJnfvHEm82DW0NG7EMbobLqaCC7C89XWULiVCHTOOi2QKLoOHZebavS64t8mFcXhQxBN88I18vu7VC-DASN5Q-7kjk-RUEK6BBn3UFA306jUlFii1VHZwEPWcrzbmaXstzH/bB_oeh48d5vA8lAyos9qoUMwNlmAFr5K/20)_

| | Dear Friend,

Here's a story I'll never forget, once I learned the truth behind it.

In 1917, a small group of revolutionaries led by Vladimir Lenin seized power in Russia. They promised a better, fairer world...sound familiar?

But soon after taking control, they began rounding up and executing anyone who stood in their way. Lenin even ordered his followers to hang landowners and seize their crops--so that for miles around, "people see, and tremble..."

Marxism began with promises of a perfect world--and ended in fear, famine, and death.

You may be familiar with that dark history, but too many Americans aren't. How do I know?

Because a century later, these dangerous ideas have been repackaged and rebranded. They're taught in schools and promoted by celebrity politicians using words such as "equity," "diversity," and "social justice."

The words sound harmless, but the ideas behind them are the same. Will they end up with the same terrifying results?

That's why Hillsdale College has created the National Quiz on Marxism, Socialism, and Communism, and I'd love for you to take it.

You can complete your National Quiz on Marxism, Socialism, and Communism using this secure link:

[https://info.hillsdale.edu/marxism-quiz-tf](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/lwpTJJ8-PIJbhI-6KSl4IgEPWkP6CjZ6tdmVE4eW7w0_MtNsVBGsZCH5bjA1zHEgZMapwRpXmyJyedBQWy9FdD00PvJboiNNcLnkPj7YN3lHcLdYKVbP9_rSyVlekrqLKN58IMcmNXc-EmOOpKNVaSgp2zTRDv5la634lla7Gnlhqm3_LaqWbsPvrmTROZyR9sD2WA2CVtylXATq0i6Uukr0pn62sOX903JbiO9qam9g4KqGck84ejLCdopUqsUN8HF9DgTY8kaL3qX_VBI_eANys3s1mewShhMZNkMNG7ccIMUX1SqHpPwBxYZZ_YTJdYZrMKpWEZ_YFACAuBwyOgq1o0ymTbT7oJ6SLcaVRzw0Wnkab2DA1nFR0FC1TCHtQaYP0zAOxwqZ4prib3wpTCaCybkyk5OaaEYxcMRqGCqKg4P1vCjKSJFX1FhyLk6KWd9ku7m2EKCsL34OrE0xbxULzAaLaI7H9oHkcQeE7IrMUKYucCPkgzCzq4-eZSNDLJT3wXCUyegbzxVzNnA-uYD-WOuSIN5kHnb7MGjliGWomugZZ1_LRnf8813wDDyb3lzORz2N8g1SW587CAhnnUKLMcQ-Td0LsIILWf8Z97KMVcoSb_Y2M62wi8goHw8k4FeSbjttQkE6FKkfhiaUXgmcUw9h-CZ6EKytGKM6ZaWJ73VaKfULQbI7YSf5dPlgH933/j5EBYncjCYrNJXUxtQaLl3qmOm5gqc4v/20)

This short quiz will test your knowledge of the history and real-world consequences of dangerous ideologies like Marxism and socialism.

And when you finish, we'll email you the answers, and you can watch our free online documentary-style course, "Marxism, Socialism, and Communism," which tells the whole story--from Lenin's Red Terror to today's Cultural Marxism.

Most young Americans have never learned this history. I know I didn't in high school. But you can help change that with your financial support today.

[After you take your quiz, please also consider giving a tax-deductible gift of $25, $50, or even $100 to help us share this free course with millions of Americans](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/dvK4mlLI4zTp7vjhq8KhzfW3Ij9PJhts9lh1pidK0mQlNjcFRrON_YnKuE6UCHii3j_bY8UouRYrt7igVLCjroPiNvQTp9uj_05apfIqZnNguVSplYlPF_Oi7yXUQySdha0Ce9PPu8SjuNiXwrAP6uJkoaDJjEioiz0tdDUElQnemdpTv9NLEPqcg5mc7D1RK4eKnBRMPyklnLNkUDnzSFNWlKCWxdR5yrduc35l9CS8RpTQZBcL9URJAwwXy-2XL118GTlmigns0dfcx6JwI8oPYU3KxJxw0DDAP_NNsjwjfA0J-slAIqBIURmL9ngikuhfD-iPe978aVGypko_ebyIFOVpLwee6zlb0creKP_3f6g7AJf5wQ0oh9tg6zsz1QQAs4Xp-QYz3rTU0V2diWIBs0-pfySsYxKVHkwJc3vW2NiFJE8hIoR6-8trh8J5IN5hqXGRK6iz76VwagqBqsdX8wUmGXBheWXadZhJczKkIUGqjU3ASt_px7vretTWQERhSG26xwo_1YMOd9OIITFZVeC9tqQiP082Zr1cGMRjafO8p90XP4XXrNENmVDsJSLYQLo4M1PiQ1gfkrxGHkY0loFzuNcSKYxqR4dBpIJ5ZIH7fSyiOzTvqefc3mQBaKJ7iR0WW98kxEMJM5Y3zjqUWdu6W4IyJ7gUoQTEqWN21JPMU41KT9LNrXJqFPlq51xC/RaANlraUussiNEqtksUrJY_WqcTu6Ity/20)--especially young people who are misled by the same false ideas that once brought such misery to Russia and the world.

Because of you, more Americans will learn that the principles of liberty in the Declaration of Independence--not Marxism--are the true path to ordered freedom and human flourishing.

Good luck on the quiz.

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

P.S. Lenin's revolution began with promises of equality but ended with terror. The same ideas are spreading again today under new labels.

Please take your National Quiz on Marxism, Socialism, and Communism now using the secure link below--and, if you can, include a gift to help us reach and teach millions about the principles of liberty and limited government.

[https://info.hillsdale.edu/marxism-quiz-tf](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/sjAFI5i_NP5-A_BnWQ-EeXUEZMwPaShl7cCy5con2UKaLmYe8dOL0EO7hu2i1nP7aiD4AdrFzlXQhitZK39vDcbYfGLG8b8AyvEAa6wH2alK5nlZS0o9_4GqYqFO_WwNcv_QtXJIfE08JestgsmQDJQrmITjHFpQ2qJ7osnSs_388PmypO1IgGkapLLXrUOQ8SboOqcV57_AlyGE-1DunJ1CbLy0PhKSBVAraqV-xjg0A7epI4z4G7Vei_-e6djBT5DlG905N7N9bcxc8KmQjekZzkUegWjRj6ucYpX4GoWVAEPyKfj-vslqzkWVANfoV1GXMBfmW5SILFK1Q12i4f9d23cFWQgS3MxWZNb0yu4DRH-foh-d1F6v_p_qL1hhMUymaXKdXtEWUozaELO-BQQ5ywnOYhpVmTdJ2_VAH44Zfc2VoXZW_vX49VwZRhPBnv8_17Q-_X9b-K2Au7X02MgV3PJVEQtUmaMamlrTLRZsL7vvP5K-NVqCppE5JLL0EsoZCsq3qRBxxTIzUxkJrmOemEvG44tuP5nwuO8AQShHl4PKRYb72NJsTYq-EJd5A201_XKerXDw956KUd8Y-BttRm9WG4ByrGFjr8i7i8Z8acThgxEliwXdWYrmMIijDdKhEtnrsSA3pfx5oEeh1vC999I55yGGCHWh4h9VN9MMgGMRWPjBt3uQfKKveTqw4ry7/UrLa6ZuuaaoOq_j6iz4fcoSR_hU97O60/20)

body:

Before we finalize our Senate strategy, **I need to hear from you.**

We're at a critical moment. The decisions we make in the coming months will determine what Republicans can deliver for the American people. **That's why I put together a strategy poll for our most committed supporters.** I want real input from the people who are actually in this fight with us.

President Donald Trump **[gave a chilling message to reporters](https://substack.com/redirect/76675ca5-e20b-4afd-8849-de42a47556ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** at the White House late last night after an attempted shooter rushed into the hotel hosting the White House Correspondents' Dinner he was attending with many members of the administration.
_" I can't imagine that there is any profession that is more dangerous,... I accept the risks..."_
## [Watch Trump's message here: ](https://substack.com/redirect/76675ca5-e20b-4afd-8849-de42a47556ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# **White House Correspondents ' Dinner gunman targeted "administration officials," told police he wanted to breach ballroom**
Cole Tomas Allen, 31, **[told police he was targeting"administration officials"](https://substack.com/redirect/37f34268-cff1-45fa-b5a9-bbbf064a3c72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** at the White House Correspondents' Dinner Saturday night but wasn't after anyone in particular, with one law enforcement source saying "He was trying to just breach his way in and take whoever he could." The CalTech-educated teacher from Torrance, Calif., rushed a security checkpoint armed with a shotgun, handgun and multiple knives around 8:30 p.m.--about 20 minutes after Trump and first lady Melania Trump made their grand entrance--attempting to barge into the ballroom of approximately 2,500 people including the first couple, VP JD Vance, Trump cabinet members and journalists.
[| | | | | California Post
---
@californiapost
Today's cover: Trump rushed from DC press gala as shooter taken down. trib.al/7rvODaZ Subscribe for home delivery: trib.al/Cc0qHFu
| 12:51 PM * Apr 26, 2026 * 10.1K Views22 Replies * 10 Reposts * 34 Likes
---](https://substack.com/redirect/0bed2dfd-75e9-4019-bb9a-1bfcab40f7c7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Secret Service agent shot, suspect subdued in hotel lobby** : Allen appeared to gain entry by paying for a hotel room and smuggling weapons inside, with a volunteer saying they saw him assemble a long weapon "in [an] unsecured room" before making a mad dash toward the ballroom. A Secret Service agent was shot in the vest during the gunfight and rushed to a hospital in "great spirits," while Secret Service agents whisked away Trump and the first lady as guests took cover under tables.
* **Suspect took cross-country train from LA to DC** : Acting AG Todd Blanche said Allen took a cross-country train originating in Los Angeles, traveling through Chicago before ending up in DC--possibly to "sidestep tracking methods and security procedures" or because "he had those firearms with him." Allen has been charged with using a firearm during a crime of violence and assault of a federal officer. Trump said he didn't believe the attack was related to the Iran war and assured the dinner will be rescheduled within 30 days.

# **Who is the WHCD shooter: What we know so far**
Cole Allen, 31, a computer scientist from Torrance, California, went from designing first-person shooter games **[to becoming an alleged shooter himself](https://substack.com/redirect/321954fd-3fb5-4580-b908-0fb1e3117e49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** at the White House Correspondents' Dinner Saturday night.
[| | | | | The Telegraph
---
@Telegraph
The suspect in the Washington shooting is a "teacher of the month" who donated to Kamala Harris's presidential campaign. Cole Tomas Allen is accused of rushing a security checkpoint before opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner on Saturday night.
| 10:15 AM * Apr 26, 2026 * 36K Views95 Replies * 227 Reposts * 463 Likes
---](https://substack.com/redirect/d983dba4-a017-44fa-934c-be1ed9a62adf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **According to his LinkedIn profile,** Allen graduated from CalTech in 2017 with a BS in mechanical engineering, worked as a NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory summer research fellow in 2014, and created "First Law," a physics-based role-playing shooter game, plus another complex 2-D physics game called "Bohrdom" released on Steam.
* **Recent career and political activity** : Allen joined C2 Education tutoring company in March 2020 and earned an MS in computer science from California State University, Dominguez Hills in May 2025, with a December 2024 Facebook post naming him "teacher of the month." Federal Election Commission records show Allen donated $25 to Kamala Harris during the 2024 election cycle.
* **What happened** : Allen has been charged with two counts of using a firearm during a crime of violence and assault on a federal officer using a dangerous weapon, with more charges expected. Trump called Allen a "lone wolf" and "very sick person" after being whisked away by Secret Service.

clean_body: "I'm here to do a job... I accept the risks" | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? "I'm here to do a job... I accept the risks" | | | Apr 26 | | | | | | | | | | | | President Donald Trump at the White House late last night after an attempted shooter rushed into the hotel hosting the White House Correspondents' Dinner he was attending with many members of the administration. " I can't imagine that there is any profession that is more dangerous,... I accept the risks..." How do you stop the mainstream media? | | ---|---|--- Become a PolitiBrawl paid subscriber today and help us take back our nation's voice: White House Correspondents ' Dinner gunman targeted "administration officials," told police he wanted to breach ballroom Cole Tomas Allen, 31, at the White House Correspondents' Dinner Saturday night but wasn't after anyone in particular, with one law enforcement source saying "He was trying to just breach his way in and take whoever he could." The CalTech-educated teacher from Torrance, Calif., rushed a security checkpoint armed with a shotgun, handgun and multiple knives around 8:30 p.m.--about 20 minutes after Trump and first lady Melania Trump made their grand entrance--attempting to barge into the ballroom of approximately 2,500 people including the first couple, VP JD Vance, Trump cabinet members and journalists. Secret Service agent shot, suspect subdued in hotel lobby : Allen appeared to gain entry by paying for a hotel room and smuggling weapons inside, with a volunteer saying they saw him assemble a long weapon "in [an] unsecured room" before making a mad dash toward the ballroom. A Secret Service agent was shot in the vest during the gunfight and rushed to a hospital in "great spirits," while Secret Service agents whisked away Trump and the first lady as guests took cover under tables. Suspect took cross-country train from LA to DC : Acting AG Todd Blanche said Allen took a cross-country train originating in Los Angeles, traveling through Chicago before ending up in DC--possibly to "sidestep tracking methods and security procedures" or because "he had those firearms with him." Allen has been charged with using a firearm during a crime of violence and assault of a federal officer. Trump said he didn't believe the attack was related to the Iran war and assured the dinner will be rescheduled within 30 days. | | ---|---|--- No, it's not Nvidia... It's Mode Mobile , 2023's fastest-growing software company according to Deloitte. Their EarnPhone has helped users earn and save over $1B, driving $115M+ in revenue and . And having secured partnerships with Walmart and Best Buy , Mode's not stopping there... Like Uber turned vehicles into income-generating assets, Mode is turning smartphones into . The difference is, investors like you still have a chance to invest in Mode's pre-IPO offering at $0.50/share. They've just been granted the stock ticker $MODE by the Nasdaq and over 59,000 investors participated in their previous rounds. $71M+ already invested -- and earn up to a 20% bonus. Disclaimers Please read the and related risks at. This is a paid advertisement for Mode Mobile's Regulation A+ Offering. Mode Mobile recently received their ticker reservation with Nasdaq ($MODE), indicating an intent to IPO in the next 24 months. An intent to IPO is no guarantee that an actual IPO will occur. The Deloitte rankings are based on submitted applications and public company database research, with winners selected based on their fiscal-year revenue growth percentage over a three-year period. Pro forma revenue and EBITDA, includes full year numbers of the businesses acquired throughout 2025. Who is the WHCD shooter: What we know so far Cole Allen, 31, a computer scientist from Torrance, California, went from designing first-person shooter games at the White House Correspondents' Dinner Saturday night. According to his LinkedIn profile, Allen graduated from CalTech in 2017 with a BS in mechanical engineering, worked as a NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory summer research fellow in 2014, and created "First Law," a physics-based role-playing shooter game, plus another complex 2-D physics game called "Bohrdom" released on Steam. Recent career and political activity : Allen joined C2 Education tutoring company in March 2020 and earned an MS in computer science from California State University, Dominguez Hills in May 2025, with a December 2024 Facebook post naming him "teacher of the month." Federal Election Commission records show Allen donated $25 to Kamala Harris during the 2024 election cycle. What happened : Allen has been charged with two counts of using a firearm during a crime of violence and assault on a federal officer using a dangerous weapon, with more charges expected. Trump called Allen a "lone wolf" and "very sick person" after being whisked away by Secret Service. Kari Lake voices alarming security concerns: * You're currently a free subscriber to .
