| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

| Peter, I don’t say this lightly: this Supreme Court race is our best chance to fight back against the attacks on our rights here in Georgia. Everything from voting access to personal freedoms could be shaped by this court for a generation. Republicans want justices who answer to powerful interests. We’re fighting for Miracle Rankin and Jen Jordan — justices who will stand for the rule of law with no fear or favor for the powerful. This isn’t just about winning seats — **it’s about safeguarding our democracy and our constitutional rights.** With early voting starting tomorrow, every dollar we raise tonight goes straight into reaching voters at the most critical moment. So I’m asking you personally: will you stand with us in these final hours? Peter, will you chip in $10 before midnight to help us hit our $50,000 goal and make sure we have the resources to fight for a fair future for Georgians? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dpg-em-fr-apr26?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130208978/616987259/-1692272226?&amount=10&source=DPG_EM_FR_042626_B1_EARLYVOTING3_D1_S3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YTg2ZWM1NC04ZDQxLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=haw3GCyr3BE6Db5Wnxzow2AGjNE-MYHKEg3_47Fm8cw=) Thank you for being in this fight with us, Charlie Bailey

name: Evan Hunt
subject: Trump lied about how badly his illegal war damaged our troops.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elect Evan Hunt
body:
Keith Self keeps on celebrating the chaos. | | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYE5U9NTRbOMLNsSMiwuHsSrn4JnyYtk1EREaCYHldIw2Es-lCsKe-k7RYeRErCjUmpL6YWg0aqprxBCSlqaUKjL6uf8EDq6vQVEaFZEFWaTvOKtPnFc2PEalY2SnM4_7ciriLeMNhvR5pQ-N6xP3XVSySIJVWwB0vU4kn3_9T85cyblJzdW-B6cAlBGdGp8HughdKPF_8MEhbX53QabwRFHyxxNfygMWhotLfJ4SnvHQdroVUennIaxHSQUgQI-OQ/4q4/xOMIpQV5RGaeFJa0bT1O-w/h0/h001.H4NnEPcMc2q5NKTC-etEMXYVTCU89tOsSlmmO7-w-3M)
---
Peter, NBC News broke the shocking news this weekend that Iranian strikes on American military bases across the Middle East were far more extensive and costly than this administration has publicly acknowledged. We are talking about more than 100 targets hit across 11 bases in seven countries with a price tag in billions for repairs - damage so extensive that some facilities have to be decommissioned altogether and we'll have to start from scratch. The Navy's 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, the nerve center of American naval operations across the entire region, sustained serious damage. A runway at Al-Udeid Air Base in Qatar, where I served for more than a year running combat missions over Afghanistan, was destroyed. An Iranian F-5 fighter jet penetrated air defenses and bombed Camp Buehring in Kuwait, the first time an enemy fixed-wing aircraft has struck an American military base in years. Other critical command infrastructure in bases across Kuwait, including the port strike that killed six soldiers and wounded dozens, were also hit in the first wave of retaliatory strikes from Iran. **As a career Air Force officer I want to be perfectly clear:** Withholding specific operational details after an incident for legitimate national security reasons is sometimes necessary and appropriate. **Lying about the scale of what happened to make a president look tougher than the reality warrants is something else entirely, and it puts our troops in danger.** [**Help us flip this seat and send a combat veteran to Congress who will demand the truth about the cost of this war before more families pay the price for decisions made in the dark.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqs6gc2C5hUDUAX_oNaSPVDobFeqfVfzIW2x4J26hzyt85ro4mRrYa08e8RVnJG-BGkYMbeSVpL9iHK3sxWhbnhnf7LY7TEy7kQpKODugL2Ng8tTHA-_dpdnzIsOl5dGqfMEwbrqet0ZBjh-Q0ZIfq8PidP1u_s4c3Z2htmu_yibt8QdyHi-07pQIgtVZqeOTh73rx4W_rujKfWu8f8FSDgxWu8VBfm0J9nVWpJmVWkipRTFPst-tedd4jOY7Aj4yfX6eJpjSV2K6jtYsvuDZYXYKs2J06cHYP8zT2C1dClrQRADswP89t4XViYTQ79639Na2B-wTVgA6WRd3vrPoNKc3eXeTFWVgCO4hqcCS4HklQ/4q4/xOMIpQV5RGaeFJa0bT1O-w/h1/h001.u21m_4ps241cb9Qv0MhHp3p_RwH0-JPp6LJCfohbYxo) I think about the families connected to these bases. The spouse who uprooted their life and moved their kids across the world to be near their partner, trusting that the people in charge were being straight about the risks. The service member who returned to a base where the hangar is rubble while the administration that put their lives at risk is still insisting publicly that everything went fine. **Military families absorb an enormous amount on behalf of this country. ** **The absolute least they are owed in return is the truth about what happened and why.** This administration has given them spin instead. A Pentagon more focused on protecting Trump's image than on leveling with the people bearing the real cost of this war. Keith Self has sat on the House Foreign Affairs Committee through every cover-up and defended every one of them. **At his isolationist, Islamophobic core, any American bombs in the Arab world are acceptable to him, even when it means lying to the military families paying the price for every one of them.** [**Help us make November the moment that bigotry and moral failing finally catches up with him.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqs6gc2C5hUDUAX_oNaSPVDobFeqfVfzIW2x4J26hzyt85ro4mRrYa08e8RVnJG-BGkYMbeSVpL9iHK3sxWhbnhnf7LY7TEy7kQpKODugL2Ng0qe9iEcJ5c23TRlNpHXWXYMa4I8ac5vuyOVo4HENjj21j-Opqvaip9lfZXdaA6CHgx_b7z7SAvV9N88uGVk5aJPmFlRQD2Vdxt0eNzYyrYFulJcHn89ouVCIYdPTzb0h3714dYC_sQqWFqHAWd1wOopn8J3pOVvi3KhGdQjuIvQmRZFEgNi5vZts7GZttr0yTz3xOCH0T3qAFt2Q7EvZIABX1Rh6Fqv7vGOLQD3sqOoyk4io-cblF24swouJMV2DA/4q4/xOMIpQV5RGaeFJa0bT1O-w/h2/h001.52lzPlEwXhjXwCiDsKMrIivqkXyS8LOkcf4kLd7318w) Very respectfully, Evan Hunt Democratic Nominee TX-03

clean_body: Keith Self keeps on celebrating the chaos. | | Peter, NBC News broke the shocking news this weekend that Iranian strikes on American military bases across the Middle East were far more extensive and costly than this administration has publicly acknowledged. We are talking about more than 100 targets hit across 11 bases in seven countries with a price tag in billions for repairs - damage so extensive that some facilities have to be decommissioned altogether and we'll have to start from scratch. The Navy's 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, the nerve center of American naval operations across the entire region, sustained serious damage. A runway at Al-Udeid Air Base in Qatar, where I served for more than a year running combat missions over Afghanistan, was destroyed. An Iranian F-5 fighter jet penetrated air defenses and bombed Camp Buehring in Kuwait, the first time an enemy fixed-wing aircraft has struck an American military base in years. Other critical command infrastructure in bases across Kuwait, including the port strike that killed six soldiers and wounded dozens, were also hit in the first wave of retaliatory strikes from Iran. As a career Air Force officer I want to be perfectly clear: Withholding specific operational details after an incident for legitimate national security reasons is sometimes necessary and appropriate. Lying about the scale of what happened to make a president look tougher than the reality warrants is something else entirely, and it puts our troops in danger. I think about the families connected to these bases. The spouse who uprooted their life and moved their kids across the world to be near their partner, trusting that the people in charge were being straight about the risks. The service member who returned to a base where the hangar is rubble while the administration that put their lives at risk is still insisting publicly that everything went fine. Military families absorb an enormous amount on behalf of this country. The absolute least they are owed in return is the truth about what happened and why. This administration has given them spin instead. A Pentagon more focused on protecting Trump's image than on leveling with the people bearing the real cost of this war. Keith Self has sat on the House Foreign Affairs Committee through every cover-up and defended every one of them. At his isolationist, Islamophobic core, any American bombs in the Arab world are acceptable to him, even when it means lying to the military families paying the price for every one of them. Very respectfully, Evan Hunt Democratic Nominee TX-03 | | | Evan Hunt is a combat aviator and veteran, a community and business leader, and a Texas family man, running against MAGA Rep. Keith Self in TX-03. We need all hands on deck to flip this seat. clean_body: This is Vice President JD Vance. I'm going to be completely direct with you: The Radical Left has one goal in mind. Take back the House majority and kill our America First movement. They will lie, cheat, and steal their way back to a House Majority. They know the math. They know that without patriots like Guy Reschenthaler, the House falls. Guy is the single most reliable ally we have in Congress. He's a warrior who doesn't flinch when the swamp comes calling. President Trump has endorsed him twice for a reason.I want to make sure you understand what we're dealing with this year: If we lose the House, we lose President Trump.The moment the Left takes the gavel, they won't be focused on helping your family or fixing the border. They will immediately launch a sham impeachment to paralyze the President's agenda and overturn the mandate of the American people. They want to tie his hands so he can never finish the work we started and fought so hard for.Guy and I both know the meaning of service. I served in the Marines, and Guy served in the Navy. We don't shy away from a fight, but you can't win a war without ammo. The Left is trying to bleed Guy's campaign dry because they know he is the line of defense between them and the President. that line breaks, everything we've built, the America First agenda, the border security, the economic comeback, it's all over in the blink of an eye.I refuse to let a pack of RINOs and radicals impeach our President out of spite. This is why I am coming to you, Peter. Will you stand by and let that happen? I didn't think so. not let them destroy our future. Stand with the President's biggest ally in Congress. name: LeVar Burton
subject: From here...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mandela for Wisconsin
body:
This is a critical moment
| [](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-apr26-mmfr.FkyFOx&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_042626_B1_LevarBurtonResend&t=2&akid=6092%2E196590%2EfT5-32) Hi, LeVar Burton here. You might know me from Roots, Reading Rainbow, or Star Trek: The Next Generation. **Today, I’m more than proud to share that I'm endorsing my good friend Mandela Barnes to be the next governor of Wisconsin.** This is a critical moment in our country – no one can deny that. Mandela’s bold leadership is exactly what we need to tackle today’s problems with creative, innovative solutions. **[Will you chip in to Mandela’s campaign today?](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-apr26-mmfr.FkyFOx&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_042626_B1_LevarBurtonResend&t=3&akid=6092%2E196590%2EfT5-32)** I come from a family of educators, and it has been my life’s mission to make sure that future generations have the skills to learn, grow, and be civically engaged. Education and the arts have been under constant attack by this current administration. And after hosting Reading Rainbow for more than 20 years on PBS, I was devastated to see federal funding for public broadcasting completely decimated. I believe that protecting services like these is vital. Hearing each other’s stories brings us together, fosters connection, and keeps us engaged with our communities. I know that as governor, Mandela is going to do just that. You don’t have to take my word for it — Mandela has an important story to tell. I look forward to seeing him on the campaign trail as he shares his vision for a Wisconsin where everyone can live up to their fullest potential. **[I hope you’ll join me in supporting Mandela with a contribution of $15 or $27.](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-apr26-mmfr.FkyFOx&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_042626_B1_LevarBurtonResend&t=4&akid=6092%2E196590%2EfT5-32)** _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now._ | | | [DONATE $15 »](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1271?c0=mb-em-fr-apr26-mmfr.eYM9Ce&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_042626_B1_LevarBurtonResend&t=5&akid=6092%2E196590%2EfT5-32)

name: Dax Alexander
subject: 30 days. 10,145 square miles. We need your help.
body:
| |
---
We have a once-in-a-lifetime shot to flip this district, but only if we turn out the vote. | [View in browser](https://gxwexbq.clicks.mlsend.com/tj/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjE2NjExMzIsXCJsXCI6MTg1ODMxMjY2NjkyNjI5ODM0LFwiclwiOjE4NTgzMTI3ODY4OTM4NzY2M30iLCJzIjoiOTJjYTdlZTllMmViOTM2OCJ9)

# We need your help.
|
---
Hey, it’s Dax. I know you’re tired of fundraising emails (believe me, so am I), but I ask that you read until the end. We have exactly **30 day** s until the primary run off election. Our district spans about **10,145 square miles** , and the key to winning a run off election is turnout. We need every single democratic voter to
1. Know there’s a primary run off election, and
2. Go vote on **May 26th.**

To do this, we need your help.
* $25 gets signs into 4 yards.
* $61 reaches 100 voters in the district.
* $115 covers literature for 500 doors.

As a token of our appreciation, if you donate $100 or more before midnight tonight, we’ll send you a Dax for Congress t-shirt (they are SO soft, you’ll love it).

With your generosity, we have a once in a lifetime shot at flipping this district in November. We can’t do it without you.
With gratitude and hope, Dax

|

name: Gina Hinojosa
subject: Serious business
body:
We’ve only got six of these deadlines left.
# [](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)[](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)
This deadline is serious business, Peter.
In just a few days, we’re closing the books on our April fundraising — and we’ve still got some major ground to make up before the deadline hits on April 30.
**[If you’re already with me, can you rush in your $5 donation right now?](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260426_1_goalintro.18ZO9j&refcode=em20260426-989&amount=5&t=1&akid=989%2E299639%2EioB9zw)**
[****](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260426_1_goalintro.18ZO9j&refcode=em20260426-989&amount=5&t=2&akid=989%2E299639%2EioB9zw)
Why does this monthly deadline matter so much?
**After April, we’ll only have _six more monthly deadlines_ before Election Day.**
It may feel like we’ve got plenty of time, but this election is right around the corner. We can’t afford to slow down for even a moment if we’re going to win this race.
**This $100,000 goal is ambitious, but I know this team can make it happen.**
[**Make a $5 contribution now to support my campaign to take back Texas.**](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260426_1_goalintro.18ZO9j&refcode=em20260426-989&amount=5&t=3&akid=989%2E299639%2EioB9zw)

Thank you,
Gina

name: Team Katie Porter
subject: This is urgent
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=41221%2E3422442%2E6glPRO)
Peter,
We’re just days away from the end of the month, and we need grassroots supporters to step up now to help us reach our **$100,000 goal.**
This is the make-or-break stretch for our campaign. With about five weeks left until the June primary—and only two candidates advancing to November’s ballot—what we do right now will shape the rest of this race.
**[Can you rush a donation to Katie’s campaign right now to help us reach our $100,000 goal ahead of the end of the month?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260426_1_ak._JkHM4&refcode=em20260426-41221&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=41221%2E3422442%2E6glPRO)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260426_1_ak._JkHM4&refcode=em20260426-41221&t=4&akid=41221%2E3422442%2E6glPRO)
This past Wednesday, Katie took the debate stage and said what a lot of Californians are living: rents that are out of control, child care costs that don’t add up, and everyday prices squeezing working families at every turn.
She talked about her plans for free child care, tuition-free college degrees, less expensive housing, and eliminating state income tax for families making under $100,000.
The other people on stage—a self-funded billionaire, Big Oil–backed candidates, and Republicans eager to push Trump’s agenda in California—don’t understand what California families are struggling with.
Katie doesn’t take corporate PAC money and she doesn’t self-fund. She counts on people-powered support from folks like you. That’s what keeps this campaign running, Peter.
Hitting our $100,000 goal means we can keep organizing, keep reaching voters, and stay competitive in these final weeks before the primary. Falling short means slowing down at the exact moment we need to be accelerating.
**[So if you can, chip in $5 or whatever makes sense to you to help us reach our $100,000 goal before April ends and power this campaign through the finish line.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260426_1_ak._JkHM4&refcode=em20260426-41221&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=5&akid=41221%2E3422442%2E6glPRO)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260426_1_ak._JkHM4&refcode=em20260426-41221&t=24&akid=41221%2E3422442%2E6glPRO)
Thank you.
— Team Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20260426-41221&amounts=&t=25&refcode2=41221_3422442_6glPRO&akid=41221%2E3422442%2E6glPRO)**

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026
PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)

body:
My parents fled Laos with nothing | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquO38L98SklfBdoDq94y0bmIPp1sMsxRlzUR9IaKq2v2m5fFVkRWIVkpk_t-aXvxyDGzqEL57tK52TxMZKIf-Ke349gbheXU_Mq2IuHUJN8OWCXwgWB_1eeDYYpo2C4FLXkpCIOrCOaZzyX7IozElEkSzA2W2zlw0BSsS8gMWK3WMCMYsnO_IKu1FOEfOf1kfMW_uIHFqB59d6jYPrecKeXcCE5g53F-E8QnHv8IFRFjgroxw5pqIg86Qs-iDSMWm01oD9f_Q-r1mx5CDpGeSA49wYrzryOE0MmEGUG47T1HScHLfkE5QVMi91f_4Mbbek/4q4/xxRU1ZelSFe2BUKwqU0meg/h0/h001.szWGth__h4788hAIkT_WuaZD9w8erLWUNmO6AKSDIY8)

There's been a lot happening in this race — but today I want to slow down and share a little more about who I am and where I come from.

I am the proud daughter of Hmong refugees. My family came to this country seeking a better life — and like so many immigrant and refugee families, they have been targeted by this administration.

This fight is incredibly personal to me.

For those who may not know, the Hmong people were recruited by the CIA to fight in the Secret War of Laos during the Vietnam War. It was a war fought in the shadows, one that many Americans never knew about. What followed for our people was devastating — some would call it a complete genocide.

My parents fled Laos with nothing, and came to this country to build a better life from the ground up.

I share this because we are now living in some really dangerous and troubling times. The families I grew up with — Hmong families, immigrant families, refugee families — are under attack. I refuse to stay on the sidelines.

**I will never take money from ICE contractors or the corporations enabling the authoritarian attack on the immigrant community. My opponent Doris Matsui has. That is a fundamental difference in this race — and a big reason why I’m running.**

I have fought hard, and I will keep fighting, with every ounce of who I am — for working families, for our communities, and for the values that brought my parents here in the first place.

That is my commitment to you.

**[And if you're able to chip in anything today — $5, $25, whatever you can — it helps us keep this fight going.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquO38L98SklfBdoDq94y0bmIPp1sMsxRlzUR9IaKq2v2m5fFVkRWIVkpk_t-aXvxyDGzqEL57tK52TxMZKIf-Ke349gbheXU_Mq2IuHUJN8OcgVyjLW4HRNQ6tSNv8yFv3_RqSuqJPdUPtlRrPR1QUOSOlKzJtpf2m7u8o8SYwt6VRjis9gpgkTRfcHaEk5zd2cWHwFpPdqYxAwKBSK_Z35vxVFcX_k4thuZE8UeGvT3X0TN4UcRViefNc6JxlO0aSOa3ixu6sJTv5kNHIvw57_ohXXd77eVtuF_DSMgeDBsurX2tlUE3Yek2pcFgSP6YA/4q4/xxRU1ZelSFe2BUKwqU0meg/h1/h001.OoWUEToKIz4S0_yGkdPkbonDXXbtGdadDHIe7O0MP9w)**

Mai is the daughter of Hmong refugees, born and raised in Sacramento. Mai’s parents fled war in Laos and arrived in California to find a new life.

As the oldest of 16 children, she helped raise her siblings while working hard to build a career dedicated to helping her community thrive. Mai is a proud product of public schools and was the first in her family to attend college. As a young organizer, Mai returned home to serve the community that raised and supported her.

name: Aria, United We Dream
subject: The fight to keep families together
body:
We will not stop pressuring lawmakers to end these cruel attacks on the immigrants who power our economy and country. | | | Peter,
Across the country, everyday people are meeting authoritarian threats with powerful solidarity. As masked federal agents continue to target our communities, neighbors are bravely stepping up to protect each other. _Still, our friends and neighbors are being arrested without due process, and families are being torn apart. Children are being locked up. And it’s putting their health at risk, even leading to potentially deadly consequences._ A recent report1 from _The New Yorker_ talks about the dangerous medical neglect that detainees face. **Our immigrant coworkers, friends, and classmates are continuing to be detained and cruelly separated from their families every day. And the detrimental consequences are compounded by the health risks that come with it.** The failure to uphold legal protections for DACA recipients and statutory limits on detaining and separating children is harming immigrant families and demands an urgent response. **** **[We need your help fueling our advocacy efforts to pressure lawmakers to end these cruel attacks. Can you make a donation to United We Dream today? clean_body: We will not stop pressuring lawmakers to end these cruel attacks on the immigrants who power our economy and country. | | | Peter, Across the country, everyday people are meeting authoritarian threats with powerful solidarity. As masked federal agents continue to target our communities, neighbors are bravely stepping up to protect each other. Still, our friends and neighbors are being arrested without due process, and families are being torn apart. Children are being locked up. And it’s putting their health at risk, even leading to potentially deadly consequences. A recent report1 from The New Yorker talks about the dangerous medical neglect that detainees face. Our immigrant coworkers, friends, and classmates are continuing to be detained and cruelly separated from their families every day. And the detrimental consequences are compounded by the health risks that come with it. The failure to uphold legal protections for DACA recipients and statutory limits on detaining and separating children is harming immigrant families and demands an urgent response. ** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | In this moment of deeply alarming attacks on our immigrant neighbors and loved ones, you have shown up for our communities, Peter. Thank you for stepping up and joining us at such a crucial time. We’re here, we’re organizing, and we’re facing this moment together. — Aria, United We Dream 1. “The Return of Family Detention.” The New Yorker. April 13, 2026. Follow us on: | | | | |

name: JD Vance
subject: This has me concerned
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Moody for Florida
body:
| | | | Fellow Patriot, **Time is short, so I’ll be blunt: If we lose this year’s U.S. Senate special election in Florida, I believe that we will lose the Senate majority.** Fortunately, we have a stellar candidate defending this seat in Ashley Moody. I had the distinct honor of personally swearing her in to replace Marco Rubio, and I’ve seen her work side-by-side with me and President Trump to deliver on his America First mandate. _**But we’ve gotten a serious wake-up call that this race is anything but decided. Democrats are racking up special election victories in Florida, and this U.S. Senate race is their next target.**_ They’re circling like sharks, and they smell blood in the water. I believe in Ashley. I believe that we can rise to the occasion and make history by helping us keep the Senate. [**But belief isn’t enough – we need support from patriots like you. Are you in?**](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=40e48e9648af40bebf0240e057d8b40e&_e=a-hHq6Zqs_Y1IOaQZHQxvUA8RfVxb2yj7kzfUZqdC15G0v0YYYSxPld0-1mO0WawJkhwut3-BzLieskLUYhiu5-hrXWhr1S2fGZegWbLnyw-OpS-Gvgyk-pY1vBzWbxraQZ5pabPm4nAei5fpaP_O9QvUJVSJFMfUHx5i518BAp_bdStRwvVVy3QSWYNkD9hvX2Rqqc7hRCO2XRFhNk7pNNeCpgmWNjmRMWrCym14sVGcpb045ZXJkseP46zwO9GRMsBLBR9o89bvO_H471_JWRNejcHvphjZtq8e9DnaMxj5FfbGy4ktlohbP-7OYhXSk1ZAtmDYK7EB9T2j_ysrNkaS0pBsGKe6iuc5KJX9heMed1Ph9A6rRnF3XUqvqGCU8LXKLw5JIeJ4HQMvdfM8c1OjS0WQ0bTZhyFWdAjZWSzS4HX6GetEbSTzotmNMwWqC4JY_pS9VSW0uhWsNayQ8oCD_xFRz13CwuCC-PjTTG-pxeLf_gEOhpXQ4-57I5Xw5H8FztVm0n0FaW5q_G0dZO1l18liYnDShhBZw_vDOVEO9nF3iDN27rVXcy1mzOyiCd4EzTMwzt0pT_oUuNvOOgRA9USULEWXjbwdjAV7iSQVnMnsSNC3bvIAEmt3uiYYNp1Lf-Mhu7D1Ds8zN29KANRVQIMgTj7QesCL5lUL60%3D)

clean_body: | | | | Fellow Patriot, Time is short, so I’ll be blunt: If we lose this year’s U.S. Senate special election in Florida, I believe that we will lose the Senate majority. Fortunately, we have a stellar candidate defending this seat in Ashley Moody. I had the distinct honor of personally swearing her in to replace Marco Rubio, and I’ve seen her work side-by-side with me and President Trump to deliver on his America First mandate. But we’ve gotten a serious wake-up call that this race is anything but decided. Democrats are racking up special election victories in Florida, and this U.S. Senate race is their next target. They’re circling like sharks, and they smell blood in the water. I believe in Ashley. I believe that we can rise to the occasion and make history by helping us keep the Senate. What we do now could very well decide the future of our nation. Thank you for standing with me, and for standing with Senator Moody. Sincerely, JD Vance Vice President of the United States | | Paid for by Moody for Florida Moody for Florida 301 West Platt Street #663 Tampa, FL 33606

name: Disgusting
subject: MAGA bill forces homeless people into unpaid labor
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jamie Davis for U
body:
The Bible says what you do to the least among us, you do to Him. MAGA just voted to force them into unpaid labor.
| | | | | [](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TqPpDPy2QD-0NHyxdoXyZXr17OA6oR57pe_Gnouc5ev3Ob0CXYHQY49zL7epIpjozjiPRMX2pJc6Oqf2XU4O7_AsURD3yWFz6TKPd1ZqWMR42tJRNsVURNehLg0lQk_iBag8ZrVVJi_e-SLiRxkKvJ8bC9zzlMCA6mMqy6-KR9PJlA5PgeXUNpErwXKCAH47n8_IP6sDGA5eT6ptI0t8b7futFey4yyR&c=C7km96x-k83bO1D7NF7D-AjAMGNIGU1z8FGr9vKWmAbiKq39VVAORw==&ch=y8vamly6sO_o4MXzskXKcqJjDHRzbzL3Y1s_xbKmVlaGTFRoJ8Ostg==)
---
Peter, Louisiana Republicans just advanced one of the cruelest anti-homeless bills in the country.
Under this bill, homeless people would be forced to choose between jail and involuntary treatment. They'd be billed for that treatment. And if they can't pay, they'd be forced to perform unpaid labor to work it off.
---
| [](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TqPpDPy2QD-0NHyxdoXyZXr17OA6oR57pe_Gnouc5ev3Ob0CXYHQY49zL7epIpjozjiPRMX2pJc6Oqf2XU4O7_AsURD3yWFz6TKPd1ZqWMR42tJRNsVURNehLg0lQk_iBag8ZrVVJi_e-SLiRxkKvJ8bC9zzlMCA6mMqy6-KR9PJlA5PgeXUNpErwXKCAH47n8_IP6sDGA69legTx-jawoblDbGtu7Do&c=C7km96x-k83bO1D7NF7D-AjAMGNIGU1z8FGr9vKWmAbiKq39VVAORw==&ch=y8vamly6sO_o4MXzskXKcqJjDHRzbzL3Y1s_xbKmVlaGTFRoJ8Ostg==)
---
I am a man of Christian faith. And I have to be honest with you: this is disgusting.
This is not what Jesus stands for. This is not what any faith that preaches love, dignity, and compassion for the least among us stands for.
MAGA has weaponized our faith. They wave the cross while punishing the poor, criminalizing suffering, and treating our most vulnerable neighbors as inconveniences to be disposed of. That perversion of Christianity is why their support is crumbling. Americans of faith are waking up.
That's why I'm running for U.S. Senate in Louisiana.
I'm running to take a seat from MAGA, help Democrats flip the Senate, and stop the worst of what they're doing to our country and our values. Louisiana deserves a senator who actually lives the faith they claim.

name: Justice Democrats
subject: Dr. Adam Hamawy and Hasan Piker
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Justice Democrats
body:
Dr. Adam is the clear progressive choice in the crowded NJ-12 primary.
#
Peter,
**Dr. Adam Hamawy sat down with Twitch streamer and left-wing activist Hasan Piker** last week to talk about his Democratic primary race in NJ-12, his experience saving U.S. Senator Tammy Duckworth’s life as a U.S. Army surgeon in Iraq, and why he supports a full arms embargo and sanctions on Israel.
[](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260426-ah.onVekI&refcode=em20260426-37711&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=37711%2E1203867%2ECHWT3c)
When Hasan hit him with a rapid-fire round of questions, it couldn’t have been easier. Here’s what Dr. Adam said when asked: “Do you support...”
Medicare for all? _Yes._
Banning congressional stock trading? _Yes._
Abolishing and prosecuting ICE? _Yes._
Taxing billionaires? _Yes._
Cancelling student loan debt? _Yes._
Cancelling medical debt? _Yes._
Universal childcare? _Yes._
Implementing the Green New Deal? _Yes._
Ending aid to Israel? _Yes._
Ending America’s war on Iran? _Yes._
Ending America’s blockade on Cuba? _Yes._
It’s really that simple.
**Dr. Adam is the clear progressive choice in this open primary, which is why corporate super PACs and the Israel lobby will stop at nothing to defeat him.**
**[This race is ramping up, and we need all the support we can get to win in June. Pitch in $5 or more to fuel Dr. Adam’s campaign and Justice Democrats before Thursday’s end-of-month deadline!](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260426-ah.onVekI&refcode=em20260426-37711&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=3&akid=37711%2E1203867%2ECHWT3c)**
**[Contribute $5](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260426-ah.onVekI&refcode=em20260426-37711&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=22&akid=37711%2E1203867%2ECHWT3c)**
In solidarity,
Justice Democrats

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20260426-37711-footer&t=23&refcode2=37711_1203867_CHWT3c&akid=37711%2E1203867%2ECHWT3c)**

The math doesn't work anymore.
And working people didn't break it — **Republicans did.**
For years, Democrats have fought to raise the minimum wage. But as long as Republicans control the Senate, it goes nowhere. They'd rather protect _billionaire tax breaks_ than make sure someone working full-time can afford to keep the lights on.
That's not the America I grew up in.
Working people built this country. They deserve a Senate that fights for them — not one that works against them.
If we flip the Senate, we can pass a minimum wage that actually reflects what it costs to live. We can restore the basic promise that hard work pays off.
[**Will you chip in $10 or more to help us flip the Senate blue and fight for working Americans?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130211694/616994916/-499167411?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.04.26_My-First-Job_B1_F1_S1_C1_U1_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NjY2YTAxMS05MzQxLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ytijJd2S_UTEETEqSou3cGtngbpOfZNmMHHaC_0MBvg=)

name: New Hampshire Senate Update via Team Pappas
subject: This is a message for anyone who cares about taking back the Senate majority 🇺🇸
body:
[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1001&akid=6323%2E582294%2EcypWYv ]Chris Pappas for Senate[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1002&akid=6323%2E582294%2EcypWYv ]Chris Pappas for Senate

We wanted
to share a quick, candid update with you.

Grassroots fundraising has been slower than we expected this month. We
don’t want to panic or sound alarmist, but we do need to be clear about
what that means for our race.

National Republicans are already spending money to flip this Senate seat
red — and they’ve already committed to spend an additional $17 million. It
is clear they’re not holding back.

At the same time, taking back the Senate majority hinges on winning here
in New Hampshire.

The only way we win is by staying competitive every step of the way — and
we’ve seen our fundraising lose steam at a critical moment.

That’s why today we’re lowering our ask to just $1.
[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260426_1.qkVbMs&refcode=em20260426-1&t=1003&akid=6323%2E582294%2EcypWYv ]Will
you spare $1 to help us win in New Hampshire and flip the Senate majority
blue?

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260426_1.qkVbMs&amount=1&refcode=em20260426-1&t=1004&akid=6323%2E582294%2EcypWYv ]Donate $1 »

It may not seem like much, but even $1 can make an impact.

That’s because there are hundreds of thousands of people reading this
email, including you.

If even a fraction of folks chip in, we’ll get our fundraising back on
track.

It’s the collective effort of this grassroots team that makes all of this
possible. Together we can build the people-powered campaign it takes to
win.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260426_1.qkVbMs&refcode=em20260426-1&t=1005&akid=6323%2E582294%2EcypWYv ]So we’re asking one more time: Can we count on you to contribute $1 or
more today?

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260426_1.qkVbMs&amount=1&refcode=em20260426-1&t=1024&akid=6323%2E582294%2EcypWYv ]Donate $1 »

Chris’s campaign has always been powered by people like you — Granite
Staters and supporters across the country who believe in commonsense
leadership, protecting fundamental rights, and creating more opportunities
for working families.

If we’re going to win this race and take back the Senate majority, we have
to do it together.

Thank you for being part of this.

Team Pappas

clean_body: [ ]Chris Pappas for Senate[ ]Chris Pappas for Senate We wanted to share a quick, candid update with you. Grassroots fundraising has been slower than we expected this month. We don’t want to panic or sound alarmist, but we do need to be clear about what that means for our race. National Republicans are already spending money to flip this Senate seat red — and they’ve already committed to spend an additional $17 million. It is clear they’re not holding back. At the same time, taking back the Senate majority hinges on winning here in New Hampshire. The only way we win is by staying competitive every step of the way — and we’ve seen our fundraising lose steam at a critical moment. That’s why today we’re lowering our ask to just $1. [ ]Will you spare $1 to help us win in New Hampshire and flip the Senate majority blue? [ ]Donate $1 » It may not seem like much, but even $1 can make an impact. That’s because there are hundreds of thousands of people reading this email, including you. If even a fraction of folks chip in, we’ll get our fundraising back on track. It’s the collective effort of this grassroots team that makes all of this possible. Together we can build the people-powered campaign it takes to win. [ ]So we’re asking one more time: Can we count on you to contribute $1 or more today? [ ]Donate $1 » Chris’s campaign has always been powered by people like you — Granite Staters and supporters across the country who believe in commonsense leadership, protecting fundamental rights, and creating more opportunities for working families. If we’re going to win this race and take back the Senate majority, we have to do it together. Thank you for being part of this. Team Pappas You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:

clean_body: This isn’t a mass email. | Fellow American, Here's why: When we reviewed the latest batch of Voter Verification results from Operation Red Strike, we noticed your responses were And honestly? That surprised us. Based on everything we know about your engagement history, you're exactly the kind of patriot whose opinion be shaping our 2026 playbook. But right now, it isn't. | We've asked the data team to hold your file open for so you can go back in and finish. After that, if it is still incomplete, it gets archived - and your input won't be included in the final strategy report that goes to Chairman Hudson. | We're reaching out because Let's get this done. -ORS Data Team | | Paid for by Operation Red Strike, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Hudson for Congress and NRCC. 320 First St SE Washington, DC 20003 | This email is sent to %%Emailc%% by Operation Red Strike. By providing your cell phone or mobile phone number you are consenting to receive calls and texts, including autodialed and automated calls and texts, to that number with campaign notifications from Operation Red Strike. Operation Red Strike is happy to help at 301-244-9479. Reply HELP for help, STOP to end. MessFreqMayVary. Msg&DataRatesMayApply. . | Thank you for supporting our mission Friend! Emails like this are the best way for Operation Red Strike to break through the Mainstream Media and Liberal Lies. body:
Peter,
[I'm not going to sugarcoat it. We're in the fight of our lives here, and I need you to step up.](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/gttfHYNrOr_5e1iAZ19H5T_OpVTjSGvlE5IK5TYQhsv0coGzTAuGeFYq7jbD56LxdHSlMsF1ZVDusNZcuMBJLwJsIj4rUX_qy7fVHC-HCN76SN0t26zvC4hBWbH84ZE38CjUaFUE9tFFpFJ2mq8NNcFk3sED99w9CQw-Y5RP64acrA4UvSeNysRzr_TOVNWsVUmRix8WNmcYvhE9ECUV5pVi6CW7Whe8sS9kEaN93vZrK4AqHerxsuXXO4SymFaX74m51iGyGEjqxLklllEwKVypwIngr4vlzUvckBKGSd_uJ7ePOq_67KuYHYyhwrKzKu2uJzHOwdVvleogmmx84sojmm2r5hLtMMEbHyKUXWWdtJTGsxHoynTLQSayeVxfTDX63W7cuuB9azRRI_xAI0HemqArYinHGuopIiig1aQglxIo2fnFD_WNacFKCdmyEVmkgXNQQBZlbMJMe2_sWGqUuH9uc5DS_LtKkEvzYWQyaurY3z1uSxTrmG8FDUC4cy0envSWwBdC0AsO4-VWvmz-IF1bB0IEEQ4CIBhgEol0aQw9x47l-A85kdJBVdppd9Ki4USouI7EFNpS7rn0knhahhUYGYLA3yV4L8d6PY2ywRjhpFIEUsAMZ80i6J4mJQRzpXFFWVZA6NDFETW8y7UdnFB_m_dej5wnAv4CpssUJViFUlOAAC186iSx5ToZkt8klsHNUjoVw4b_OdYgWaAeHanXvhQbAJ6qW76EOVcjF9hNlV3MV62p2-eK_-p5j6zC0e5cfKXh1gu4oWakWDxvp5VKKAn-aYFdoxISDRUWcr09cMf0N1oW0SLOPTxF1dcgVJxOknJSuKh9A5yY30oKBkTTq5EBFh7D7F9HekbNx8DzFRgiUWrUy-kL/oF08xhiTazfy4Zkpgh39a3uYMdvDEw1H/20)
As a key member of our team, I'm counting on you to help us take back control of our country. The Democrats are going to stop at nothing to undermine our values and destroy our way of life, but I know we can beat them if we work together.
That's why I need you to take just [1 minute to fill out your Voter Verification Survey before it closes this evening.](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/D-biHDEyiSz1fiKysT4Vqiy_gGOk21I2Vi6g7wJqFlJ0YsyoLyx7AsQdZLVWD-i45DDp1hbb6oj7Jz1Xk-_YqIuTmKfgkRIDgeIhp3_W7UrCYNLHSgJxAQHavfgQTqUEAXGbGoMVfbU02-mtbbwM1tS-K4B7vIcWLn3kq85yQJ4uCvt5nZUIWPshfF41dlH5Dw-5mE30rKvhLqbwbfXMcPq5bErRnwtLsldhVK931w6fi3CNQ64QGdpeLAlCZbqRj70pURqqc1G4RMg-qTi33UXJwaqhMMVhueTd3VzsbVgswbMiy1XHZdfJX1Xhb4HEeqc8i94M8_1z1SkdMF3MUmdQhImxSPaTlsm7E1ecT4cdrjAWTgs69IJpcUBNb5ZXsjFgAqFitgGdSYZZM3c2JWQ7pg0CP0cYC-cluDX9KgFwjGQWPtLeQbBMK216Ljm-l6BnI2uh-1tg9tQAgRsnqvhpbZrkpCfpYgsfm7w7VmHbYt7MFdrsklzR9GZs8w_Bz22sAbpZi5N0BC5NIqYBsQs7FYwRLMXvi3qZHo95m_4hxS9UOgbwwxgZlTNRmeHy6Zp_BxtOkoVkLf1cbbXIJ2NwuNJJH8k3n0sim1jlYbrav6KrKjQbguh2S-I66j-oOsWqsSCbQBf0BiRuZ3sIibX9beDkWRDZ6-14eGsfxGzG8fxB2K1BAYJCsB7t3t8I2FSqDDq85hMi624P5S8-OU7Pa-EF9R-oN7LJxyLi0jGdRTw3-IFcpJ6eULQIOjh9ovFsUYFOQKCHSb3gUxcbQqXkMBYhvwzjj7K-MEwC8zHSjefxntUJL8p1DP_05XJRU9qZKKBE6yw5lpu5qBmLcb8ulMJtomtaGlCFjFCQqyyL-o2fbZ4B7l6VcOzV/xi3Uak9-i2GSE3iU5S-tGYgbJZE8VGRc/20)

name: Steven Wendelin
subject: Now is the for action
body:
Dear Friends and Supporters, When you signed up for my updates, I made you a promise. I would not flood your inbox with noise or constant asks. I would respect your time and speak to you when it matte | | | [](https://q8w2.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=69ee4965bc053d61b10e8087&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwendelinforcongress.com%2F%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D69ee45b59ed7d421d0a2303c%26ss_email_id%3D69ee4965bc053d61b10e8087%26ss_campaign_name%3DNow%2Bis%2Bthe%2Bfor%2Baction%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2026-04-26T17%253A20%253A58Z&w=64d253ff0603db3c564434a9&c=b_69ee45b59ed7d421d0a2303c&l=en-US&s=nnKJs1etU0_sHToshHavD0BlyWI%3D)
---
| Dear Friends and Supporters,When you signed up for my updates, I made you a promise. I would not flood your inbox with noise or constant asks. I would respect your time and speak to you when it mattered.This is one of those moments.For the past few months, I have driven every corner of the 2nd Congressional District. I have met many of you at “Meet the Candidate” events and community gatherings. I have sat down with journalists and taken every opportunity on the radio to deliver a simple message: our democracy is on the ballot.Now we act.Early voting starts this Wednesday and runs through Saturday, May 9th. The primary is Tuesday, May 12th. This is not a routine election. This is a test of whether we still have the will to stand up and be counted.Voting is not enough. Each of you needs to bring others with you. Call your friends. Talk to your neighbors. Turn out your family. Build the momentum that wins elections.This is not just about sending me to face Riley Moore in November. It is about drawing a line. West Virginians are being made sicker, poorer, and less free. We are being told to accept it. I reject that. And I know you do too.I have welcomed this primary because it demands the best from all of us. Stephanie and Ace have run honorable campaigns, and we have treated each other with respect. But this moment requires more than good intentions. It requires experience, discipline, and the will to fight and win.I have spent nearly four decades in service to this nation. I know how to lead under pressure. I know how to take a fight to the opposition and finish it. And I will start that fight the day after the primary.This race is winnable. Do not let anyone tell you otherwise. But it will take effort. It will take turnout. It will take each of us doing our part.If you know independents or non-affiliated voters, remind them they can vote in this primary. All they have to do is request a Democratic ballot at their polling place.Stand up. Show up. Bring others with you.I will see you on the other side. And in the words of John Paul Jones, “I have not yet begun to fight.”Very Respectfully,Steven
Commander Steven M. Wendelin, USN (Ret.)
Democratic Candidate for the U.S. House of Representatives
West Virginia’s 2nd Congressional District
---
| [](https://q8w2.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=69ee4965bc053d61b10e8087&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwendelinforcongress.com%2F%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D69ee45b59ed7d421d0a2303c%26ss_email_id%3D69ee4965bc053d61b10e8087%26ss_campaign_name%3DNow%2Bis%2Bthe%2Bfor%2Baction%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2026-04-26T17%253A20%253A58Z&w=64d253ff0603db3c564434a9&c=b_69ee45b59ed7d421d0a2303c&l=en-US&s=nnKJs1etU0_sHToshHavD0BlyWI%3D) Wendelin For Congress, PO Box 623, Wardensville, WV 26851, United States Powered by [Squarespace](https://q8w2.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=69ee4965bc053d61b10e8087&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.squarespace.com%3Fchannel%3Dproduct_refer%26subchannel%3Dcustomer%26source%3Demail_campaigns_button%26campaign%3D64d253ff0603db3c564434a9%26utm_medium%3Dproduct_refer%26utm_source%3Demail_campaigns_button%26ss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D69ee45b59ed7d421d0a2303c%26ss_email_id%3D69ee4965bc053d61b10e8087%26ss_campaign_name%3DNow%2Bis%2Bthe%2Bfor%2Baction%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2026-04-26T17%253A20%253A58Z&w=64d253ff0603db3c564434a9&c=b_69ee45b59ed7d421d0a2303c&l=en-US&s=RXgTClbsB5GAsxdHR305k-uvFiw%3D) [ Unsubscribe ](https://q8w2.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=69ee4965bc053d61b10e8087&u=https%3A%2F%2Fcampaign-preferences.com%2Funsubscribe%2Fl1Kdvh-URbEt1-K0MC2xN3yow1xdAoP96l6AgbKEwHwSkWEhosqKvbpcFKYza0rJXTJEchpnQ63LFXKQ7P05T96RvZ9fkvMEvzeBzBURUu4EXbDjNNysvZywwXuN3jdj9jJ37jQM05O3-4xfkJjCbiikL0qGToTRCeCOd6jNXlVJsy94OHjEhCIbnFnSeeGQ21hHrf6MYOLsIBylDIl5_bnPDhLakYJ3QOo1J9_JU4QsMdZz5WvWNoBZ-j1McB0aZdVHU-QDD1XYgXZ-6R_GBHZbfR6Fh6AcUynipOnWXxrl9L-j2H2c0hHGmy4O0ogG47QvBmunU4TSGDM_Xm8weZb3UwpUTJdgBxoGvPgu-J5tl9YhPMb9tdQ%3D&w=64d253ff0603db3c564434a9&c=b_69ee45b59ed7d421d0a2303c&l=en-US&s=v9S-qoOUDIroOcNjZH3mO98WPc8%3D)

name: David Hogg
subject: Remember McConnell’s playbook?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
This is why our mission is so important.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260426-1&t=1&akid=3403%2E587540%2EKT-s67)
Peter, it is infuriating to watch the Trump administration wreak havoc on our government and enact their dangerous, destructive agenda.
We need to be taking pages out of Mitch McConnell’s playbook that he used for years to stop Obama, to stop Biden, and prevent Democrats from passing our agenda. Everything has to be on the table to stop and slow Trump.
On top of that, we need to be organizing against vulnerable Republicans and turning the screws on them. We have to make their lives as painful as possible and line up the incentives for them to oppose Trump.
And finally we have to look ahead to upcoming elections and get behind people that are filled with rage over Trump’s actions and have a burning fire within them to stop Trump. People who will fight aggressively for a vision of this country that allows us to have more affordable housing, better healthcare, and provides some real economic opportunity and security.
That’s where we come in.
**[Can you pitch in $5 now to power our mission to elect the next generation of leaders?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260426_1_infuriating.yIvqb_&refcode=em20260426-3403&amount=5&t=2&akid=3403%2E587540%2EKT-s67)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260426_1_infuriating.yIvqb_&amount=5&refcode=em20260426-3403&t=21&akid=3403%2E587540%2EKT-s67)**
This is no joke. _Every single day counts in the fight ahead._
**Here’s the truth:** The GOP is already gearing up for the next fight. If we don’t keep our fundraising on track, the billionaires and corporate elite will keep pouring money into the Republican right-wing agenda while Democrats and progressives are left scrambling.
**But we’re not going to let that happen.** We may not have the special interests on our side, but I know that the grassroots can be even more powerful.
**We need leaders who fight for working families, voting rights, and climate justice. Leaders who will not back down when things get tough.**
That’s exactly why Leaders We Deserve exists. Now, we need your help to make sure we can keep electing the young, progressive leaders we deserve.
**[Can you chip in $5 now to help power our game-changing work?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260426_1_infuriating.yIvqb_&refcode=em20260426-3403&amount=5&t=22&akid=3403%2E587540%2EKT-s67)**
Thank you for being part of this movement,
David Hogg
Leaders We Deserve
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20260426-1&t=23&refcode2=3403_587540_KT-s67&akid=3403%2E587540%2EKT-s67)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=24&akid=3403%2E587540%2EKT-s67)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=3403%2E587540%2EKT-s67)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

**[Support The Capitalist, become a paid subscriber today](https://substack.com/redirect/dfd534f2-3839-49c7-943b-acb42edff4ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**
[Subscribe Here](https://substack.com/redirect/dfd534f2-3839-49c7-943b-acb42edff4ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
When a reporter once asked Pope John XXIII "how many people work at the Vatican?" he replied "half". Brilliant response. Even the Pope realized bureaucrats are useless.
Over 100,000 bureaucrats **" work"** at the Department of Agriculture though it is more honest to write "are on payroll". I doubt they all work. Considering there are fewer than 2 million farms in the U.S., each bureaucrat has only 18 farms in his portfolio.
I want to meet my farm bureaucrat. I'm going to call him George.
What does a farm bureaucrat know about sheep farming in rural Virginia? Nothing. George likely does not even own boots, but he regulates us. Or manages us. Likely George just screws up life with administrative nonsense to keep busy. Font sizes. Paperwork and forms and compliance and oversight. I could be a sheep farmer or a puppet show manager and George would perform the same meaningless, valueless tasks.
**The disconnect is enough to make one become bitter and speculative.**
Now, imagine my thoughts of my Congressman, or, my soon-to-be Congressman in Virginia's newly gerrymandered district. Like George the farm bureau bureaucrat, my Congressman, I will call this one Dick, does not or will not know a damn thing about me, my community, my neighbors, our area. But he regulates us. Or legislates on our behalf. Likely, Dick just screws up life with administrative nonsense: laws and spending programs and trans flags. And Dick's ego.
**The disconnect is enough to make one become bitter and speculative.**
I take gerrymandering personally. My Uncle was in Congress 16 years ago. After Rep. Anthony Weiner's scandal, when that district's Dick was caught posting pics of Anthony's weiner to the internet and forced to resign, my 70-year-old Uncle ran for the open seat, and defying the odds, won. Here was a white, male, Roman Catholic, conservative Republican winning a seat in deep-blue New York City. The seat once held by Senator Chuck Schumer and the first female Vice Presidential nominee Geraldine Ferraro.
Congressman Robert Turner would not last more than a term.
Democrats in Albany redrew the map, and my old neighborhood of New York City was "redrawn" to eliminate any and all support for a white, male, Roman Catholic, conservative Republican. Uncle Bob was now living in an amorphous blob of a map, the boundary lines drawn by a drunk person, weaving in and out of neighborhoods, crossing streets, to create a black supermajority district who would elect a democrat.
In a city of 8 million people, the one lone Republican voice, a dissenting voice, a minority voice, was successfully eliminated. "For democracy."
Of course, gerrymandering has been used by whites against blacks, against immigrants, against all groups, and that seems to be the accepted explanation for doing it today. Restitution. Equity.
**We are righting a wrong by doing a wrong because two wrongs DO make a right if by "right" you mean "democrat".**
Virginia's gerrymandered district eliminates the dissent from the right: rural voices. Seven districts now stem from one county (Fairfax) a DC suburb where very likely seven congressmen will live together representing districts that extend for hundreds of miles in the country, into Appalachia, into the Blue Ridge mountains, into my sheep farm. Seven congressmen will live on busy streets with train stations, metro stops, bus stops, with little futuristic R2D2 delivery robots bringing soft-skinned liberals a beyond meat burger (on lettuce, not a bun, because gluten).
Seven congressional districts intolerant of anyone who does not act, think, protest, march, vote, exactly as they do, for no one is more intolerant, no one is more closed minded, **no one is more unyielding than the person with a "Hate Has No Home Here" lawn sign.**
I was not surprised by the election results, though I was, foolishly, hopeful. I wear my hope in humanity as a badge of honor though it certainly does stomp on the soul. It has made me become bitter and speculative. No, my fellow Virginians would not vote to eliminate my vote. No, my fellow Virginians would try to violate the state constitution.
But these aren't Virginians. They are democrats. Virginia is just the place they have temporarily parked. They can't live in DC: **they 'll get murdered.** They can't send their kids to school in DC: **they 'll get murdered.** They want everything we have provided: stability, both economic and cultural, safety, wholesomeness, peace, prosperity, but they don't want the values or the culture or quite frankly even the people, who provided it. Rural America? Inbred hillbillies. Unsophisticated. Probably racist. Confederates. Unworthy of a seat at the table.
Yes, it is a good step that within 24-hours a judge found the gerrymander referendum unconstitutional, and the entire proposition will likely be thrown out by the courts, but that will only make the ethos worse. No democrat will see this as a learning opportunity. Queue the piano music, index finger scratching the temple, "I guess we learned our lesson. Everyone has a voice and everyone needs to be heard" as the septum ring ghouls nod in agreement as they place their "No Kings" signs in the trash can responsibly.
No, like the scene in "Blazing Saddles" when the Waco Kid stopped Sheriff Bart bringing a gun to confront Mongo, "Oh, no, don't do that...you'll only make him mad". A court loss will make these folks irate. They'll find another way, legal or illegal, it does not matter. They do not accept defeat, and they will do anything to win. Don't forget Jay Jones was running to be Virginia's attorney general when his text messages surfaced calling for the murder of republican children to teach his political opponent a lesson.
And he won.
"Virginians" i.e. cowardly liberals who would never dream of living in Washington, DC but who are hellbent on turning us into Washington, DC, found his fight refreshing.
In my bitterness and speculation, I wonder which is worse: sending pics of your Anthony wiener or advocating "one bullet for each kid" to get your political win?
The stuff I think about sitting on my porch, with my dog, my drink and my smoke, my shotgun maybe a little closer now, staring into the distance where over the mountains a hundred miles away my bureaucrat Goerge and my new Congressman Dick reside.
I bet they have matching lawn signs.
* * *
_**Daniel Turner is the founder and executive director of Power The Future, a national nonprofit organization that advocates for American energy jobs. He also runs a sheep and cattle farm in rural Virginia. Contact him at daniel@powerthefuture.com and follow him on Twitter @DanielTurnerPTF**_

clean_body: Surely my fellow Virginians would never vote to eliminate my vote...? | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Surely my fellow Virginians would never vote to eliminate my vote...? | | | Apr 26 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Subscribe to defeat the mainstream media, promote capitalism and American exceptionalism. . When a reporter once asked Pope John XXIII "how many people work at the Vatican?" he replied "half". Brilliant response. Even the Pope realized bureaucrats are useless. Over 100,000 bureaucrats " work" at the Department of Agriculture though it is more honest to write "are on payroll". I doubt they all work. Considering there are fewer than 2 million farms in the U.S., each bureaucrat has only 18 farms in his portfolio. I want to meet my farm bureaucrat. I'm going to call him George. What does a farm bureaucrat know about sheep farming in rural Virginia? Nothing. George likely does not even own boots, but he regulates us. Or manages us. Likely George just screws up life with administrative nonsense to keep busy. Font sizes. Paperwork and forms and compliance and oversight. I could be a sheep farmer or a puppet show manager and George would perform the same meaningless, valueless tasks. The disconnect is enough to make one become bitter and speculative. Now, imagine my thoughts of my Congressman, or, my soon-to-be Congressman in Virginia's newly gerrymandered district. Like George the farm bureau bureaucrat, my Congressman, I will call this one Dick, does not or will not know a damn thing about me, my community, my neighbors, our area. But he regulates us. Or legislates on our behalf. Likely, Dick just screws up life with administrative nonsense: laws and spending programs and trans flags. And Dick's ego. The disconnect is enough to make one become bitter and speculative. I take gerrymandering personally. My Uncle was in Congress 16 years ago. After Rep. Anthony Weiner's scandal, when that district's Dick was caught posting pics of Anthony's weiner to the internet and forced to resign, my 70-year-old Uncle ran for the open seat, and defying the odds, won. Here was a white, male, Roman Catholic, conservative Republican winning a seat in deep-blue New York City. The seat once held by Senator Chuck Schumer and the first female Vice Presidential nominee Geraldine Ferraro. Congressman Robert Turner would not last more than a term. Democrats in Albany redrew the map, and my old neighborhood of New York City was "redrawn" to eliminate any and all support for a white, male, Roman Catholic, conservative Republican. Uncle Bob was now living in an amorphous blob of a map, the boundary lines drawn by a drunk person, weaving in and out of neighborhoods, crossing streets, to create a black supermajority district who would elect a democrat. In a city of 8 million people, the one lone Republican voice, a dissenting voice, a minority voice, was successfully eliminated. "For democracy." Of course, gerrymandering has been used by whites against blacks, against immigrants, against all groups, and that seems to be the accepted explanation for doing it today. Restitution. Equity. We are righting a wrong by doing a wrong because two wrongs DO make a right if by "right" you mean "democrat". Virginia's gerrymandered district eliminates the dissent from the right: rural voices. Seven districts now stem from one county (Fairfax) a DC suburb where very likely seven congressmen will live together representing districts that extend for hundreds of miles in the country, into Appalachia, into the Blue Ridge mountains, into my sheep farm. Seven congressmen will live on busy streets with train stations, metro stops, bus stops, with little futuristic R2D2 delivery robots bringing soft-skinned liberals a beyond meat burger (on lettuce, not a bun, because gluten). Seven congressional districts intolerant of anyone who does not act, think, protest, march, vote, exactly as they do, for no one is more intolerant, no one is more closed minded, no one is more unyielding than the person with a "Hate Has No Home Here" lawn sign. I was not surprised by the election results, though I was, foolishly, hopeful. I wear my hope in humanity as a badge of honor though it certainly does stomp on the soul. It has made me become bitter and speculative. No, my fellow Virginians would not vote to eliminate my vote. No, my fellow Virginians would try to violate the state constitution. But these aren't Virginians. They are democrats. Virginia is just the place they have temporarily parked. They can't live in DC: they 'll get murdered. They can't send their kids to school in DC: they 'll get murdered. They want everything we have provided: stability, both economic and cultural, safety, wholesomeness, peace, prosperity, but they don't want the values or the culture or quite frankly even the people, who provided it. Rural America? Inbred hillbillies. Unsophisticated. Probably racist. Confederates. Unworthy of a seat at the table. Yes, it is a good step that within 24-hours a judge found the gerrymander referendum unconstitutional, and the entire proposition will likely be thrown out by the courts, but that will only make the ethos worse. No democrat will see this as a learning opportunity. Queue the piano music, index finger scratching the temple, "I guess we learned our lesson. Everyone has a voice and everyone needs to be heard" as the septum ring ghouls nod in agreement as they place their "No Kings" signs in the trash can responsibly. No, like the scene in "Blazing Saddles" when the Waco Kid stopped Sheriff Bart bringing a gun to confront Mongo, "Oh, no, don't do that...you'll only make him mad". A court loss will make these folks irate. They'll find another way, legal or illegal, it does not matter. They do not accept defeat, and they will do anything to win. Don't forget Jay Jones was running to be Virginia's attorney general when his text messages surfaced calling for the murder of republican children to teach his political opponent a lesson. And he won. "Virginians" i.e. cowardly liberals who would never dream of living in Washington, DC but who are hellbent on turning us into Washington, DC, found his fight refreshing. In my bitterness and speculation, I wonder which is worse: sending pics of your Anthony wiener or advocating "one bullet for each kid" to get your political win? The stuff I think about sitting on my porch, with my dog, my drink and my smoke, my shotgun maybe a little closer now, staring into the distance where over the mountains a hundred miles away my bureaucrat Goerge and my new Congressman Dick reside. I bet they have matching lawn signs. Daniel Turner is the founder and executive director of Power The Future, a national nonprofit organization that advocates for American energy jobs. He also runs a sheep and cattle farm in rural Virginia. name: Wes Moore
subject: Because of you
body:
I really mean it when I say that this is a people powered movement.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1328%2E1086043%2EMAOWsG)
**[Hi, Peter, I want to share a quick story about the grassroots movement powering our campaign. If you’re in a rush, you can donate right now.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260426-1.5eCv-Y&refcode=em-20260426-1&t=2&akid=1328%2E1086043%2EMAOWsG)**
_“I think you’re the one candidate who can, you know, really bring up the full potential in Baltimore and that’s why I support you and always have.”_ _“I’m grateful to be a part of the Moore-mement. Moore-mentum! I love that word!”_ _“Your campaign and your beliefs are exactly what this state needs so looking forward to assisting you and helping you.”_ _“If you believe it, you can definitely achieve it.”_ _“You are impacting so many people and we’re so hopeful and excited.”_
---
Peter, I’ve always made a habit of calling donors to thank them. I did it back in 2022, I do it often in this campaign for re-election, and these are just a few moments from conversations that stuck with me.
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260426-1.5eCv-Y&refcode=em-20260426-1&t=3&akid=1328%2E1086043%2EMAOWsG)
I want you to know how grateful I am for every single grassroots donation. Truly. And whenever I can, I’ll take the time to say thank you directly.
**_Right now, 73% of our donors have given $100 or less. That’s something I’m incredibly proud of._**
While some politicians spend their time with corporate PAC donors at fancy galas, I’m focused on building this campaign with people like you.
**[If you’re able, I’d be grateful for your support today. Can I count on you to chip in $5 to help power this grassroots movement?](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260426-1.5eCv-Y&refcode=em-20260426-1&t=4&akid=1328%2E1086043%2EMAOWsG)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20260426-1.5eCv-Y&amount=5&refcode=em-20260426-1&t=23&akid=1328%2E1086043%2EMAOWsG)**
Thank you. I'm grateful for this team.
-- Wes

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20260426-1&t=24&refcode2=1328_1086043_MAOWsG&akid=1328%2E1086043%2EMAOWsG)**
**[Volunteer »](https://support.wesmoore.com/a/volunteer?source=em-20260426-1)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=25&akid=1328%2E1086043%2EMAOWsG)

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=1328%2E1086043%2EMAOWsG)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

clean_body: We are SIX MONTHS out from Election Day. That's it. | = Peter, we sent you something important earlier this week, and we want to make sure it didn't get buried in your inbox. We are SIX MONTHS out from Election Day. That's it. The runway is short, the stakes are high, and the women we're fighting to elect need our support RIGHT NOW — before the final sprint begins. = | = Here's something political insiders know — and we want you to know too:How we finish this month sends an important message to our opponents. We've gotten a first look at Republican fundraising numbers, and they’re alarming. The GOP has the resources to run massive programs in every battleground state, and we can’t afford to fall behind and let their message override ours as we close in on this critical final stretch. So, Peter, we need the full support of this team to finish strong. ******=****** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thanks, Tiernan Donohue (she/her/hers) Chief Campaigns Officer, EMILYs List \-------------Original Message------------- Sent: Saturday, April 25 From: EMILYs List = = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = = | = | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. Hi there, I’m Jindalae. 👋

As Derek Tran’s campaign manager, it’s my job to make sure we have the tools we need to keep this seat blue.

I know you’ve heard a lot lately from Derek and the team about the upcoming fundraising deadline. I want you to know that we don’t reach out unless we need to – and the stakes are _really_ that high in CA-45.

I’ve worked on some tough campaigns before. Believe me when I tell you that Derek’s re-election is the most consequential for control of the House and Democrats’ ability to hold the Trump Administration accountable.

As I spent more time on the ground in the district, it’s clear that working families need representation they can rely on. Derek takes that responsibility very seriously, and while he’s fighting for results in Washington, we need to have his back.

**[Will you chip in $3 before the weekend is over to keep our voter outreach fully funded? 653 votes separated us and our GOP opponent in 2024, and with four challengers vying for this seat, we need a surge of support to prepare for the June primary.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130213359/616999099/1897980251?refcode=em-4.26.26-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDAxMTQyZTgtOTk0MS1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Dc9Umo-FIQFZHj6xJIBkzIqRbqaDvUsVM4BVWWgvzkg=)**
