clean_body: We’ve built a foothold. Now what? | | | | | Peter, I'm going to be blunt with you. We are closer to losing this country than most people are willing to admit. Not someday. Not "if things keep going this way." Right now. And the scariest part? It's not happening in Washington. It's happening in classrooms. High schools. Colleges. Places where the next generation is being shaped -- or more accurately... reshaped. If you've ever looked at what's coming out of our schools and thought: " What happened to this country?" You're not wrong. You're seeing it clearly. Because while most people are arguing online... while they're hoping things will turn around... while they're waiting for the next election to fix everything... The other side is doing something far more dangerous. They're raising the next generation. Earlier. Faster. And with zero resistance in most places. That's how countries are lost. Slowly. Then all at once. But here's the thing that should give a patriot like you hope... There is one place where that trend is actually being challenged. And it shouldn 't even exist. Just one year ago, Turning Point USA 's high school program -- which is now called "Club America" -- was in about 1,200 schools. That alone felt like a miracle. Because starting even ONE chapter today takes courage most adults don't have. Conservative students get mocked. Targeted. Threatened. Shut down. And yet... They stepped up anyway. About that time, Charlie called me. He didn't sound optimistic... He sounded urgent. "Andrew, we've got to move faster." I told him we were. He stopped me. "No. You're not thinking what I'm thinking. I want to be in 35,000 high schools." I actually laughed. "Charlie... there aren't even 35,000 high schools in America." "Okay," he said. "How many are there?" "About 27,000." And without hesitation: "Good. Let's do 27,000." I remember sitting there thinking to myself... we're barely in 1,200 schools. We didn't have the staff. We didn't have the infrastructure. We didn't even have the roadmap yet. But Charlie wasn't fazed. " We'll figure it out." That was him. He didn't wait for perfect conditions. Because he knew something most people don't want to face: If we wait... we lose everything. And now? Less than a year later... We 're in 3,300 schools. Thirteen percent of all high schools in America. Eight states are now backing this effort to put a Club America chapter in every high school statewide. That shouldn 't be possible. Not in this environment. Not with the level of hostility these students face every single day. And yet... it's still happening. But Peter, here 's the part you need to understand: This is not a victory. It's a foothold. Because in the other 87% of schools? There is no resistance. None. Just one-sided propaganda from the Left. One-sided pressure on kids who are too young to even realize what's happening to them. That's the reality. The question isn't whether we're fighting. We are. The question is whether it's enough. Because right now, the students stepping up through Club America... They're not the majority. They 're the exception. They're the ones willing to take the heat. The ones willing to stand alone. The ones willing to risk being targeted just to say: " I love this country." And every time one of those students raises their hand... We have a choice. Back them. Or leave them on their own. Because when a student starts a chapter, we send them an activism kit -- with American flags. Pocket Constitutions. Leadership tools. Everything they need to actually stand their ground. Not symbolically. For real. They've already made the decision to lead. All they're waiting on... is you and me. Each activism kit costs $25. That 's it. $25 to make sure one student doesn't have to stand alone against the Left in a hostile school. $25 to put real tools in their hands. $25 to push back -- even if it's one campus at a time. But here's what gives me hope, Peter... Club America isn't slowing down. It's accelerating. Because every day, more students are stepping forward. Not because it's easy or popular... But because they know what 's at stake. They're starting chapters in schools where they were told not to. They're speaking truth in classrooms where the Left once went unchallenged. They're choosing courage in places designed to make them conform. And they're not alone. They're finding each other. They're bringing more students with them every day. They're building something real -- rooted in faith and freedom, grounded in truth, and unafraid to say that America is worth defending. That's how movements are built. Not from the top down... But from the ground up. One student. One school. One chapter at a time. And Peter -- that's exactly how we take this country back. But only if we move fast enough. Because the Left isn't slowing down. They're not taking a break. They're not afraid to push their anti-American ideas in every classroom they're in. And if we don't match that urgency... if we don't meet that moment with equal conviction... Then the generational window Charlie Kirk opened to save our country closes. And once it closes... It closes for good. That's what's at stake. Let's not waste this moment. Let's back our brave students -- while it still matters. | | For Charlie, Andrew Sypher Chief Field OfficerTurning Point USA P.S. I need you to hear this clearly... Charlie Kirk wasn't just dreaming big when he called me last year about Club America's progress. He was warning us: "The enemy is moving younger. They're coming after students earlier. We don 't have time to wait." And he was right. Look around. Club America may be in 13% of high schools... But in the other 87%? There is no pushback against the Left. No one telling those kids the truth. Just a Leftist pipeline -- from classroom... to belief... to voting booth. And once that pipeline is complete... It 's over. Peter, this is one of the last places a patriot like you can stop it before it 's too late to save our country. You may not be able to step into a hostile high school and stand for truth like these students do. But you can give critical support to a young freedom fighter who will. There's still time to turn this country around... and this is one of the few ways we can actually do it -- one student, one school, one chapter at a time. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

**FBI's Urgent Warning Sounds the Alarm** America's enemies have found a way to strike without firing a single shot. Hackers are attacking city water systems, overriding safety protocols to weaponize our utilities against us and positioning themselves to strike when the signal comes. National security experts warn China isn't alone. Iran, Russia and North Korea are preparing coordinated attacks. Overflowing, damaging and poisoning the lifegiving systems we trust to keep us safe. **Like it or not, your local tap water may be our country's weakest link.** Deteriorating infrastructure. Systems years beyond their expiration dates. Negligent corporations. And God-knows-what in the water we drink. We're up against arsenic, plastics, chlorine, forever chemicals, hormone-altering drug residue and a host of other contaminants. Listen, I'm not trying to be the messenger of doom... _**But protecting the water supply in your home is more important than ever.**_ If you don't take steps to be prepared, you and your family could end up vulnerable. Forced to choose between drinking poisoned, toxic or dirty water, or going without water altogether. It sounds harsh... But the truth is that even good people with good intentions can make serious mistakes. Mistakes like...
* **Getting filters that claim to purify your water, but don't prove it.**
* If a company doesn't have certifications from a third-party lab, show lab tests or reveal how many gallons were tested... that's a bad sign. After a couple gallons, most water filters on the market stop working for scary contaminants and start passing them into your water.
* **Getting bottled water with a 2-year shelf life and risking plastic leaching into your supply.**
* Not to mention all the money you'll waste, and the work required to carry cases of water. Plus with bottled water, you never really know what's been filtered out.
* **Or simply using a pitcher in the fridge... with slapshot filtration power and limited capacity.**
* Most pitchers hold a couple cups of water at a time. Not nearly enough to drink, cook and clean in an emergency. You'll run out in no time, just when your life depends on it.

* * *
_**by Lawrence Winnerman, Blue Amp Media COO**_
I was eight years old the first time a man decided my body was his to use.
He was a stranger. Some old man in a JCPenney dressing room at a shopping mall. My mother didn't know him. Nobody knew him. We never caught him. I was a child, and I learned something that afternoon that no child should have to learn: that there are men in the world who see a smaller body and feel entitled to it, and there is an entire civilization prepared to look the other way.
I'm fifty-six now. Five-ten, two-sixty, built like a lumberjack. Nobody looks at me and thinks _victim_. And yet. I'm an openly gay man. I bottom--and if that word makes you uncomfortable, good, stay with me, because your discomfort is a fraction of what I'm about to talk about. I know what toxic masculinity sounds like from the inside, because it pervades the gay community too--the hierarchy of tops over bottoms, the sorting of men into who penetrates and who receives, the quiet assumption that receiving makes you less. I've lived in that hierarchy my entire adult life. I know exactly how the gears turn. The same machinery that sorts men in a bedroom is the machinery that sorts women in a legislature, that sorts bodies in a culture, that produces--reliably, generationally--the system I'm about to describe.
I have standing to make this case. And I mean that in a way that goes deeper than opinion.
Eric Swalwell was arrested by no one. [Four women came forward](https://substack.com/redirect/0d524ade-fd75-4f26-af0e-f6394ebee7fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--one of them a twenty-year-old intern who says he raped her--and within a week he [resigned from Congress](https://substack.com/redirect/4f416241-45a3-4021-add0-6ec79f9f752b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), abandoned his campaign for governor of California, and vanished into the fog of the next news cycle. By May it will be a footnote.
I read the allegations and I didn't feel outrage. I'm past outrage. What I felt was recognition. The machine that produced what happened to me in that dressing room is the same machine that produced Eric Swalwell, the same machine that protected Jeffrey Epstein for decades, the same machine that right now--today, this week, while you're reading this--is grinding through legislatures and courtrooms and algorithm-fed rabbit holes to ensure that women, half the human species, remain controllable, available, and silent.
**So let me make a case.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.uSU3TG-t-Jf_dh8VH2hntZVAhThHAjZNGYzGlYw7dgQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=195558153)
• • •
### **Count 1: The Predator Class**
Every few months, another name surfaces. We perform the ritual--shock, denial, investigation, consequences for some, impunity for most--and then we move on, and we never once talk about the ecosystem that keeps producing the names.
Eric Swalwell. Four women. Sexual misconduct including rape, sexual assault of intoxicated women, unsolicited explicit messages sent via Snapchat. One accuser started interning for him in 2019 when she was twenty years old--hadn't even graduated from college. The [House Ethics Committee opened an investigation](https://substack.com/redirect/845a36ac-3b11-4456-8228-d4edd764e9ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); approximately forty-five minutes later, Swalwell announced his resignation. He was a Democrat. He sat on the Intelligence Committee. He went on cable news and talked about accountability.
And here's the thing about Swalwell that nobody wants to say out loud: Democrats forced him out in a week. As they should have. Al Franken was gone in days. Anthony Weiner, gone. Katie Hill, gone. The left holds its own to account because that's what accountability looks like. But Matt Gaetz faced a sex trafficking investigation involving a seventeen-year-old and was nominated for Attorney General. Jim Jordan was accused of ignoring sexual abuse of wrestlers when he was an assistant coach at Ohio State and went on to chair the House Judiciary Committee. Donald Trump was found liable for sexual abuse by a civil jury and was elected president. Again. The right doesn't hold its people to account--not because it can't, but because it won't. And the result is a perverse optical illusion: Democratic names dominate the headlines about sexual misconduct because Democrats are the only ones generating consequences. Republican predators stay in office, stay quiet, and stay powerful. In the court of public opinion, it starts to look like the problem belongs to one party--when what's actually happening is that only one party treats it as a problem at all. The asymmetry isn't a bug. It's the strategy. Hold no one accountable, and you never have to explain anything. Let the other side do the right thing, and weaponize their integrity against them.
Jeffrey Epstein. [Three million pages of files released in January 2026](https://substack.com/redirect/47d0915e-71e2-4cf5-b065-a3c8aa3863e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--two thousand videos, one hundred eighty thousand images. The Department of Justice acknowledged that six million pages may ultimately qualify for release. Communications between Epstein and a vast web of figures in politics, academia, and business continued _after he registered as a sex offender_. Both parties. All industries. A December 2025 poll found that 91 percent of Democrats, 78 percent of independents, and 74 percent of Republicans wanted the files released. The only people who didn't want sunlight were the people in the files.
And then there's the one that should have stopped the world and didn't. In March 2026, [CNN published the results of a months-long investigation](https://substack.com/redirect/46dc0c2c-e51c-4633-80a9-02341f313ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) into what they called a global "online rape academy"--a pornographic website called Motherless.com hosting more than twenty thousand videos of "sleep content," which is the sanitized term for footage of men drugging and raping their unconscious partners. A linked Telegram group called "Zzz" had nearly a thousand members actively teaching each other the craft of it. How to select the drug. How to dose it. How to avoid detection. A [man in Poland was arrested](https://substack.com/redirect/55c55ee0-b5ec-4a96-bad3-674f220dfd04?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as a direct result of the reporting.
A thousand men in a chat room, workshopping rape. Twenty thousand videos. Hundreds of thousands of views.
Swalwell, Epstein, the men in that Telegram group--they are the visible tips of something that goes all the way down. A system that produces predators reliably, at scale, and then provides them with legal, cultural, and technological infrastructure to operate. The predator class isn't a conspiracy. It's an output.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=195558153&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1NTU4MTUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzcyMzUxOTAsImV4cCI6MTc3OTgyNzE5MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RbRqEUb_utjv1YI_2FTCS_Tv0IaCmfQHXVK-pi0y12Y)
• • •
### **Count 2: The Pipeline**
If you have a son under twenty-five, he is one algorithm away from the top of this funnel. I'm not being dramatic. That is what the research says.
It starts with looksmaxxing--a trend in which young men use apps to score their facial attractiveness on the PSL scale, a rating system invented in incel communities that ranks faces using terms like "low-tier normie" and "chadlite." One looksmaxxing influencer alone racked up more than [a hundred million views in 2025](https://substack.com/redirect/a86616d8-acfc-44b9-bd13-c4c0d3daffcf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The apps are in the App Store right now. Your kid can download one during lunch. And in case you think this is just vanity with a weird name: looksmaxxing communities actively encourage boys to strike themselves in the face with hammers--to fracture their own facial bones so the bones will, supposedly, grow back stronger and more masculine. Boys are hitting themselves in the face with hammers to meet a beauty standard invented by incels. That's where the pipeline starts.
The Alan Turing Institute's Centre for Emerging Technology and Security published a study titled ["From Looksmaxxing to Mass Shootings: Radicalisation and Online Misogyny."](https://substack.com/redirect/6045ff02-7e5d-4ba6-a152-f41a7fc152a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) The title tells you everything. The pipeline works like this: a boy downloads an app that scores his face. The app uses language and frameworks created by incels. The algorithm feeds him more content. The content gets darker. He moves from TikTok to Discord to Telegram. The communities he finds there are actively directing young men toward incel ideology, neo-Nazi content, and material that glorifies mass shooters and suicide.
All of this is documented. [OFCOM](https://substack.com/redirect/f14b12cf-6cdb-47b8-8a10-18f3ef619b51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--the UK's communications regulator--published a sixty-five-page report on young people's engagement with the manosphere. Research in Wiley's _Child and Adolescent Mental Health_ journal found the manosphere encouraging sexist attitudes in schools, deepening existing inequalities, spreading dangerous messages about mental health. The [University of Alabama's Institute for Human Rights](https://substack.com/redirect/5e236082-0513-4d51-a1bc-d9d8a5e3a293?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) mapped what they call "modern sexism" from its digital roots to its violent endpoints.
The pipeline is designed. It has an architecture. It has an on-ramp that looks like self-help and an off-ramp that looks like a manifesto. And every man who built it is counting on you--personally, specifically you--to assume your son is too smart to fall for it.
[Leave a comment](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=195558153&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1NTU4MTUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzcyMzUxOTAsImV4cCI6MTc3OTgyNzE5MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RbRqEUb_utjv1YI_2FTCS_Tv0IaCmfQHXVK-pi0y12Y&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment)
• • •
### **Count 3: The Legislative Assault**
In August 2025, [Doug Wilson](https://substack.com/redirect/f8f96750-d944-44bc-8cbe-df41b4b1f7ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--pastor of the Communion of Reformed Evangelical Churches, a growing denomination with approximately a hundred and fifty congregations--told CNN that the Nineteenth Amendment granting women the right to vote "was a bad idea."
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth [attends a CREC church](https://substack.com/redirect/522198ec-8397-4ab9-b4a2-e8ba07ac5ba4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). His Pentagon spokesman confirmed the affiliation and said Hegseth "very much appreciates many of Mr. Wilson's writings and teachings." Democratic congresswomen, several of them military veterans, [demanded his resignation](https://substack.com/redirect/c3de1edf-3bd9-4ea0-b4ec-d3a58382fa15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
That's the Secretary of Defense of the United States, aligned with a church whose leader wants to repeal women's suffrage. And here's the part that should keep you up at night: another CREC pastor admitted the quiet part out loud. "I don't think Christians should make repealing the Nineteenth Amendment their main issue right now," he said, "because it's not a winning issue yet."
**Yet.**
The word "yet" is doing all the work in that sentence. The strategy is to make fringe ideas seem plausible enough that less extreme shifts toward theocracy and patriarchy seem reasonable by comparison. Move the Overton window so far that taking away a woman's right to vote sounds radical, but taking away her right to control her own body sounds like a compromise.
Meanwhile, in state legislatures across the country, a study by the [Carnegie Endowment for International Peace](https://substack.com/redirect/4227a6fa-04cc-4ad1-8871-1d86bcaba2ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that 11.85 percent of state legislators belonged to far-right Facebook groups. That small minority sponsored 66 percent of all anti-abortion bills and 62 percent of all anti-LGBTQ bills introduced in their legislative sessions. A fraction of the political class is driving the overwhelming majority of the legislative assault on bodily autonomy--and they're doing it in gerrymandered districts where their positions are wildly out of step with public opinion.
And the economic data is unambiguous. Women in states with [robust reproductive health care access](https://substack.com/redirect/07012c98-437e-4a60-b78b-06da9cbcc6c7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--contraceptive coverage, expanded Medicaid, public funding for medically necessary procedures--have higher earnings, face less occupational segregation, and are less likely to be trapped in part-time work. Every dollar spent on contraceptive services saves three dollars in pregnancy-related care. Reproductive access is the economic foundation on which women build independent lives. Strip it away and you strip away the independence. That's the point. That has always been the point.
And then there's [Project 2025](https://substack.com/redirect/f73f6cfb-c3b5-4bf2-bb4e-77af65e4417a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--the Heritage Foundation's nine-hundred-page blueprint for the next Republican administration--which proposes treating gender education as a sex offense and makes zero reference to the rates of violence targeting LGBTQ people. Nine hundred pages. They had room. They chose silence.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.uSU3TG-t-Jf_dh8VH2hntZVAhThHAjZNGYzGlYw7dgQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=195558153)
• • •
### **Count 4: The Structural Failure**
In April 2024, the New York Court of Appeals [overturned Harvey Weinstein's landmark sexual assault conviction](https://substack.com/redirect/63c7048f-4953-465b-b982-a77c5cb11788?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on a 4-3 vote. The technicality: testimony from "prior bad acts" witnesses shouldn't have been admitted because it served "only to establish defendant's propensity to commit the crimes charged."
Sit with that for a second. A court ruled that evidence showing a rapist had a pattern of raping was too prejudicial to the rapist.
Judge Madeline Singas wrote in dissent that the decision continued "a disturbing trend of overturning juries' guilty verdicts in cases involving sexual violence" and perpetuated "outdated notions of sexual violence" that "allow predators to escape accountability." In the retrial, Weinstein was convicted on one count, acquitted on another. Partial justice for a man dozens of women accused over decades. The system working exactly as it was designed to work.
And look--between 2016 and 2022, state legislatures across the country introduced more than three thousand bills aimed at gender equity and workplace safety. They passed 382 of them. Pay equity, harassment training, reporting procedures, leave policies, elimination of the nondisclosure agreements that protect predators. Real progress, passed by real legislators who fought for every vote.
**The legal system still cannot reliably convict a rapist.**
Me Too changed the culture. It changed who we believe, how quickly we respond, what we're willing to say out loud. But it did not change the machine. The machine still runs. The courtrooms still fail. The statutes of limitations still expire. The burden of proof still falls on the body that was violated, not the body that did the violating. And every time a conviction gets overturned on a technicality, every man in that Telegram group, every lobbyist drafting an abortion ban, every influencer teaching boys to score women's faces on a numerical scale receives the same message: _the system is yours_.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=195558153&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1NTU4MTUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzcyMzUxOTAsImV4cCI6MTc3OTgyNzE5MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RbRqEUb_utjv1YI_2FTCS_Tv0IaCmfQHXVK-pi0y12Y)
• • •
### **Count 5: The Global Playbook**
Everything I've described so far has been American. The dysfunction, the polarization, the broken institutions. But the playbook is global, and if you don't see that, you're looking at the trees and missing the forest that's on fire.
In November 2024, [RAND published a study](https://substack.com/redirect/50224e7e-15e9-492e-8499-5d1c303528ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) concluding that "regressive ideas on gender and gender equality can be a useful proxy metric for democratic backsliding and authoritarian rise." The United States government's own Women, Peace, and Security strategy recommends treating misogyny--online or codified in policy--as an early indicator of authoritarian consolidation.
Read that again. Our own government's own framework says: when misogyny shows up in law, authoritarianism is coming. Every time. Without exception.
_**Russia**_ : Vladimir Putin positioned himself as the guardian of "traditional Christian values," told women their place was at home raising children, and systematically rolled back domestic violence protections. _**Turkey**_ : Erdoğan withdrew from the Istanbul Convention on gender-based violence, reversed secular marriage requirements, and opened the legal door to underage marriages and polygamy. _**Hungary**_ : Orban moved to ban abortion and restrict LGBTQ rights as cornerstones of his "illiberal democracy." _**Poland**_ : the Law and Justice party packed courts with political allies, then used those compromised judges to eliminate abortion rights--against the clear will of the Polish majority. _**Brazil**_ : Bolsonaro wasn't even particularly religious before his campaign, but evangelical churches powered his 2018 victory. Once in office, six of his twenty-four cabinet members were evangelical Protestants who restricted LGBTQ rights, banned gender discussions in classrooms, and cast doubt on the need for gender equality policies altogether.
The [Carnegie Endowment for International Peace published the definitive analysis](https://substack.com/redirect/4227a6fa-04cc-4ad1-8871-1d86bcaba2ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in November 2024: patriarchy and authoritarianism are "mutually reinforcing political projects." Their research demonstrated that voters with sexist attitudes are significantly more likely to support authoritarian leaders--even after controlling for political ideology. A 2017 European study found that traditional gender views predicted support for radical-right parties across twenty-three countries.
[Harvard Kennedy School researchers](https://substack.com/redirect/cf1773e8-3835-4abf-a55f-62bb607a9ae3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) put it plainly: authoritarian leaders seek to "displace democracy with hierarchies controlled by male elites and to re-confine women in traditional roles." They minimize women's equal rights, frame equality as niche identity politics, and they do this because patriarchy is the structural foundation on which authoritarian power is built. The hierarchy of men over women is the load-bearing wall. Pull it out and the authoritarian project collapses.
Which is why every one of these regimes came for women's rights first. Or simultaneously with the power grab. Never after. The sequence is the tell.
[Leave a comment](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=195558153&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1NTU4MTUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzcyMzUxOTAsImV4cCI6MTc3OTgyNzE5MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RbRqEUb_utjv1YI_2FTCS_Tv0IaCmfQHXVK-pi0y12Y&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment)
• • •
### **Count 6: The Foundation**
Now the data. And I need you to stay with me here, because this is where the floor drops out.
[Georgetown University's Institute for Women, Peace and Security](https://substack.com/redirect/ea6e602d-0006-4b23-b01a-4a021aa2b81a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) studied the relationship between women's status and democratic health--free elections, free association, checks on government power--across dozens of countries and found them strongly correlated. The [Varieties of Democracy project](https://substack.com/redirect/03c24e90-f6ef-43e7-b66b-213f00acb19f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), spanning a hundred countries over four decades, demonstrated that women's political empowerment directly strengthens democratic participation. When women lead, cooperative governance increases, peacemaking improves, social welfare expands, economies grow. This is not ideology. This is the data set.
The economics alone should end every argument anyone has ever had about this. [McKinsey projected a one-trillion-dollar gain](https://substack.com/redirect/0a6300aa-1da9-4b7f-8b1f-b2e617c0b2d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the global economy from closing gaps in women's health care, including reproductive health. One trillion. The UNFPA documented that every one-percentage-point increase in gender-based violence in sub-Saharan Africa correlates with up to an 8 percent decline in economic activity, driven primarily by drops in female employment. Intimate partner violence costs the global economy an estimated 5 percent of GDP--five trillion dollars, every year, hemorrhaging out of civilization like an open wound that we just keep walking past.
The [Brennan Center for Justice](https://substack.com/redirect/9480da33-13cc-4225-87c5-1d227beaafcd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that more than 40 percent of state legislators have experienced threats. Women legislators are three to four times more likely to face explicitly sexualized abuse. Women are being threatened out of public life. And the threats are working. And the men making the threats know the threats are working. And the rest of us are watching it happen.
Women's freedom is the foundation on which democratic societies are built. Every piece of data confirms it. Every country that has tried to build democracy while constraining women has failed. Every authoritarian who has consolidated power has done so by attacking women's autonomy first. The correlation is so strong, so consistent, so thoroughly documented across decades of research and dozens of countries that _to deny it at this point is to deny gravity_.
If women are not equally free--equally free, with the same control over their bodies and their futures and their participation in civic life--then this whole experiment in self-governance is bullshit. I mean that word precisely. I mean it as a factual assessment of the evidence I have just laid in front of you. Democracy that does not include the full freedom of women is a word with nothing behind it--a promise made to half the species and broken every single day.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.uSU3TG-t-Jf_dh8VH2hntZVAhThHAjZNGYzGlYw7dgQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=195558153)
• • •
### **The Verdict**
Six counts. Predators at the top. A radicalization pipeline feeding boys into the machine from the bottom. A legislative apparatus stripping rights in the middle. A legal system that fails to convict. A global playbook that repeats the same pattern on every continent. And a mountain of data proving that when you attack women's freedom, you are attacking democracy itself--at the foundation, at the load-bearing wall, at the thing without which the whole structure is a lie.
I've made my case. Now I'm talking to men.
Women already know all of this. They've been living it. They've been screaming it. They are exhausted from explaining it to men who nod sympathetically and go back to their lives. I am done watching that happen.
I'm talking to you. The man reading this right now. The husband, the father, the brother, the son, the friend, the colleague, the guy who considers himself one of the good ones.
Are you fighting for the women in your life? I don't mean performing allyship on social media. I don't mean the pin on your jacket or the post you shared that one time. Are you intervening when the joke gets told at dinner? Are you calling your state legislator when an abortion ban hits committee? Are you monitoring what your son is watching, who he's following, what pipeline is pulling him toward a chat room full of men who think consent is optional? Are you showing up at school board meetings? Are you voting in every single election--every one, including the ones that bore you--like your daughter's freedom depends on it?
**Because it does.**
Are you risking something? Losing something? Spending social capital, burning a friendship, making yourself uncomfortable in a room full of men who expect your silence? Because if you aren't doing those things, you aren't fighting. You're watching. And watching is a choice.
I know what the machine does. I learned it when I was eight years old, in a dressing room in a JCPenney, from a man no one ever caught. I've lived inside the toxic masculinity that sorts men into hierarchies of dominance and submission and calls it natural order. And I am telling you--from inside the experience, from inside the evidence, from inside the cold fury that comes from knowing both at once--that the time for choosing sides is practically over.
The forces arrayed against women's freedom are organized. They are funded. They are global, patient, and strategic. They have a legislative agenda and a judicial strategy. They have a radicalization pipeline that starts with a face-scoring app and ends with men in a chat room teaching each other how to rape. They have a Secretary of Defense who attends a church whose pastor wants to repeal women's suffrage. They have Project 2025. They have three million pages of Epstein files proving that the most powerful men in the world operated a sexual abuse network for decades with functional impunity. They have momentum.
**What do you have?**
If the answer is good intentions and a vague sense that things will work out--then I need you to hear me very clearly.
> **You are a coward. You are a stupid, selfish, ugly, mean, small, weak, cowardly coward.**
You are watching a war--a real war, fought every single day on the bodies of women and girls and queer people--and you are choosing comfort over duty, passivity over action, your own ease over someone else's survival. History will judge you. Your daughters will judge you. And they will be right.
Every generation plants the seeds of the fights the next generation will have to wage. The fights we will be having for the next hundred years are being seeded right now. In legislatures. In courtrooms. In algorithms. In churches. In chat rooms. In the silence of men who know better and do nothing.
Pick up a weapon. It doesn't have to be a literal one--make it your voice, your vote, your time, your money, your willingness to be uncomfortable, your refusal to let one more joke slide, one more policy pass, one more boy disappear into the pipeline while you tell yourself it won't happen to your kid.
The war is already here. It has been here. Women have been fighting it alone for long enough.
#### **What side are you on?**

Pick Up a Weapon. | | and | Apr 26 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- This article was on Lawrence Winnerman's Substack on 4/17/26. It is republished here with permission. Cliff 's Note: Friends, this article is the kind of writing that Blue Amp Media exists to promote: unabashed truth-telling in a time where there is so little truth out there. This article contains triggers--you may find it hard reading--but it's worth the read, regardless. We hope you'll support us by if you can't, that's OK. We get it. . We're trying to build something big and lasting here, where truth tellers of all kinds have a space at which they can raise their voices to guide us all to that freer and more perfect union that we all imagine we can be. --Cliff by Lawrence Winnerman, Blue Amp Media COO I was eight years old the first time a man decided my body was his to use. He was a stranger. Some old man in a JCPenney dressing room at a shopping mall. My mother didn't know him. Nobody knew him. We never caught him. I was a child, and I learned something that afternoon that no child should have to learn: that there are men in the world who see a smaller body and feel entitled to it, and there is an entire civilization prepared to look the other way. I'm fifty-six now. Five-ten, two-sixty, built like a lumberjack. Nobody looks at me and thinks victim. And yet. I'm an openly gay man. I bottom--and if that word makes you uncomfortable, good, stay with me, because your discomfort is a fraction of what I'm about to talk about. I know what toxic masculinity sounds like from the inside, because it pervades the gay community too--the hierarchy of tops over bottoms, the sorting of men into who penetrates and who receives, the quiet assumption that receiving makes you less. I've lived in that hierarchy my entire adult life. I know exactly how the gears turn. The same machinery that sorts men in a bedroom is the machinery that sorts women in a legislature, that sorts bodies in a culture, that produces--reliably, generationally--the system I'm about to describe. I have standing to make this case. And I mean that in a way that goes deeper than opinion. Eric Swalwell was arrested by no one. of them a twenty-year-old intern who says he raped her--and within a week he , abandoned his campaign for governor of California, and vanished into the fog of the next news cycle. By May it will be a footnote. I read the allegations and I didn't feel outrage. I'm past outrage. What I felt was recognition. The machine that produced what happened to me in that dressing room is the same machine that produced Eric Swalwell, the same machine that protected Jeffrey Epstein for decades, the same machine that right now--today, this week, while you're reading this--is grinding through legislatures and courtrooms and algorithm-fed rabbit holes to ensure that women, half the human species, remain controllable, available, and silent. So let me make a case. • • • Count 1: The Predator Class Every few months, another name surfaces. We perform the ritual--shock, denial, investigation, consequences for some, impunity for most--and then we move on, and we never once talk about the ecosystem that keeps producing the names. Eric Swalwell. Four women. Sexual misconduct including rape, sexual assault of intoxicated women, unsolicited explicit messages sent via Snapchat. One accuser started interning for him in 2019 when she was twenty years old--hadn't even graduated from college. The ; approximately forty-five minutes later, Swalwell announced his resignation. He was a Democrat. He sat on the Intelligence Committee. He went on cable news and talked about accountability. And here's the thing about Swalwell that nobody wants to say out loud: Democrats forced him out in a week. As they should have. Al Franken was gone in days. Anthony Weiner, gone. Katie Hill, gone. The left holds its own to account because that's what accountability looks like. But Matt Gaetz faced a sex trafficking investigation involving a seventeen-year-old and was nominated for Attorney General. Jim Jordan was accused of ignoring sexual abuse of wrestlers when he was an assistant coach at Ohio State and went on to chair the House Judiciary Committee. Donald Trump was found liable for sexual abuse by a civil jury and was elected president. Again. The right doesn't hold its people to account--not because it can't, but because it won't. And the result is a perverse optical illusion: Democratic names dominate the headlines about sexual misconduct because Democrats are the only ones generating consequences. Republican predators stay in office, stay quiet, and stay powerful. In the court of public opinion, it starts to look like the problem belongs to one party--when what's actually happening is that only one party treats it as a problem at all. The asymmetry isn't a bug. It's the strategy. Hold no one accountable, and you never have to explain anything. Let the other side do the right thing, and weaponize their integrity against them. Jeffrey Epstein. thousand videos, one hundred eighty thousand images. The Department of Justice acknowledged that six million pages may ultimately qualify for release. Communications between Epstein and a vast web of figures in politics, academia, and business continued after he registered as a sex offender. Both parties. All industries. A December 2025 poll found that 91 percent of Democrats, 78 percent of independents, and 74 percent of Republicans wanted the files released. The only people who didn't want sunlight were the people in the files. And then there's the one that should have stopped the world and didn't. In March 2026, into what they called a global "online rape academy"--a pornographic website called Motherless.com hosting more than twenty thousand videos of "sleep content," which is the sanitized term for footage of men drugging and raping their unconscious partners. A linked Telegram group called "Zzz" had nearly a thousand members actively teaching each other the craft of it. How to select the drug. How to dose it. How to avoid detection. A as a direct result of the reporting. A thousand men in a chat room, workshopping rape. Twenty thousand videos. Hundreds of thousands of views. Swalwell, Epstein, the men in that Telegram group--they are the visible tips of something that goes all the way down. A system that produces predators reliably, at scale, and then provides them with legal, cultural, and technological infrastructure to operate. The predator class isn't a conspiracy. It's an output. • • • Count 2: The Pipeline If you have a son under twenty-five, he is one algorithm away from the top of this funnel. I'm not being dramatic. That is what the research says. It starts with looksmaxxing--a trend in which young men use apps to score their facial attractiveness on the PSL scale, a rating system invented in incel communities that ranks faces using terms like "low-tier normie" and "chadlite." One looksmaxxing influencer alone racked up more than . The apps are in the App Store right now. Your kid can download one during lunch. And in case you think this is just vanity with a weird name: looksmaxxing communities actively encourage boys to strike themselves in the face with hammers--to fracture their own facial bones so the bones will, supposedly, grow back stronger and more masculine. Boys are hitting themselves in the face with hammers to meet a beauty standard invented by incels. That's where the pipeline starts. The Alan Turing Institute's Centre for Emerging Technology and Security published a study titled The title tells you everything. The pipeline works like this: a boy downloads an app that scores his face. The app uses language and frameworks created by incels. The algorithm feeds him more content. The content gets darker. He moves from TikTok to Discord to Telegram. The communities he finds there are actively directing young men toward incel ideology, neo-Nazi content, and material that glorifies mass shooters and suicide. All of this is documented. UK's communications regulator--published a sixty-five-page report on young people's engagement with the manosphere. Research in Wiley's Child and Adolescent Mental Health journal found the manosphere encouraging sexist attitudes in schools, deepening existing inequalities, spreading dangerous messages about mental health. The mapped what they call "modern sexism" from its digital roots to its violent endpoints. The pipeline is designed. It has an architecture. It has an on-ramp that looks like self-help and an off-ramp that looks like a manifesto. And every man who built it is counting on you--personally, specifically you--to assume your son is too smart to fall for it. • • • Count 3: The Legislative Assault In August 2025, of the Communion of Reformed Evangelical Churches, a growing denomination with approximately a hundred and fifty congregations--told CNN that the Nineteenth Amendment granting women the right to vote "was a bad idea." Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth . His Pentagon spokesman confirmed the affiliation and said Hegseth "very much appreciates many of Mr. Wilson's writings and teachings." Democratic congresswomen, several of them military veterans, . That's the Secretary of Defense of the United States, aligned with a church whose leader wants to repeal women's suffrage. And here's the part that should keep you up at night: another CREC pastor admitted the quiet part out loud. "I don't think Christians should make repealing the Nineteenth Amendment their main issue right now," he said, "because it's not a winning issue yet." Yet. The word "yet" is doing all the work in that sentence. The strategy is to make fringe ideas seem plausible enough that less extreme shifts toward theocracy and patriarchy seem reasonable by comparison. Move the Overton window so far that taking away a woman's right to vote sounds radical, but taking away her right to control her own body sounds like a compromise. Meanwhile, in state legislatures across the country, a study by the found that 11.85 percent of state legislators belonged to far-right Facebook groups. That small minority sponsored 66 percent of all anti-abortion bills and 62 percent of all anti-LGBTQ bills introduced in their legislative sessions. A fraction of the political class is driving the overwhelming majority of the legislative assault on bodily autonomy--and they're doing it in gerrymandered districts where their positions are wildly out of step with public opinion. And the economic data is unambiguous. Women in states with coverage, expanded Medicaid, public funding for medically necessary procedures--have higher earnings, face less occupational segregation, and are less likely to be trapped in part-time work. Every dollar spent on contraceptive services saves three dollars in pregnancy-related care. Reproductive access is the economic foundation on which women build independent lives. Strip it away and you strip away the independence. That's the point. That has always been the point. And then there's Heritage Foundation's nine-hundred-page blueprint for the next Republican administration--which proposes treating gender education as a sex offense and makes zero reference to the rates of violence targeting LGBTQ people. Nine hundred pages. They had room. They chose silence. • • • Count 4: The Structural Failure In April 2024, the New York Court of Appeals on a 4-3 vote. The technicality: testimony from "prior bad acts" witnesses shouldn't have been admitted because it served "only to establish defendant's propensity to commit the crimes charged." Sit with that for a second. A court ruled that evidence showing a rapist had a pattern of raping was too prejudicial to the rapist. Judge Madeline Singas wrote in dissent that the decision continued "a disturbing trend of overturning juries' guilty verdicts in cases involving sexual violence" and perpetuated "outdated notions of sexual violence" that "allow predators to escape accountability." In the retrial, Weinstein was convicted on one count, acquitted on another. Partial justice for a man dozens of women accused over decades. The system working exactly as it was designed to work. And look--between 2016 and 2022, state legislatures across the country introduced more than three thousand bills aimed at gender equity and workplace safety. They passed 382 of them. Pay equity, harassment training, reporting procedures, leave policies, elimination of the nondisclosure agreements that protect predators. Real progress, passed by real legislators who fought for every vote. The legal system still cannot reliably convict a rapist. Me Too changed the culture. It changed who we believe, how quickly we respond, what we're willing to say out loud. But it did not change the machine. The machine still runs. The courtrooms still fail. The statutes of limitations still expire. The burden of proof still falls on the body that was violated, not the body that did the violating. And every time a conviction gets overturned on a technicality, every man in that Telegram group, every lobbyist drafting an abortion ban, every influencer teaching boys to score women's faces on a numerical scale receives the same message: the system is yours. • • • Count 5: The Global Playbook Everything I've described so far has been American. The dysfunction, the polarization, the broken institutions. But the playbook is global, and if you don't see that, you're looking at the trees and missing the forest that's on fire. In November 2024, concluding that "regressive ideas on gender and gender equality can be a useful proxy metric for democratic backsliding and authoritarian rise." The United States government's own Women, Peace, and Security strategy recommends treating misogyny--online or codified in policy--as an early indicator of authoritarian consolidation. Read that again. Our own government's own framework says: when misogyny shows up in law, authoritarianism is coming. Every time. Without exception. Russia : Vladimir Putin positioned himself as the guardian of "traditional Christian values," told women their place was at home raising children, and systematically rolled back domestic violence protections. Turkey : Erdoğan withdrew from the Istanbul Convention on gender-based violence, reversed secular marriage requirements, and opened the legal door to underage marriages and polygamy. Hungary : Orban moved to ban abortion and restrict LGBTQ rights as cornerstones of his "illiberal democracy." Poland : the Law and Justice party packed courts with political allies, then used those compromised judges to eliminate abortion rights--against the clear will of the Polish majority. Brazil : Bolsonaro wasn't even particularly religious before his campaign, but evangelical churches powered his 2018 victory. Once in office, six of his twenty-four cabinet members were evangelical Protestants who restricted LGBTQ rights, banned gender discussions in classrooms, and cast doubt on the need for gender equality policies altogether. The in November 2024: patriarchy and authoritarianism are "mutually reinforcing political projects." Their research demonstrated that voters with sexist attitudes are significantly more likely to support authoritarian leaders--even after controlling for political ideology. A 2017 European study found that traditional gender views predicted support for radical-right parties across twenty-three countries. put it plainly: authoritarian leaders seek to "displace democracy with hierarchies controlled by male elites and to re-confine women in traditional roles." They minimize women's equal rights, frame equality as niche identity politics, and they do this because patriarchy is the structural foundation on which authoritarian power is built. The hierarchy of men over women is the load-bearing wall. Pull it out and the authoritarian project collapses. Which is why every one of these regimes came for women's rights first. Or simultaneously with the power grab. Never after. The sequence is the tell. • • • Count 6: The Foundation Now the data. And I need you to stay with me here, because this is where the floor drops out. studied the relationship between women's status and democratic health--free elections, free association, checks on government power--across dozens of countries and found them strongly correlated. The , spanning a hundred countries over four decades, demonstrated that women's political empowerment directly strengthens democratic participation. When women lead, cooperative governance increases, peacemaking improves, social welfare expands, economies grow. This is not ideology. This is the data set. The economics alone should end every argument anyone has ever had about this. for the global economy from closing gaps in women's health care, including reproductive health. One trillion. The UNFPA documented that every one-percentage-point increase in gender-based violence in sub-Saharan Africa correlates with up to an 8 percent decline in economic activity, driven primarily by drops in female employment. Intimate partner violence costs the global economy an estimated 5 percent of GDP--five trillion dollars, every year, hemorrhaging out of civilization like an open wound that we just keep walking past. The found that more than 40 percent of state legislators have experienced threats. Women legislators are three to four times more likely to face explicitly sexualized abuse. Women are being threatened out of public life. And the threats are working. And the men making the threats know the threats are working. And the rest of us are watching it happen. Women's freedom is the foundation on which democratic societies are built. Every piece of data confirms it. Every country that has tried to build democracy while constraining women has failed. Every authoritarian who has consolidated power has done so by attacking women's autonomy first. The correlation is so strong, so consistent, so thoroughly documented across decades of research and dozens of countries that to deny it at this point is to deny gravity. If women are not equally free--equally free, with the same control over their bodies and their futures and their participation in civic life--then this whole experiment in self-governance is bullshit. I mean that word precisely. I mean it as a factual assessment of the evidence I have just laid in front of you. Democracy that does not include the full freedom of women is a word with nothing behind it--a promise made to half the species and broken every single day. • • • The Verdict Six counts. Predators at the top. A radicalization pipeline feeding boys into the machine from the bottom. A legislative apparatus stripping rights in the middle. A legal system that fails to convict. A global playbook that repeats the same pattern on every continent. And a mountain of data proving that when you attack women's freedom, you are attacking democracy itself--at the foundation, at the load-bearing wall, at the thing without which the whole structure is a lie. I've made my case. Now I'm talking to men. Women already know all of this. They've been living it. They've been screaming it. They are exhausted from explaining it to men who nod sympathetically and go back to their lives. I am done watching that happen. I'm talking to you. The man reading this right now. The husband, the father, the brother, the son, the friend, the colleague, the guy who considers himself one of the good ones. Are you fighting for the women in your life? I don't mean performing allyship on social media. I don't mean the pin on your jacket or the post you shared that one time. Are you intervening when the joke gets told at dinner? Are you calling your state legislator when an abortion ban hits committee? Are you monitoring what your son is watching, who he's following, what pipeline is pulling him toward a chat room full of men who think consent is optional? Are you showing up at school board meetings? Are you voting in every single election--every one, including the ones that bore you--like your daughter's freedom depends on it? Because it does. Are you risking something? Losing something? Spending social capital, burning a friendship, making yourself uncomfortable in a room full of men who expect your silence? Because if you aren't doing those things, you aren't fighting. You're watching. And watching is a choice. I know what the machine does. I learned it when I was eight years old, in a dressing room in a JCPenney, from a man no one ever caught. I've lived inside the toxic masculinity that sorts men into hierarchies of dominance and submission and calls it natural order. And I am telling you--from inside the experience, from inside the evidence, from inside the cold fury that comes from knowing both at once--that the time for choosing sides is practically over. The forces arrayed against women's freedom are organized. They are funded. They are global, patient, and strategic. They have a legislative agenda and a judicial strategy. They have a radicalization pipeline that starts with a face-scoring app and ends with men in a chat room teaching each other how to rape. They have a Secretary of Defense who attends a church whose pastor wants to repeal women's suffrage. They have Project 2025. They have three million pages of Epstein files proving that the most powerful men in the world operated a sexual abuse network for decades with functional impunity. They have momentum. What do you have? If the answer is good intentions and a vague sense that things will work out--then I need you to hear me very clearly. You are a coward. You are a stupid, selfish, ugly, mean, small, weak, cowardly coward. You are watching a war--a real war, fought every single day on the bodies of women and girls and queer people--and you are choosing comfort over duty, passivity over action, your own ease over someone else's survival. History will judge you. Your daughters will judge you. And they will be right. Every generation plants the seeds of the fights the next generation will have to wage. The fights we will be having for the next hundred years are being seeded right now. In legislatures. In courtrooms. In algorithms. In churches. In chat rooms. In the silence of men who know better and do nothing. Pick up a weapon. It doesn't have to be a literal one--make it your voice, your vote, your time, your money, your willingness to be uncomfortable, your refusal to let one more joke slide, one more policy pass, one more boy disappear into the pipeline while you tell yourself it won't happen to your kid. The war is already here. It has been here. Women have been fighting it alone for long enough. What side are you on? Will you help us elect leaders who will stand up to MAGA extremism?

George Latimer is a lifelong Westchester resident who has built a career in public service by delivering unparalleled progressive results for local residents. Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/-Phg2RhDIEGJVPbb5vK87g2?unsubscribedata=RrJarkw08EoVhgYV9bOQTvyX%2F%2BorD0dUa9mnL%2FbNZD0Us8zR9y9XraS%2FQLkVu%2Fg0ysrFjNyqs%2FfZnQ94SPpkzgVAjhmkpIgsBpuqibfz3kMvhMYT1uZCaN38ImcWZj7nlJl64OINXmytQ6WZQ1TUKN7Si4ljV%2FZjgPJHDPiMFGNNPwU6yT2aKaY68PjlcCt%2F%2BhQ3lRcXbWGxaO8N%2BgecsLwCg5FiFiDqLr4KN6vaB%2FFyHmbkPuAHAtVxYzZkG8bL2ZyacWbF2Wq1ixaMYeafhDvJjd7amLMpy2afKqwdze8YT664MvwGsYAnJqJUDHg%2BSWQd%2BuxtqTbsfk0PUAtTKilo2phVququhIwXObgoygvRzKNLvgvZFCtbn%2B%2FvkcCcD31NfrDgtU%2FYR8iUg00RLwB0wCPlZGvB0LgjLUNmAnE35jL4yabXBlLaODrW9yLJ)

Peter, When I decided to run for Congress, it was for a simple reason: I believe public service is about showing up, listening, and delivering results for the people you represent. I’ve always believed that working and middle class families deserve a fair shot — a strong economy, good jobs, affordable healthcare, and a government that understands the pressures people feel in their daily lives. **These aren’t just political issues, they’re real conversations happening every day at kitchen tables like mine and, I imagine, yours too.** Unfortunately, the current Republican majority is more interested in partisan fights and political theater than in solving real problems for working families. That’s why in November we need to elect steady, responsible leaders focused on results — not extremism and chaos. **[So I have to ask: will you make a donation to my re-election campaign before our end-of-month fundraising deadline? Your support right now will help us take back the House majority from MAGA Republicans.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130217080/617011973/872870985?amount=10&source=GL_EM_FR_042626_B1_EOM1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQwMzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDFiMGEyZmItYWQ0MS1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=YdlwGLAVo9ZoX1cRqZpqICha6dhHBOtlwDeoWOSweoI=)** This is an important race, and we need to be prepared for what lies ahead. If we’re going to keep standing up for working families, and elect a Democratic majority, we need the resources to do it. My team and I are working every day to build the kind of campaign that can withstand Mike Johnson’s MAGA warchest and keep the focus where it belongs: on the people we serve. **[So can you pitch in $10, $25, or even $50 to help us meet our fundraising goal this month?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130217081/617011974/872870985?amount=10&source=GL_EM_FR_042626_B1_EOM1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQwMzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDFiMGEyZmItYWQ0MS1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=YdlwGLAVo9ZoX1cRqZpqICha6dhHBOtlwDeoWOSweoI=)** | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

For over three decades, George Latimer has been a champion for progressive policies and leaders, helping lead the effort to make Westchester, a formerly solid Republican County, into a blue firewall that has withstood multiple red waves in recent years. George’s unparalleled track record in advancing Democratic goals includes successfully cutting regressive taxes, fighting for civil rights and racial equality, defending a woman’s right to choose, standing up to the NRA to keep guns off our streets, and voting for marriage equality.

clean_body: Will you help us elect leaders who will stand up to MAGA extremism? George Latimer is a lifelong Westchester resident who has built a career in public service by delivering unparalleled progressive results for local residents. Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Peter, When I decided to run for Congress, it was for a simple reason: I believe public service is about showing up, listening, and delivering results for the people you represent. I’ve always believed that working and middle class families deserve a fair shot — a strong economy, good jobs, affordable healthcare, and a government that understands the pressures people feel in their daily lives. These aren’t just political issues, they’re real conversations happening every day at kitchen tables like mine and, I imagine, yours too. Unfortunately, the current Republican majority is more interested in partisan fights and political theater than in solving real problems for working families. That’s why in November we need to elect steady, responsible leaders focused on results — not extremism and chaos. = This is an important race, and we need to be prepared for what lies ahead. If we’re going to keep standing up for working families, and elect a Democratic majority, we need the resources to do it. My team and I are working every day to build the kind of campaign that can withstand Mike Johnson’s MAGA warchest and keep the focus where it belongs: on the people we serve. = | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you for your support, George Latimer * | | ---|--- = For over three decades, George Latimer has been a champion for progressive policies and leaders, helping lead the effort to make Westchester, a formerly solid Republican County, into a blue firewall that has withstood multiple red waves in recent years. George’s unparalleled track record in advancing Democratic goals includes successfully cutting regressive taxes, fighting for civil rights and racial equality, defending a woman’s right to choose, standing up to the NRA to keep guns off our streets, and voting for marriage equality. | = = Paid for by George Latimer for Congress George Latimer for Congress PO Box 789 White Plains, NY 10602 United States If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: You can also =.

name: Jamie Davis
subject: The farming accident that changed my life
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jamie Davis for U
body:
August 8th, 2009, the worst day of my life, changed how I see everything: from family to the importance of our rural communities and it is a key part of why I am running for office.
| | | | | [](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iPQxfeIIvqOMIMoX6czXiDV1hz4LE7kU55OIe35UYfXdl8ZOYSqrT4RR5K7CsGxTkwqmtWikC_aBX7IJ-CicgqmIxGJN5ergtXQbxppGF38PpkoNjMa30c3FEmmRT_o5lGv2dTVxujO_nK3nldmWnBvmWjb78XwzFhZ1Yn1nRsoU_5lwPTaPemf2uGNsT335lhg_IXGViPHBBsc_zXGEiss8ej9ZkiZt&c=p6fRba2IRhLzikx293fuQiLJw0gqiWhKvf8M5ReETBiCifLO8ylCdA==&ch=U8uGdtz9I5jvIYxUHsDk-giuDh2nLYDnPmWcSvtJNtDG_eDm2qzFPA==)
---
Peter, August 8, 2009, is a date I will never forget.
Just days before his 19th birthday, my son Justin was out in the fields with us, doing what this family has done for over three generations--farming the Louisiana delta. A breakdown in our combine left corn spilled across the ground, and as we worked to recover it, Justin got too close. In an instant, his pant leg was caught, and the thresher pulled him in.
I can still hear his voice: "Dad, get me out."
And there was nothing I could do.
---
| [](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iPQxfeIIvqOMIMoX6czXiDV1hz4LE7kU55OIe35UYfXdl8ZOYSqrT4RR5K7CsGxTkwqmtWikC_aBX7IJ-CicgqmIxGJN5ergtXQbxppGF38PpkoNjMa30c3FEmmRT_o5lGv2dTVxujO_nK3nldmWnBvmWjb78XwzFhZ1Yn1nRsoU_5lwPTaPemf2uGNsT335lhg_IXGViPFd1oyp4_Zm_hQtkkGRJDfy&c=p6fRba2IRhLzikx293fuQiLJw0gqiWhKvf8M5ReETBiCifLO8ylCdA==&ch=U8uGdtz9I5jvIYxUHsDk-giuDh2nLYDnPmWcSvtJNtDG_eDm2qzFPA==)
---
My son and I on the farm in 2021.
Multiple miracles occurred that day: When it initially happened he wasn't in pain because his nerves were pinched so tightly in the machine. Nor was he bleeding because he was caught so tight in there.
It took the jaws of life to tear apart that machine and when the metal finally gave way he let out a scream that I will never forget for the rest of my life.
The paramedics quickly sprang into action and he was airlifted to Jackson, Mississippi, where Doctors fought to save his leg, but the injury and infection from all the dirt and debris were too severe. Justin lost his leg from below the knee.
That day could have broken us. Instead, it showed me what makes Louisiana strong.
Neighbors, first responders from two parishes (what we call counties in Louisiana), and a doctor who wasn't even on duty, Dr. Ron Gregg, a farmer himself and longtime family friend -- showed up because that's what rural communities do. They take care of each other. They don't ask if it's their job or jurisdiction. They just show up.
Today, Justin is 35. He runs our farm. He's built a life, with a fiancee and a family of his own. He still hunts, fishes, and works the land -- just with a prosthetic leg and the same determination that carried him through that day.
But the truth is, the kind of community that saved Justin is disappearing. Rural hospitals are closing. Doctors like Dr. Gregg--who live in our communities, who know our families--are harder to find. Too many of our kids feel like they have to leave home to build a future.
And my family, who first worked this Louisiana delta soil as slaves and sharecroppers, and now as farmers who own this land, have put too much blood into this soil for us to leave and go somewhere else.
I have to take a stand and fight for our way of life and it's why I am proud to be the Democrat running for Senate in Louisiana.
If you believe in that fight, I'm asking you to stand with me today. A Democratic win here is the first step in taking our state back from the far-right whose policies of atomization and vicious corporatism are tearing our communities apart.
And mark my words, a win is possible, Democrats just won a deep red special election here by a landslide. [The blue wave will wash over Louisiana and we will flip this seat but only if we fight for it. Will you please make a contribution of $25, $10, or even just $5 to help us in our fight to restore the Louisiana that we love so dearly.](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iPQxfeIIvqOMIMoX6czXiDV1hz4LE7kU55OIe35UYfXdl8ZOYSqrT4RR5K7CsGxTgnh0e_jjRpkC7QER0DldD9iWjnJ5vPXmdJs1jv-b29UMughammxxazYg7Cvg9Yi8AFklSAzRJ-4S4HGStRQwsYb3mfXxhRcrdAtb5qd5VwxIx1If828bTqhiIIyi7PgDeyU7stwFgkHfynkeseZ6sHZUvGF7qK1ZM2_L4TEed1Q=&c=p6fRba2IRhLzikx293fuQiLJw0gqiWhKvf8M5ReETBiCifLO8ylCdA==&ch=U8uGdtz9I5jvIYxUHsDk-giuDh2nLYDnPmWcSvtJNtDG_eDm2qzFPA==)

| What To Expect This Week
---
|
---
| What man is a man, without the desire to make the world better?
I was elected to represent Virginia's Fifth Congressional District, and Tuesday's highly dishonest redistricting election does not change that. Thank you to RPV, committee members, all the volunteers, and everyone else who put in countless hours putting up signs, making phone calls, and more to help us get out the NO vote.
Tomorrow is a big day for Virginia. We expect the Virginia Supreme Court to do its constitutional duty and uphold the lower court ruling on the redistricting referendum as unconstitutional and illegal.
Until something changes, I will continue to campaign and represent the amazing people of the current Virginia's Fifth District. Send prayers for tomorrows Virginia Supreme Court Ruling.
It is an honor to serve you and your family. God bless America, and God bless our great Commonwealth.
This week will be historic:
---
|
Drone Defense: I am attending a tabletop exercise focused on countering Unmanned Aircraft Systems (drones), a rapidly evolving threat to our domestic and military installations.
Department of War: I will be participating in the hearing for Secretary of War Pete Hegseth to discuss the future of our military leadership and departmental priorities.
FISA Reform: We are back on the floor to vote on further reforms to FISA Section 702. My focus remains on protecting American civil liberties while ensuring our intelligence community has the tools to stop foreign threats.
###

Farm Bill: For the first time since 2018, we are voting on a full-scale Farm Bill. This is vital for our food security and provides the necessary certainty for our farmers, ranchers, and loggers. Agriculture and Forestry are the largest industry in Virginia with a combined annual impact of over $105 billion and supporting nearly 500,000 jobs, accounting for approximately one in every ten positions in the state.
DHS Funding: We are moving to vote on the Department of Homeland Security budget framework to bypass the partisan gridlock.
King Charles III Addresses Congress: This week, we will welcome His Majesty King Charles III for a Joint Meeting of Congress. This is a significant moment for the Special Relationship, marking the first time a British monarch has addressed Congress since Queen Elizabeth II in 1991.

## SPLC Promotes Hate And Division In America?
---
| | [](https://eky6fg9ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001IO9CN2mRkAgkoTX3CriERl7udBRrG-_UTqrnkyNyaDbwYFfVVTCPlN1D7LKHeaEV_MBn0enBYzeiHu1vQDF76naal4WZLaqvQ4lwgsZT4OmaqH3laBbsj3wRp-I_TEtDocDu0QjsmSdRp00pV9Rl3ed0mDcWet22ZBICAx0G-kTD8bRRctEfolEfnU-P2ru4&c=MzJdAwQ1t3JjqvA9A-ojWGJgj3BC859tColQJcWKoBRayoTzNY5u8g==&ch=uRf7CSqGPgTv33jV-eGXg3MJpDKhz-WECRecNe5QdGNf4LD10s7ykQ==)
---
| I read the indictment of the radical leftist non-profit SPLC with shock and disgust. The grand jury found their "donated money was being used to fund leaders and organizers of racist groups, including the Ku Klux Klan, the Aryan Nation, and the National Alliance." They also funded one of the organizers of the 2017 Unite the Right rally in Charlottesville where a woman died in my district, VA-05. Even though a grand jury has found probable cause for all of these allegations, I will let the legal process follow its course. If these allegations are true, it means SPLC was raising money by promoting hate and divide in our nation. Read the link to the indictment below for yourself and notice the part that says they were paying a man to spread hate while using his image on their website to raise money to stop hate. At the same time putting conservative groups like the Family Research Council and Turning Point USA on their hate map. This allegedly inspired at least one person to pull out a gun and shoot. It looks like they knew what they were doing and found a way to raise money and demonize their political opponents by lumping them in with white supremacy groups. This dangerous divide in America apparently paid for and orchestrated by the left has been destroying our country. It must stop and if proven true, all involved need to go to jail. SPLC must lose their tax exempt status immediately. [Click here.](https://eky6fg9ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001IO9CN2mRkAgkoTX3CriERl7udBRrG-_UTqrnkyNyaDbwYFfVVTCPlN1D7LKHeaEV_MBn0enBYzeiHu1vQDF76naal4WZLaqvQ4lwgsZT4OmaqH3laBbsj3wRp-I_TEtDocDu0QjsmSdRp00pV9Rl3ed0mDcWet22ZBICAx0G-kQsJMSaSQPobHdg3e4lcB4o&c=MzJdAwQ1t3JjqvA9A-ojWGJgj3BC859tColQJcWKoBRayoTzNY5u8g==&ch=uRf7CSqGPgTv33jV-eGXg3MJpDKhz-WECRecNe5QdGNf4LD10s7ykQ==)
---
### Was Jason Kessler the Paid Organizer?
---
|
---
### How does a staunch Barrack Obama and Occupy Wall Street supporter pivot to White Nationalism?

The indictment specifies that one informant was a member of the "online leadership chat group" that planned the 2017 "Unite the Right" rally in Charlottesville, VA. According to the indictment:

* They allegedly made racist posts under SPLC supervision.

* They helped coordinate transportation for several attendees to the Charlottesville event.

Kessler has admitted to voting for Obama and participating in leftist activism before his sudden "pivot" to white nationalism around 2016. However, at this time, the DOJ has not officially named the informant, and Kessler has not publicly confirmed being a paid source for the SPLC.
While the indictment refers to this individual as an anonymous, I and others wonder if the "field source" is the most notable organizer of Unite the Right Jason Kessler. However, at this time we don't know if he is the SPLC paid operative. If he is not, that means two of the organizers are tied to liberal organizations or ideals. This investigation could fundamentally undermine everything the lefts keeps saying about the rally in Charlottesville and SPLC.
