clean_body: | | | | == == | What To Expect This Week | | What man is a man, without the desire to make the world better? I was elected to represent Virginia's Fifth Congressional District, and Tuesday's highly dishonest redistricting election does not change that. Thank you to RPV, committee members, all the volunteers, and everyone else who put in countless hours putting up signs, making phone calls, and more to help us get out the NO vote. Tomorrow is a big day for Virginia. We expect the Virginia Supreme Court to do its constitutional duty and uphold the lower court ruling on the redistricting referendum as unconstitutional and illegal. Until something changes, I will continue to campaign and represent the amazing people of the current Virginia's Fifth District. Send prayers for tomorrows Virginia Supreme Court Ruling. It is an honor to serve you and your family. God bless America, and God bless our great Commonwealth. This week will be historic: | Drone Defense: I am attending a tabletop exercise focused on countering Unmanned Aircraft Systems (drones), a rapidly evolving threat to our domestic and military installations. Department of War: I will be participating in the hearing for Secretary of War Pete Hegseth to discuss the future of our military leadership and departmental priorities. FISA Reform: We are back on the floor to vote on further reforms to FISA Section 702. My focus remains on protecting American civil liberties while ensuring our intelligence community has the tools to stop foreign threats. Farm Bill: For the first time since 2018, we are voting on a full-scale Farm Bill. This is vital for our food security and provides the necessary certainty for our farmers, ranchers, and loggers. Agriculture and Forestry are the largest industry in Virginia with a combined annual impact of over $105 billion and supporting nearly 500,000 jobs, accounting for approximately one in every ten positions in the state. DHS Funding: We are moving to vote on the Department of Homeland Security budget framework to bypass the partisan gridlock. King Charles III Addresses Congress: This week, we will welcome His Majesty King Charles III for a Joint Meeting of Congress. This is a significant moment for the Special Relationship, marking the first time a British monarch has addressed Congress since Queen Elizabeth II in 1991. | Third Assassination Attempt | | | God bless the brave men and women in the Secret Service and other law enforcement units. Thank God President Trump and everyone else is OK! | Build The Ball room Now | | | Last nights assassination attempt is further evidence of why we need to build the new Ball Room at the White House now. | SPLC Promotes Hate And Division In America? | | == | I read the indictment of the radical leftist non-profit SPLC with shock and disgust. The grand jury found their "donated money was being used to fund leaders and organizers of racist groups, including the Ku Klux Klan, the Aryan Nation, and the National Alliance." They also funded one of the organizers of the 2017 Unite the Right rally in Charlottesville where a woman died in my district, VA-05. Even though a grand jury has found probable cause for all of these allegations, I will let the legal process follow its course. If these allegations are true, it means SPLC was raising money by promoting hate and divide in our nation. Read the link to the indictment below for yourself and notice the part that says they were paying a man to spread hate while using his image on their website to raise money to stop hate. At the same time putting conservative groups like the Family Research Council and Turning Point USA on their hate map. This allegedly inspired at least one person to pull out a gun and shoot. It looks like they knew what they were doing and found a way to raise money and demonize their political opponents by lumping them in with white supremacy groups. This dangerous divide in America apparently paid for and orchestrated by the left has been destroying our country. It must stop and if proven true, all involved need to go to jail. SPLC must lose their tax exempt status immediately. == Was Jason Kessler the Paid Organizer? | How does a staunch Barrack Obama and Occupy Wall Street supporter pivot to White Nationalism? The indictment specifies that one informant was a member of the "online leadership chat group" that planned the 2017 "Unite the Right" rally in Charlottesville, VA. According to the indictment: They allegedly made racist posts under SPLC supervision. They helped coordinate transportation for several attendees to the Charlottesville event. Kessler has admitted to voting for Obama and participating in leftist activism before his sudden "pivot" to white nationalism around 2016. However, at this time, the DOJ has not officially named the informant, and Kessler has not publicly confirmed being a paid source for the SPLC. While the indictment refers to this individual as an anonymous, I and others wonder if the "field source" is the most notable organizer of Unite the Right Jason Kessler. However, at this time we don't know if he is the SPLC paid operative. If he is not, that means two of the organizers are tied to liberal organizations or ideals. This investigation could fundamentally undermine everything the lefts keeps saying about the rally in Charlottesville and SPLC. | Conservatism With Results | | == The American people want a representative who works hard, shares their values, and works across the aisle to gets things done for their constituents. In the General Assembly our hard work led to results, Policy Maker of the Year, and Legislator of the year. Getting results for the people with a great team now continues in congress. == | Endorsements: | Congressman Jack Bergman | | | I am deeply honored to receive the endorsement of my friend and fellow warrior, retired Marine Corps Lieutenant General and Congressman Jack Bergman. Serving together on the House Armed Services Committee, we remain steadfast in our shared mission: strengthening our national defense and ensuring our veterans receive the unwavering support they deserve. | Congressman Jim Jordan | | | I am deeply honored to receive the endorsement of my friend, Judiciary Committee Chairman and fellow Oversight Committee member Jim Jordan, for my reelection to Congress. His support is a testament to our shared commitment to America First and the American people. | Navy SEAL Brother Jason Redman Endorsement | | | It is an honor to be endorsed by warrior and Navy SEAL brother Jason Redman. Jason is all grit and never backs down, even when he was shot serving our nation. My Brother and Louisa County Commonwealth Attorney | I'm deeply honored to have the endorsement of sheriffs and prosecutors who work every day to keep our communities safe. It means even more when that support comes from family. Thank you to my brother, Louisa County Commonwealth's Attorney and Brigadier General Rusty McGuire, for the endorsement and unwavering support of my office. Hanover County Commonwealth Attorney | Mackenzie is doing a great job ensuring justice is served in Hanover County. Thank you for the endorsement! Appomattox County Commonwealth Attorney | | | Thank you, Leslie, for all you are doing for the great folks in Appomattox County. | Nelson County Commonwealth Attorney | | | I appreciate the endorsement, Daniel! Thanks for all you do for the good people of Nelson County. Pittsylvania County Sheriff Endorsement | | Thank you for your support, Sheriff Taylor. You're doing a great job protecting the people of Pittsylvania County. | Goochland County Sheriff Endorsement | Thank you, Sheriff Creasey, for how hard you work to keep Goochland County safe. Your efforts do not go unnoticed. Mecklenburg County Sheriff Endorsement | Thank you for the endorsement Sheriff Hawkins! You are doing an incredible job protecting the good people of Mecklenburg County. Buckingham County Sheriff Endorsement | Thank you, Sheriff Kidd, for keeping Buckingham County safe and for the endorsement! Chesterfield County Sheriff Endorsement | Thank you, Sheriff Leonard for protecting the great people of Chesterfield County, and for the endorsement. Charlotte County Sheriff Endorsement | Thank you, Sheriff Grissom, for your support. Fluvanna County Sheriff Endorsement | While I work for you in the Fifth District and in D.C., our incredible Sheriffs are keeping our communities safe. It is an honor to have their endorsement. Thank you, Sheriff Hess for your support. Lynchburg City Commonwealth Attorney | It is an honor to receive an endorsement from someone so respected in the Lynchburg community. Thank you, Bethany! Lynchburg City Sheriff Endorsement | Thank you, Sheriff Sloan, for keeping Lynchburg safe and helping us spread the word to vote NO. I appreciate the endorsement and your help in defeating the referendum. Appomattox County Sheriff Endorsement | Thank you, Sheriff Richardson, for the endorsement and everything you do to protect your community. I promise to keep working for you and the people of the Fifth District when re-elected. Louisa County Sheriff Endorsement | | Thank you, Sheriff Lowe, for keeping everyone in Louisa County safe. | Campbell County Sheriff Endorsement | | | Thank you, Campbell County Sheriff Clark, for your unwavering support and friendship. | Amherst County Sheriff Endorsement | | | Sheriff Ayers is doing an incredible job protecting the people of Amherst County. Thank you for your hard work and the endorsement. | Amelia County Sheriff Endorsement | | | Thank you, Sheriff Walker, for everything you are doing to protect people in Amelia County. | The Bait And Switch Governor | | | Why do you think Governor Spanberger has the worst approval rating of any Virginia governor? She said she was a moderate. She lied about redistricting. She lied about making Virginia more affordable. She lied about eliminating the car tax. She lied about standing up to unions. She lied about not coming after gun owners. Democrats in Richmond proposed over 50 new taxes and voted to give themselves a 300 % pay raise. I guess she ran on affordability for them not you. Policies like boys in girls bathrooms, divisive and marxist DEI, expensive green energy, and more make life more difficult and less affordable. Do you think our governor is trying to out California California? Affordability | During the four years of the Democrat Biden Open Border Policies, we estimate 15 to 20 million illegal criminal aliens crossed our border illegally. The cartels have been having their way with innocent American Citizens. In addition to robbing, raping, and murdering our fellow Americans like Laken Riley, the sheer number of illegals has also overwhelmed our resources and increased the cost of living for all of us. Thank God President Trump, Tom Homan, and others are putting Americans first and doing something about it. Why have home prices gone up? Illegals. Why has my car insurance gone up? Illegals. Why has my home insurance gone up? Illegals. Why have my food prices gone up? Illegals. Why hasn't my salary gone up? Illegals. Get it yet? | Vote Ramona Christian Next VA-05 Committee Chair | | | Ramona Christian holds a degree in political science and has been a steadfast leader in the conservative movement. She served on Donald Trump's platform team, was one of just three Fifth District delegates selected to represent President Trump at the Republican National Convention, and she played a key role on the Trump Virginia campaign team, helping organize Trump rallies across the Commonwealth last year. She also serves as Political Director for Congressman John McGuire and she currently serves as the Buckingham County GOP Chair. Ramona is a no-nonsense leader, a trusted friend, and someone who absolutely will bring people together and get things done. She has my complete and total endorsement for Virginia's 5th Congressional District Committee Chair. To learn more including how to vote for her and to join her team, email Ramona at ramonachristian862@gmail.com | I Love CVille Podcast | I Love CVille with Jerry Miller. Thank you for having me on your show. We've received a lot of great feedback! == | If you want to join Team McGuire and help us help Trump Save America, please contact Ramona Christian at ramona@mcguireva.com or call her at 434 391 4546. Take care, please tell a friend to vote, and we look forward to seeing you around the district. | For God and Country, | Congressman John McGuire VA 05 Soli Deo Gloria Jesus is my Savior Husband Father Navy SEAL Business Owner -Trump Endorsed -NRA Endorsed -Susan B. Anthony's List Endorsed -RJC Endorsed Pro-Life Pro-Gun American Legion Member VFW (Life Member) NRA (Life Member) Highest Award for Conservatism (ACU/ CPAC) Family Foundation 100 VCDL 100 (Life Member) America First The Spirit of Virginia == our Facebook Page Twitter: @JohnMcGuire4VA Instagram: JohnMcGuire4VA == == john@mcguireva.com www.mcguireva.com | | | | == | | == | == | == == == | Paid for and Authorized by McGuire for Virginia | | | | McGuire for Virginia | PO Box 242 | Manakin Sabot, VA 23103 US | | | | | | |

name: Jen Kiggans
subject: I won one of the most competitive races in the country!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kiggans for Congress
body:
Now, the Democrats are out for blood to take this seat back from me!

Peter,

In 2022, we shocked the country when I defeated one of Nancy Pelosi's most loyal Democrats in one of the closest elections in the entire country...[](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews._NSGPwPUFL2WmqtIQ)Fast forward to 2024, and we did it again! We won another tight, must-win race and helped defend our Republican majority in the House. I'm proud of what we've accomplished, but the Democrats were even angrier and pissed off this time.
But **2026 is going to be the most brutal fight yet, and I 've got some news you need to know about:**
[Elaine Luria, the Democrat I defeated in 2022, is running against me again in 2026. ](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews._75FrYAL66Ow)
The Democrats are **_desperate_** to flip this seat blue, and they'll stop at nothing to take me down. [They know that the only way to retake the House Majority is if I lose. ](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.--jlK2wJ_Ftsg)
**But I'm not intimidated.** We beat Luria before, and we can do it again. I'm counting on your support to help us build a strong campaign and spread our conservative message.
Chip in today to support my campaign. Every dollar will go directly toward our grassroots efforts to defeat the Democrats for good.[CHIP IN $100
](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.)[CHIP IN $50
](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.)[CHIP IN $25

](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews._uOGG38dKjrwtgVOw)Help me send a strong message: we will not back down.
Sincerely,
Jen Kiggans
[](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.)I would not be where I am today without the help of my incredible and faithful supporters like you. Because of your support, I can keep fighting to make our country stronger.

Together we will get our country back on the right track and secure America's success! [SUPPORT JEN KIGGANS

name: Newt Gingrich
subject: This is where Democrats think they can win
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Moody for Florida
body:
I’ve learned how to recognize when control of the Senate is truly on the line – and I believe we are at one of those moments now.

| | | | Friend, I’ve won a few elections in my day, and I’ve spent decades watching battles for control of Congress. I’ve learned how to recognize when control of the Senate is truly on the line – and I believe we are at one of those moments now. **I’ve zeroed in on Florida as the one state that could decide everything.** What’s unfolding there is not getting nearly enough attention — but it should be setting off alarm bells for every conservative paying close attention. _**I’ve put together a confidential briefing explaining exactly why this race is so critical, and what Democrats are already doing behind the scenes to exploit it.**_ [**Please give it a read ASAP:**](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkMFqhDAQhp8m3pTsROPuIYfSRehhe2yPkk1GnVYTmUSXvn2xpQ_Q28B8fHz877f-ZmmmML7aBY3bqefU54kSpceEjB6X6NjmlCcKnz1I0KWsS9AHB4U3zmsFBZpT27agWgAoJnNuvD4PdrDny-WO3lndaGnrVmpv3WloCjKHSdag4SQlyKpRaO_34eLQeakciFpuq7cZU-ViSMi7zbQjhRCPK4bKxaWYzZTzmoR6EtAJ6BK6jbF6UGD0ByGgW2L0X-UQuRzmyOStgG6kMDK5qfx9fkQKuV_LNa5CdVte-uyEuj6_vZR_mQI0H3KmMAp1zbxhwcavD5pnSroVtRwXS_NPVv7_kLuB7wAAAP__WO2Eag)

---
Republicans have a STRONG candidate running in Florida – but this isn’t a typical race. It’s a special election to fill Marco Rubio’s old seat, and that changes everything. **Democrats have been quietly overperforming in these races, even in areas Republicans should be dominating.** They see an opportunity in Florida, and they are going all-in. If they succeed, the consequences won’t be limited to one seat. It could hand them the power to block President Trump’s nominees, stall his agenda, and grind progress in Washington to a halt. [**That’s why I need you to read this very CAREFULLY, and very QUICKLY.**](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkMFqhDAQhp8m3pTsROPuIYfSRehhe2yPkk1GnVYTmUSXvn2xpQ_Q28B8fHz877f-ZmmmML7aBY3bqefU54kSpceEjB6X6NjmlCcKnz1I0KWsS9AHB4U3zmsFBZpT27agWgAoJnNuvD4PdrDny-WO3lndaGnrVmpv3WloCjKHSdag4SQlyKpRaO_34eLQeakciFpuq7cZU-ViSMi7zbQjhRCPK4bKxaWYzZTzmoR6EtAJ6BK6jbF6UGD0ByGgW2L0X-UQuRzmyOStgG6kMDK5qfx9fkQKuV_LNa5CdVte-uyEuj6_vZR_mQI0H3KmMAp1zbxhwcavD5pnSroVtRwXS_NPVv7_kLuB7wAAAP__WO2Eag) Because once you see the full picture, you’ll understand why this race may be the lynchpin for the entire Senate majority. If you wait to act, it could already be too late. Thank you, Newt Gingrich

name: Audrey Denney
subject: The truth is, most people in D.C. don’t remember what normal life is like
body:
Northern California deserves a representative who has actually lived the lives of the people they serve. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130217875/617014597/-1589313525?refcode=WUEFHP82_20260426_AudreyDenneySplit_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1058487&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjNiZDVlYS1iMjQxLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mDN_yi75W4hQ6Q6u8stLcaZqf9rQUSW8h9IKg7QiYoM=) Peter,

I’ve spent my life working, from waiting tables in Chico to teaching our next generation of farmers and educators. I know what it’s like to worry about the cost of groceries and wonder if the people representing us in D.C. even remember what life is like for the rest of us.

The truth is, they don't.

Most of them are more worried about their next corporate fundraiser than they are about the families in our district struggling with health care costs. They don't understand the pressure of living paycheck to paycheck.

**I’m running because I believe Northern California deserves a representative who has actually lived the lives of the people they serve. But to get a working-class fighter to Congress, I need the working class behind me.**

******[With the election right around the corner, your $5 or $25 makes it possible for us to compete with the deep pockets of the establishment. Let's show them what we're made of! name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: how we win young conservatives
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240812%2E444211%2ER2SXhd)
In the last few weeks volunteering on college campuses I’ve been approached by young conservative men who want to talk about what’s happening in the country and how they’re thinking about the upcoming midterm election.
In Edinburg, Texas a young man told me that although he’s conservative, he doesn’t support Trump’s war of choice in Iran. He wonders what happened to putting America first. **[You can watch part of my conversation with him here:](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/67651?t=3&akid=240812%2E444211%2ER2SXhd)**
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/67651?t=4&akid=240812%2E444211%2ER2SXhd)
In Arlington, I met a young man who wanted to tell me that he had always considered himself opposed to me and to Democrats generally but that in the past year he’s come to realize that we represented a better future for him than Trump and the MAGA movement. He said that he grew up in a conservative home and now that he was thinking for himself “I want to be part of a movement that cares about people and chooses kindness over cruelty.” He also mentioned he’d had enough of the corruption from the current administration.
And in El Paso we met a student who said he’d voted for Trump in 2024 “because of crypto” and America First rhetoric but now regretted his decision. He said that Trump hadn’t followed through on his promises and that he’s increasingly open to voting for Democrats.
In all three cases, we were able to register or confirm the registration of these young voters after having conversations that focused on dialog instead of judgement. And we will personally stay in touch with them throughout this election season, as we do with tens of thousands of other young voters, to make it more likely that they vote and that they vote for change.
It’s the same approach we take with true-Blue voters, independents and anyone else who wants something better for this country and is willing to vote for it. We need 'em all!
By being open to everyone and writing no one off, we have a chance to help Democrats win historic victories in Texas in 2026.
[**Pitch in to make sure we can keep up the work across Texas over these next six months, one conversation at a time!**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260426.iDhLI5&refcode=ema20260426-240812&t=5&akid=240812%2E444211%2ER2SXhd)
**[Donate $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260426.iDhLI5&refcode=ema20260426-240812&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=24&akid=240812%2E444211%2ER2SXhd)**
Beto

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20260426-240812-footer&t=25&refcode2=240812_444211_R2SXhd&akid=240812%2E444211%2ER2SXhd)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.

clean_body: In the last few weeks volunteering on college campuses I’ve been approached by young conservative men who want to talk about what’s happening in the country and how they’re thinking about the upcoming midterm election. In Edinburg, Texas a young man told me that although he’s conservative, he doesn’t support Trump’s war of choice in Iran. He wonders what happened to putting America first. In Arlington, I met a young man who wanted to tell me that he had always considered himself opposed to me and to Democrats generally but that in the past year he’s come to realize that we represented a better future for him than Trump and the MAGA movement. He said that he grew up in a conservative home and now that he was thinking for himself “I want to be part of a movement that cares about people and chooses kindness over cruelty.” He also mentioned he’d had enough of the corruption from the current administration. And in El Paso we met a student who said he’d voted for Trump in 2024 “because of crypto” and America First rhetoric but now regretted his decision. He said that Trump hadn’t followed through on his promises and that he’s increasingly open to voting for Democrats. In all three cases, we were able to register or confirm the registration of these young voters after having conversations that focused on dialog instead of judgement. And we will personally stay in touch with them throughout this election season, as we do with tens of thousands of other young voters, to make it more likely that they vote and that they vote for change. It’s the same approach we take with true-Blue voters, independents and anyone else who wants something better for this country and is willing to vote for it. We need 'em all! By being open to everyone and writing no one off, we have a chance to help Democrats win historic victories in Texas in 2026. Beto

name: Tim Walz
subject: Big news:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| I have some big news. I’m launching something new, and I want you to be a part of it from the jump.

I grew up in the kind of small town where everybody knew everybody, everybody’s kids played together, and everybody helped when someone was in trouble.

We used to gather together as neighbors to celebrate after football games and graduations. More often than not, our gatherings took place at our local public school.

My public school was foundational. It was more than my education and the name on my jersey: it was the place our town came to meet. _Heck, the school was the town._

As I’ve visited small towns in other states over the years, I’ve witnessed a similar sentiment. In red districts and blue, I’ve heard about cherished local schools and how they serve as the cornerstones of their communities.

But nowadays I’m seeing a disturbing new trend as those cornerstones are starting to crumble: **Local schools are closing after funding cuts, and they’re followed by other foundational pieces of the community.**

The local grocery store? Gone. The local clinic? _Gone._ The job with a decent wage? Gone.

Everyone and everything seems to be packing up and leaving town. We’re losing our neighbors. We’re losing our communities. And we aren't talking to each other anymore.

This is what it looks like to defund and divide America, and it’s because of Republicans like Donald Trump and JD Vance, who are willing to sell out our communities and working families for their own political and financial gain.

Why is Elon Musk closing schools in America? Why are we being told that any of the hardships our communities are facing have to do with immigration? The problem isn't your neighbor. It’s the tech bros and billionaires who pay to take turns cosplaying as your Congressman, your Senator, and your President.

I think folks in small towns need to band together and stand up for our schools, our jobs, and our communities before they’re all gone.

**It’s time to put the power back into the hands of our neighbors, and for Democrats to expand the map. That’s why I’m excited to launch Small Town PAC.**

I’m ready to pass the baton and get out on the road to find some teachers, some nurses, some veterans, laborers, and young people in small towns across the country who can represent their communities better than Silicon Valley and corrupt Republicans.

We need a new generation of elected leaders in this country – and lord knows the Democrats need some new faces. So let’s go recruit some good people with energy and ideas who actually understand small towns and are willing to fight for them.

We’re on a mission to build a party that invests in small towns and delivers a message that resonates with rural America.

[If you believe in putting small towns back at the forefront of American politics, please split a donation between Small Town PAC and Voter Protection Project to become a founding donor today.](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108133?t=6&akid=23947%2E878179%2EN4CWX1) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Small Town PAC and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108134?t=7&akid=23947%2E878179%2EN4CWX1)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108135?t=8&akid=23947%2E878179%2EN4CWX1)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108136?t=9&akid=23947%2E878179%2EN4CWX1)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108137?t=10&akid=23947%2E878179%2EN4CWX1)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/108133?t=11&akid=23947%2E878179%2EN4CWX1)
Let’s start talking to each other again about the issues that actually impact us. Let’s start taking the fight to the venture capitalists and robber barons who are ripping our communities apart. And let’s go win some elections!

I’m grateful to kick off this new journey with you.

More soon,

Tim

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Might be decided very soon
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Mother Jones: Republican Justices Appear Poised to Destroy What’s Left of the Voting Rights Act [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=042626_2&amount=25]
We'll hand this straight to Mother Jones, Team: " The Supreme Court, with its six-to-three Republican-appointed majority, appears ready to kneecap what remains of the Voting Rights Act’s protections for minority political representation. "
The descriptive language seems harsh, but the objective of these justices seems clear -- a complete dismantling of our seminal civil rights law . We've said it before and will say it again, but it's been clear long before the MAGA movement that Chief Justice Roberts has seen the removal of the VRA as one of his top goals for his time on the Court. It appears he may be on the cusp of succeeding.
During recent oral arguments in Louisiana v. Callais , at least five conservative justices seemed ready to handicap the VRA so that it will no longer stop white majorities from locking racial minorities out of elected office through racial gerrymandering.
The consequences are simple to understand and grave for voter participation. The harder we make it to vote, the more politicians make voter participation feel worthless, the less people actually take part. Needless to say, the less people who participate, the less our democracy and our government looks like the people it's supposed to represent.
Team: We have to spread the word about this case and the consequences of dismantling the Voting Rights Act. Fuel our work with $35 or whatever you can today and help us defend the right to vote >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=042626_2&amount=35]
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042626_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042626_2&amount=25]
GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042626_2&amount=50] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042626_2&amount=100]
GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=042626_2&amount=250] Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=042626_2&amount=35]
At issue in the case is Section 2 of the VRA, which requires that racial minorities have an equal opportunity to meaningfully participate in the electoral process. This provision has been used to strike down redistricting schemes and maps that prevented Black voters and other racial minorities from electing their preferred representatives. Since 1965, Section 2 has given people of color a seat at the table, from school boards to the halls of Congress. It appears this 60-year era is coming to an end.
“ Race is a part of redistricting always ,” Justice Sonia Sotomayor said, addressing the solicitor general from Louisiana, who was arguing against an existing map that had enabled the election of two Black members of Congress in his state. “ What you’re saying to us [is]...‘You can use [race] to help yourself achieve goals that reduce particular groups’ electoral participation, but you can’t use it to remedy that situation .’”
Striking down the last remnants of the Voting Rights Act is exactly why the Supreme Court held this case over from last year. Gutting Section 2 would take us backwards in time and allow racial gerrymandering to be a standard practice, silencing more voters. Just like the redistricting fights across the country, this is all about whether the party in power can choose their voters in order to stay in power in perpetuity.
We simply can't afford to go backwards like this. Will you chip in with $35 or more to help us fight back against these partisan efforts to silence voters? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=042626_2&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=042626_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=042626_2&amount=20]

clean_body: Voter Action Project [] Mother Jones: Republican Justices Appear Poised to Destroy What’s Left of the Voting Rights Act [] We'll hand this straight to Mother Jones, Team: " The Supreme Court, with its six-to-three Republican-appointed majority, appears ready to kneecap what remains of the Voting Rights Act’s protections for minority political representation. " The descriptive language seems harsh, but the objective of these justices seems clear -- a complete dismantling of our seminal civil rights law . We've said it before and will say it again, but it's been clear long before the MAGA movement that Chief Justice Roberts has seen the removal of the VRA as one of his top goals for his time on the Court. It appears he may be on the cusp of succeeding. During recent oral arguments in Louisiana v. Callais , at least five conservative justices seemed ready to handicap the VRA so that it will no longer stop white majorities from locking racial minorities out of elected office through racial gerrymandering. The consequences are simple to understand and grave for voter participation. The harder we make it to vote, the more politicians make voter participation feel worthless, the less people actually take part. Needless to say, the less people who participate, the less our democracy and our government looks like the people it's supposed to represent. Team: We have to spread the word about this case and the consequences of dismantling the Voting Rights Act. Fuel our work with $35 or whatever you can today and help us defend the right to vote >> [] GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [] Other Amount [] At issue in the case is Section 2 of the VRA, which requires that racial minorities have an equal opportunity to meaningfully participate in the electoral process. This provision has been used to strike down redistricting schemes and maps that prevented Black voters and other racial minorities from electing their preferred representatives. Since 1965, Section 2 has given people of color a seat at the table, from school boards to the halls of Congress. It appears this 60-year era is coming to an end. “ Race is a part of redistricting always ,” Justice Sonia Sotomayor said, addressing the solicitor general from Louisiana, who was arguing against an existing map that had enabled the election of two Black members of Congress in his state. “ What you’re saying to us [is]...‘You can use [race] to help yourself achieve goals that reduce particular groups’ electoral participation, but you can’t use it to remedy that situation .’” Striking down the last remnants of the Voting Rights Act is exactly why the Supreme Court held this case over from last year. Gutting Section 2 would take us backwards in time and allow racial gerrymandering to be a standard practice, silencing more voters. Just like the redistricting fights across the country, this is all about whether the party in power can choose their voters in order to stay in power in perpetuity. We simply can't afford to go backwards like this. Will you chip in with $35 or more to help us fight back against these partisan efforts to silence voters? >> [] Voter Action Project [] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [] Donate Now [] Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax-deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003. Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Integrity First Voter Action Project P.O. Box 19294 Portland, OR 97280 United States

body:
#

Friend —
If you knew chipping in $3 meant Mark would have everything he needed to hit his goals before his end-of-month deadline this week, would you donate?
If that $3 could help Mark mobilize voters in every state of this nation to turn out in November and defeat enough Republicans to flip Congress, wouldn’t you give that $3 right away?
How about this: What if that $3 could get us even one step closer to ending the chaos and bringing some sanity and stability back to our politics? Wouldn’t that be some of the best money you ever spent?
$3 doesn’t sound like the kind of money that can do all of that on its own, and we know that. But in the last few minutes alone, dozens of people have already chipped in what they could to help Mark out today.
Now, they’re hoping you’ll do the same.

Higher prices at the grocery store
2\. Less meat available That's why meat must be a vital part of your survival food stockpile. But there is a catch. Not all protein is created equal. And this is so critical to understand, especially in a crisis. See, your protein needs to meet 4 critical requirements: It needs to:
* Last a while
* Taste great
* Store without refrigeration
* Can't break the bank

**Spoiler alert: most protein options fall short!** Take peanut butter. Sure, most people see it as a survival food protein staple. But did you know most cans are full of added sugars? Plus, they don't last more than a few months. A year tops. Grocery store meat doesn't work either. First, it's expensive. If you've been to the grocery store recently, you know what I'm talking about. Meat prices are up big time over the past year. But it also needs to be refrigerated. So if a blackout hits and cuts power to your home, you might as well use it as a paperweight. But don't worry friend. **There is a protein option that checks all four boxes:**
* This option is guaranteed to last at least 5 years.
* It doesn't need to be refrigerated.
* And it come with an easy payment plan so it won't break the bank.
* And most importantly, it tastes delicious!

Dozens of Democratic senators are sounding the alarm on a dangerous new executive order from Donald Trump – one that could give the federal government unprecedented power over mail-in voting.
Here’s what’s at stake:
Trump’s order would force the Postal Service to rely on a federal “approved voter” list – effectively deciding whose ballots get delivered... _and whose don’t._
That means eligible voters could be blocked from casting their ballots simply because they’re not on the government’s list.
Horrifying.
[**We’re hoping to collect 5,000 signatures TODAY to push back. Add your name now and demand the Postal Service reject this attack on mail-in voting. >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130218320/617015825/131478339?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZGIwNTBiOC1iNTQxLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tmifIXgNGJYXdGDmsE8bFTF4YzvoYwSy67qOBNOccZE=&emci=fb572c4f-b041-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=7db050b8-b541-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=26828920)

# ['Iron Big Apple' by Steve Abramowicz](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6729589&post_id=195559551&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1NTU5NTUxLCJpYXQiOjE3NzcyMzkwNjUsImV4cCI6MTc3OTgzMTA2NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTY3Mjk1ODkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ZuI2O6Cxxkii6LjxlD68A4pMlzH3aSZvhRYkNOIQVWk)
### What if the future of New York looks a lot like the past people tried to escape?
| | [Heartland Journal ®](https://substack.com/@heartlandjournal)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/311b2d3b-c3de-421d-9e12-5cdbbcd37b7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

As the confetti from New Year's celebrations settles on the streets of Manhattan, a new era dawns for the Big Apple under Mayor Zohran Mamdani, the city's first Muslim and openly democratic socialist leader. In his inauguration speech yesterday, Mamdani declared: "We will replace the frigidity of rugged individualism with the warmth of collectivism."

These words, delivered with the fervor of a reformer, evoked a sense of communal solidarity amid New York's housing crises and economic inequalities. Yet, for those versed in the annals of 20th-century history, they carry a chilling resonance - one that harks back to the forcible imposition of communism in Eastern Europe, particularly in Romania, where similar promises of collective warmth masked decades of repression, famine, and economic ruin.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6729589&post_id=195559551&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1NTU5NTUxLCJpYXQiOjE3NzcyMzkwNjUsImV4cCI6MTc3OTgzMTA2NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTY3Mjk1ODkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ZuI2O6Cxxkii6LjxlD68A4pMlzH3aSZvhRYkNOIQVWk)
As a reporter who has covered the lingering scars of Soviet-era regimes across the Balkans, I cannot ignore the parallels. Romania's fall to communism in the post-World War II era serves as a stark cautionary tale. If Mamdani's rhetoric translates into deeds - policies prioritizing state-led collectivism over individual enterprise - New York City could face dark days ahead, marked by stifled innovation, eroded property rights, and a slide toward bureaucratic authoritarianism. This is not alarmism; it is history substantiated by the lived experiences of millions.

Romania's descent into communism began not with revolution from below, but with imposition from above, orchestrated by Soviet forces in the chaotic aftermath of World War II. In 1944, following the overthrow of the fascist dictator Ion Antonescu, a fragile coalition emerged between democratic parties rooted in Western liberal traditions and the nascent Communist Party, which pledged allegiance to Moscow. Despite scant popular support - the communists garnered less than 10% in genuine polls - they maneuvered into power with Stalin's backing.

Key figures like Gheorghe Gheorghiu-Dej, leader of the "native" Romanian communists, and Ana Pauker, head of the Soviet-trained "Muscovites," visited Moscow in January 1945 to secure approval for their takeover. By March 6, 1945, Soviet pressure forced King Michael to appoint Petru Groza, a pro-communist, as prime minister. From there, the communists seized control of security forces, media, and key ministries, systematically dismantling opposition. The National Peasant Party, led by Iuliu Maniu, rallied around the king and appealed for Western intervention, but the Allies' recognition of Groza's government in February 1946 sealed Romania's fate.

The November 1946 elections were a farce, rigged to claim an 80% victory for the communists and their allies, despite evidence of widespread fraud. By 1947, liberal institutions crumbled under the weight of Soviet ideology. Opposition leaders faced show trials; Maniu was imprisoned, and King Michael was coerced into abdication on December 30, 1947, paving the way for the proclamation of the Romanian People's Republic. This marked the full pivot from individualism - private enterprise, free markets, and personal freedoms - to collectivism, where the state became the arbiter of all.

Under the 1948 constitution, modeled on the Soviet Union's, power vested in the Communist Party, with state institutions merely executing its will. The Securitate, a vast secret police network, enforced compliance. Private organizations dissolved, churches were subjugated, and mass organizations mobilized to indoctrinate the populace. Economically, Stalinist central planning prioritized heavy industry, neglecting consumer goods. Agriculture, the backbone of Romania's pre-war economy, was collectivized by 1962, confiscating private lands and forcing peasants into state farms. This "warmth of collectivism" - as propagandists framed it - resulted in terror: arrests, public humiliations, executions, and the destruction of the independent farmer class, seen as a threat due to their self-sufficiency.

The consequences were catastrophic. Romania, once Europe's leading grain exporter, plunged into chronic food shortages. Communists seized crops and livestock, leading to famine and malnutrition. Productivity collapsed as incentives evaporated; owning more than others became a crime. Culturally, the regime attacked religion, replacing family and faith with party loyalty. By the 1950s, under Gheorghiu-Dej, purges eliminated rivals like Pauker, and Soviet domination deepened through organizations like Comecon and the Warsaw Pact.

The 1960s brought a veneer of "national communism." Gheorghiu-Dej relaxed some controls, improving housing, healthcare, and education while defying Soviet economic subordination in a 1964 "declaration of independence." But this was no liberalization; ideology remained rigid. After his death in 1965, Nicolae Ceaușescu ascended, amplifying the cult of personality. He expanded Western ties to reduce Soviet influence but intensified domestic repression. His 1971 "July Theses" restored orthodoxy, intruding into daily life. To repay foreign debts from failed industrialization, Ceaușescu exported essentials in the 1980s, causing shortages of food, fuel, and medicine. Living standards plummeted; industries and agriculture disorganized under Stalinist mismanagement.

Minorities suffered acutely: Hungarians in Transylvania faced assimilation policies, while "village systematization" demolished rural communities to eradicate peasant independence. By the late 1980s, Romania was a police state, with the Securitate propping up Ceaușescu amid widespread despair. The regime collapsed in December 1989 amid uprisings in Timișoara and Bucharest, culminating in Ceaușescu's execution on Christmas Day. The National Salvation Front, dominated by ex-communists, oversaw a halting transition, but the scars - economic backwardness, eroded trust, and generational trauma - persist.

Romania's collectivism was not voluntary warmth but forced subjugation: 90% of farmland collectivized by the 1960s, millions imprisoned or killed, and a once-vibrant economy reduced to ration lines. The shift from individualism to state control promised equity but delivered tyranny.

**Parallels in Mamdani's Manifesto**

Mayor Mamdani's invocation of "the warmth of collectivism" mirrors the seductive language used by Romanian communists to justify their power grab.

In his speech, he positioned it as an antidote to individualism's "frigidity," echoing how Eastern Bloc leaders framed capitalism as cold and exploitative, while collectivism offered communal embrace. But as a Romanian immigrant highlighted in a viral video, these are "famous words spoken by every communist in history."
The man, [identifying as Bogdan](https://substack.com/redirect/7ba37e3a-4def-4189-af19-8cc0034c4a41?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), recounted how such rhetoric preceded land confiscation, terror, and famine in Romania - a narrative corroborated by historical records.
Mamdani, a Democratic Socialist of America member, has pledged policies that align with this shift: reviving "big government" through rent freezes, city-run grocery stores, and [defunded policing in favor of community oversight](https://substack.com/redirect/823922f1-1fe8-4553-8945-cbf616d982e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

These echo Romania's early steps: state seizure of property (akin to rent controls as "textbook land confiscation"), suppression of independent classes (small landlords targeted as "threats"), and replacement of market freedoms with central planning (public groceries supplanting private trade).If words match deeds, New York's dark days could manifest in several ways. First, economic stagnation: Collectivization in Romania killed incentives, turning exporters into beggars. In NYC, aggressive interventions could deter investment, exacerbating the exodus of businesses and high-earners already fleeing high taxes. Second, erosion of freedoms: Romanian communists attacked faith and family; Mamdani's platform, while not explicitly anti-religious, emphasizes state authority over individual choice, potentially clashing with diverse communities. Third, repression: The Securitate enforced compliance; defunded police and "equity" mandates could foster a culture of surveillance and public shaming, as seen in DSA pushes for activist councils.

Critics, including Rep. Chip Roy and commentator Brit Hume, warn this is "Marxist" ideology repackaged.

Hume [shared a post](https://substack.com/redirect/eb0eb3c6-693f-46a9-bc89-ccb09dc297e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) linking Mamdani's words to historical famines like the Holodomor, where collectivism starved millions.

[Ayn Rand](https://substack.com/redirect/2de9cd20-1f60-4ca9-9fc4-f213f51fb0e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a refugee from Soviet collectivism, predicted such trends in 1959, decrying them as "enslavement."

New York, a beacon of rugged individualism - think Wall Street's entrepreneurs or immigrant hustlers - risks becoming a laboratory for failure.

**A Warning for the World's Financial Capital**

New Yorkers elected Mamdani amid frustration with inequality, much as Romanians initially tolerated communists amid post-war chaos. But history shows collectivism's warmth is illusory; it burns out individual spark, leaving ashes of dependency. If Mamdani's administration confiscates via policy what markets built, the city could face productivity collapse, shortages (imagine rationed housing), and moral decay, as self-reliance yields to state patronage.

This is not inevitable. Romania escaped in 1989 through revolution; New York can course-correct via markets and pluralism. But ignoring the echoes invites peril. As Bogdan urged, "Some Americans are the dumbest morons on the planet" for repeating history.

For a city that prides itself on resilience, the test is whether it heeds the lessons of the past - or succumbs to a chilling embrace.

Editorial comments expressed in this column are the sole opinion of the writer

name: Team Schrier
subject: Kim to RFK Jr.: "You're basically sentencing thousands of kids"

name: Cori Bush
subject: We’re officially 100 days out from the primary
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori For Us
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-26q2?refcode=X_260426_bankrolled_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-26q2?refcode=X_260426_bankrolled_actrs]
We’re officially 100 days out from the primary, and this race is TIED.
And let me tell y’all something.
Fighting back against Donald Trump doesn’t start with speeches. It starts with action: opposing his agenda, voting to impeach him, and holding every single person in his administration accountable.
But here’s the truth too many politicians won’t say out loud: You can’t fight Trump if you’re funded by the same billionaire donors and PACs that put him in power.
(Cough, cough... my opponent.)
If you’re taking money from people who are actively working to harm our communities, drag us into illegal wars, and attack our bodily autonomy... They own your vote. They shape your agenda. And at that point, they own you.
No one owns me. Shade or no shade, I’ll say it, I’ve got a spine.
That’s why I’ve always stood up, unapologetically, to fight for what’s right.
I’ll keep working to stop Trump’s agenda. I’ll keep pushing to end these reckless, illegal wars. And I will continue fighting to abolish ICE.
Because I don’t take money from the same people who are trying to tear our communities apart.
But standing firm like this comes with a cost, and the people who want to silence this movement are spending big to do it. Can you chip in right now to help me keep fighting, no strings attached, no corporate PACs, just us? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-26q2?refcode=X_260426_bankrolled_actrs]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

This campaign belongs to the people. Always has. Always will.
In solidarity,
Cori
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-26q2?refcode=X_260426_bankrolled_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-26q2?refcode=X_260426_bankrolled_actrs] Cori Bush is a pastor, nurse, survivor, and formerly unhoused single mother of two who knows what it means to struggle and what it means to rise .
As a Congresswoman, she has always shown up when it’s hardest: securing disaster relief, lowering insulin costs, and delivering millions for our communities.
Across St. Louis, families are being priced out and pushed out. Cori Bush is fighting back for affordable housing, fair wages, and utilities as a human right. Be part of this people-first movement. Visit CoriBush.org [https://www.coribush.org] to learn more.

clean_body: [] We’re officially 100 days out from the primary, and this race is TIED. And let me tell y’all something. Fighting back against Donald Trump doesn’t start with speeches. It starts with action: opposing his agenda, voting to impeach him, and holding every single person in his administration accountable. But here’s the truth too many politicians won’t say out loud: You can’t fight Trump if you’re funded by the same billionaire donors and PACs that put him in power. (Cough, cough... my opponent.) If you’re taking money from people who are actively working to harm our communities, drag us into illegal wars, and attack our bodily autonomy... They own your vote. They shape your agenda. And at that point, they own you. No one owns me. Shade or no shade, I’ll say it, I’ve got a spine. That’s why I’ve always stood up, unapologetically, to fight for what’s right. I’ll keep working to stop Trump’s agenda. I’ll keep pushing to end these reckless, illegal wars. And I will continue fighting to abolish ICE. Because I don’t take money from the same people who are trying to tear our communities apart. But standing firm like this comes with a cost, and the people who want to silence this movement are spending big to do it. Can you chip in right now to help me keep fighting, no strings attached, no corporate PACs, just us? [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [] DONATE $10 [] DONATE $25 [] DONATE $50 [] DONATE $100 [] OTHER [] This campaign belongs to the people. Always has. Always will. In solidarity, Cori [] Cori Bush is a pastor, nurse, survivor, and formerly unhoused single mother of two who knows what it means to struggle and what it means to rise . As a Congresswoman, she has always shown up when it’s hardest: securing disaster relief, lowering insulin costs, and delivering millions for our communities. Across St. Louis, families are being priced out and pushed out. Cori Bush is fighting back for affordable housing, fair wages, and utilities as a human right. Be part of this people-first movement. Visit CoriBush.org [] to learn more. $5** ** to keep the lights on at HQ.
$5 to reach undecided voters with my vision for Colorado's future on the air and through social media.
$5** ** to get volunteers knocking on every door, in every neighborhood, across every corner of Colorado.
$5 to fight back against the guaranteed onslaught of attack ads from right-wing, billionaire-funded super PACs.
$5** ** to forge a better politics here than what we see out of Washington – and to make Colorado the best state to live, work and raise a family.
Since Trump's reckless tariffs have sent the purchasing power of a dollar plummeting, you might not think your $5 would make that big of a difference.
Well, as you read this email, lots of other grassroots supporters are, too. _[**_And if all of you pitched in _**_**$5 _ now, we'd hit our $100,000 public filing deadline goal in no time._**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130218623/617016803/-1487442751?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&refcode=jtk1367-fr-nd&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjFjMTllNi1iOTQxLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TRhhhSn4kv_gRYlZicELNNQary4fns3ELYc5-6lsEL0=)_
Realistically, though, I know not everyone will.
Still, I'm not a billionaire who can bankroll my own campaign like the president, and I don't take shady dark money gifts like his Republican allies, either.
**So if we're going to assemble the manpower, resources and reach that we'll need to win the governor's race this November, we have to start laying the groundwork ahead of time** – and I can't do that without you.
_[**_Will you please rush just _****$5**** _ (or anything you can spare) right away? More than just helping us reach this goal by 11:59 p.m. on Wednesday, it will immediately be put to work WINNING the Colorado governor's race._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/130218624/617016804/-1487442751?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&refcode=jtk1367-fr-nd&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YjFjMTllNi1iOTQxLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TRhhhSn4kv_gRYlZicELNNQary4fns3ELYc5-6lsEL0=)_

name: Election Integrity Task Force
subject: Was it something we said?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elect Common Sense
body:

name: Cori Bush
subject: Even after everything they’ve thrown at us
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Did y’all hear?

The latest poll shows this race is tied.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260426-cb.7z2fRR&refcode=em20260426-37710&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37710%2E1203867%2E-7hE-N ]State of the race: Bush 40, Incumbent 44, Unsure 17. Cori Bush for U.S.
Congress

After millions of dollars spent trying to tear me down, after the attacks,
the smears, all of it, we are still standing strong.

Because in this district, people don’t want a politician who cozies up to
systems that harm our communities. And they don’t want someone bankrolled
by hundreds of thousands of dollars in lobbyist money.

They want someone like me. Someone who’s been on the frontlines. Someone
who answers to constituents, not special interests.

They’re seeing what we’ve been seeing all along. The ties to ICE
contractors. The vote to thank ICE for “protecting the homeland.”

Honestly, after everything they’ve thrown at us, this poll shows that our
movement is moving the needle, and we can win this.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260426-cb.7z2fRR&refcode=em20260426-37710&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=37710%2E1203867%2E-7hE-N ]Y’all, I need you with me. We need to push now, to reach voters, tell
the truth, and take the lead before outside money tries to drown us out
again. With our primary in less than 4 months, can I count on you to chip
in right now to help us break through?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260426-cb.7z2fRR&refcode=em20260426-37710&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1022&akid=37710%2E1203867%2E-7hE-N ]Contribute $5

Let’s finish this.

In solidarity,
Cori

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20260426-37710-footer&t=1023&refcode2=37710_1203867_-7hE-N&akid=37710%2E1203867%2E-7hE-N

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1024&akid=37710%2E1203867%2E-7hE-N

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster, PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

Peter,
---
It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 _ _ and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. **But I've never been more excited to serve as an Honorary Grand Marshall than I am this year - the year of America's 250****th**** birthday.**
---
[](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=744ae7e0232447ff8e3b9cf2b9bf611c&_e=dnHIWx1xZ7_nUdrGRmx7FYvN2oRu10dsX4OqEcfme3tq40SlYkDR7MXVpz7-a2C-A-4rAad4I58f22709xGcwhmi_tRkWOK8FRqFydNPTZt6Ml9AZ7Vn9jtJWLvOLyt2rH8XftXSlyF5RR1lNVqxaSeaVCWLJrVFfaGeZamI_qZxQUVXiZPvyrlXK4GD3qTh033zQuDQu-vUZH19Qaq5Nx91Nz7KYItAmIEUxtpEqZ8ouNPmh0j3LwdhE-yKRoKHhxTG4BeWvH1VbajzYRVY-08OBLxb-PxBiJux_HN4udj187WnxnL1wxBxCq-7bDPBUPYrtcxc4Yy2rj7dqUgair1tAMVUe9hrjq4zLVDp7nRtNDDV4qZzPikOR-4rwzQEMP8_4F90IKQ50L6TFK2DOLtYj6MtFsQLbGofiR6Qmzs44K6qyNTluH6bRkf6wZV6Y7q0EhJO6NURy2i9HzdlZjUxCJjK1-np2LAoiGMAYXw39mkllzem3bK7QdEvA_uvr7_CzyHiKlBZ2XGQPwyGLYSgKDB5r4Az1YGc796BzYS3Rl7pIoXwUEIGHmhcyXEvRm0DgbkETkw6z2QJ6V6jEIm9YzJ3Yr9GXwsg8Tqd0Oy6KrZy3j12CYcyadPXlNyP_JoXL68F4WX5R4kHp0EJrM8Bm9CNkYQKjO9HBK80dsbVRHpd5cQfnwHSSzHTwAFM)

Peter, this Memorial Day marks the first major national act of remembrance of the veterans who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend America from its enemies and ensure it has remained the Land of the Free for the last 250 years. And this year's National Memorial Day Parade will be a special edition - the biggest parade yet - where national memory meets living history as veterans from every military generation celebrate America and honor the service and sacrifice of their fallen brethren. **Peter, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous patriots like you.** **[In light of America’s 250th birthday and the special opportunity we have to honor veterans this year, will you chip in today to help support the parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=744ae7e0232447ff8e3b9cf2b9bf611c&_e=dnHIWx1xZ7_nUdrGRmx7FYvN2oRu10dsX4OqEcfme3tq40SlYkDR7MXVpz7-a2C-A-4rAad4I58f22709xGcwhmi_tRkWOK8FRqFydNPTZt6Ml9AZ7Vn9jtJWLvOLyt2rH8XftXSlyF5RR1lNVqxaSeaVCWLJrVFfaGeZamI_qZxQUVXiZPvyrlXK4GD3qTh033zQuDQu-vUZH19Qaq5Nx91Nz7KYItAmIEUxtpEqZ8ouNPmh0j3LwdhE-yKRoKHSm8iusntAzXZ05OmViOnWR66iARR3C2u07Rdz-goOqKdimtUSpWso78M2flQiK1Hf4yEFQwyrCGvO7k3Wneg_uU1O_BL7a9tgPsWSMjCseG2OFAQCOcVk2QFciPvK9tKvVqkNF-_gfXJ2pNtGlvgDLHkoHPo5EZmpD8bcQ_V55jZQMgBPKVraM5O0OncCV_JOtMumpE8L9qWonoDGrAIuiMyIZLZlY4_KbT9AGHKamzim6sd3je4CKP8u8t7_wy45MJRBxoHL6M5rDjYodvkPthmyR7WlQua2uq5xgAPgStRKCO_UAeCxTTC4asVTuR_ipZ22ePQ8Ax4Q-2AypocXQFvntE-BHZNhn9XTjTqoIEoo5ZaezOoy3W2fFkfJ4ohSLLzqTUrejUBKhRddkfVzSoSfOd1qr9A8_3xQlGPbNVsYamcXQCZdRMR7LR0i9W-)**

The 2026 National Memorial Day Parade is a once-in-a-lifetime moment where we need to do all we can to honor our country and our veterans. _Bigger_ crowds. _More_ veterans. _More _young people learning what courage, service, and sacrifice really mean.
---

Historically, AVC has been able to do this at a fraction of what other major public events cost to put on each year. In fact, _the entire parade_ costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! _That means that every dollar that's contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event._ **But I want to reiterate that American Veterans Center does not take ANY federal funding for this event. It is funded ****_entirely_**** by private citizens like you who want to honor those who have sacrificed so much for our country.** **[Peter, as we look ahead to this historic event, can I count on your generous gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=744ae7e0232447ff8e3b9cf2b9bf611c&_e=dnHIWx1xZ7_nUdrGRmx7FYvN2oRu10dsX4OqEcfme3tq40SlYkDR7MXVpz7-a2C-A-4rAad4I58f22709xGcwhmi_tRkWOK8FRqFydNPTZt6Ml9AZ7Vn9jtJWLvOLyt2rH8XftXSlyF5RR1lNVqxaSeaVCWLJrVFfaGeZamI_qZxQUVXiZPvyrlXK4GD3qTh033zQuDQu-vUZH19Qaq5Nx91Nz7KYItAmIEUxtpEqZ8ouNPmh0j3LwdhE-yKRoKHRH7dN9BLlZRN8JcisSBBp-M5js8vDmmXu5ph7wPji0zVpBwhR03vKAxrreY2eMMONp9AbWONbxTHNL3H6TcCLojoiQbaY6QdE3LgjVm2NuH3z_v31iHoCGvk47pX6JRAzlPmGpS3jEw2YK8vLpbU3Z1-EuFjyzLz2qlhfIo3hBUckyk6lkvsBqEqEGHQVuLkUKP-RdoSxGiIRtaxHGnY7yAanKozvPHwvOtjDQqmqFtTfIiEsN4osN4c01YNtvb9S7aHEWJoqxFrQE4R117MVtp8Ef1RvsRjw6Pc-tt4j29CJ7TTvCVqNYwH0lMGxg763hHQ-6J96FGq1q5UODwpDWLv2lAMkdqvKs-kXHdDpEnM16YVFe8axCVbcSxp6LyL66NT6ffc1Erq6UqAsqRXbKwPjM9QhEKW0haaNij-m2xMdMl9ijKDqeLWqhOw7rl2)[](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=744ae7e0232447ff8e3b9cf2b9bf611c&_e=dnHIWx1xZ7_nUdrGRmx7FYvN2oRu10dsX4OqEcfme3tq40SlYkDR7MXVpz7-a2C-A-4rAad4I58f22709xGcwhmi_tRkWOK8FRqFydNPTZt6Ml9AZ7Vn9jtJWLvOLyt2rH8XftXSlyF5RR1lNVqxaSeaVCWLJrVFfaGeZamI_qZxQUVXiZPvyrlXK4GD3qTh033zQuDQu-vUZH19Qaq5Nx91Nz7KYItAmIEUxtpEqZ8ouNPmh0j3LwdhE-yKRoKH0sO1GAQjStenxwgl0tB37qrVdoUl7OtCcgQQ0fSXH83eF4-Wa6_R81mP6GucPIqby2bnSotmQiAPwLuHFKbRPPBM9bclf4pddOPyLAZfaM7fIs2OH4v-I8re49sm52okkV_7Qn0q8uSuXM7IivvbQCzAo9YK5zosnZJsZgqTdMfh_d92IVC5UMTDk5En0NA5ELuGLTf3bcC5Tvr_QQ_NUF1tQozkE5jl63y6MtbV_rr5BTBXfjZu9sxJuJUDUNONfKbmC7ttSAhXNadHF9-aLubzG8zccp4DquQ8nBCeZKAN8wp9wwfp2Whu6yr46gDZ3NQKEcTaV_oFI4V3LlTaOfWG-WVGkQVHRerN6n65mycSzjfd9zfba3LZPaPvtiuRHL0_6R3DTHWVDhSPE08txjIVwWDCyPoMP1Wr0lBO8lypkBY6ekTLe-gl2mMam11d)**

| | Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the parade, but also supports great programs like:
---
* Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to today before we lose these stories forever.

* Hold our annual American Veterans Conference, which allows young cadets a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, Vietnam, and our modern wars.

* Produce veteran-focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom.

---
But of course, the National Memorial Day Parade is AVC's flagship event honoring America's veterans. And this year's parade will be the largest in their history of hosting the parade. If you've been looking for an opportunity to give back to those who have given so much for our freedom, this is one of the best possible ways you could do it. _And for a special group of veterans, this might be one of the last chances we get._ You see, with each year that goes by, we lose more and more of our World War II veterans, and for the few we have remaining, traveling to Washington, D.C., for the parade is difficult.

| | WWII veterans were a key part of the first parade when it was revived in 2005, as they have been every year since for the last 20 years. _But this may be the last year we have the opportunity to have these brave men and women with us for the parade._** ** **This year is a rare moment when living witnesses to World War II and America 's 250****th**** birthday overlap. ** **[Will you join AVC and me in making a concerted effort to ensure these veterans are at this year’s historic National Memorial Day parade to honor them one last time?](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=744ae7e0232447ff8e3b9cf2b9bf611c&_e=dnHIWx1xZ7_nUdrGRmx7FYvN2oRu10dsX4OqEcfme3tq40SlYkDR7MXVpz7-a2C-A-4rAad4I58f22709xGcwhmi_tRkWOK8FRqFydNPTZt6Ml9AZ7Vn9jtJWLvOLyt2rH8XftXSlyF5RR1lNVqxaSeaVCWLJrVFfaGeZamI_qZxQUVXiZPvyrlXK4GD3qTh033zQuDQu-vUZH19Qaq5Nx91Nz7KYItAmIEUxtpEqZ8ouNPmh0j3LwdhE-yKRoKHl1rX9T82qy7h_TzB0ZcpKDJW9WhsDomsnTB0iOhrGNKiN3P3oei3WB_FnzXwjpelFw2SZNxfr95XuXpvSU4FL8QpyMiSUSEuDJjDPGfP_Ew6bIJWHEi8pit6OqgUCpyY0P4s9YrWfgCbMaxigOusurX1a_HkXslVIjkMBAoIkDL1zpShPT3pfu95stPUgzL1M5sDY3HqcmdxHdLjNDxj6AMz6PDJNYN8hQo_DNi5hLebHNAeAUKc9vAFsxmbA1vITUBGH832z5gzBrB776-6igrxmpvJ8IOxvaeAZXJp0xQeahe_URB4qL7TlAJdPGDzuWvZthTNOdQ2lBvplnkBpijhUmdS3VPCrgObOt2Pch9Kudhxhgg8p-HO7AQIRL4MR5m5bRUH5vrFYHxwSvV0kBkYnpwYEWSLL89PRfqMYbAll0ULicKGdfVH9wAFb17M)**

On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more. **Peter, thank you for your support of America 's heroes. ** [**And please, if you’re able, make a gift to fund the National Memorial Day parade right now during America’s 250****th****birthday.**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=744ae7e0232447ff8e3b9cf2b9bf611c&_e=dnHIWx1xZ7_nUdrGRmx7FYvN2oRu10dsX4OqEcfme3tq40SlYkDR7MXVpz7-a2C-A-4rAad4I58f22709xGcwhmi_tRkWOK8FRqFydNPTZt6Ml9AZ7Vn9jtJWLvOLyt2rH8XftXSlyF5RR1lNVqxaSeaVCWLJrVFfaGeZamI_qZxQUVXiZPvyrlXK4GD3qTh033zQuDQu-vUZH19Qaq5Nx91Nz7KYItAmIEUxtpEqZ8ouNPmh0j3LwdhE-yKRoKHA_jM-IO1li6uxUY7Z8MgeVTxMmy47stPoXKAL3w1Q-idsPUWS_wBJjmxHIEtxjnsLnf9Lisv7WGJk2jF5JjlgzqilJsL9fqCQas7XaNDV9UEgK0LLnZo19KYUT9-iPb7GNE6ugFiLe_wP5HPBgaidEezqxl6QMzLH5NFXnPZ3_uoVzex-vJbMoPZxnHzXDtIpAYxSaGlqRvNtRH0mipbTFVXj8XBV0xV5CNAhhDPggmAPBDGL94dXCYOzL2u_Wh0ySqO1YqAHZuosektr8N3lNIoYUrs-OOWteaX6sLWlN0-nnQOHcgBKWPM6IFg2QZTdJQmJpUUCaoYBBegVrfsS8wfp41-KoRch84slQ1rtCVRzrUIUWagf5hrezTJUqWPSF0tidtgx_pPMfLIKpC3BudwkuKfZheCqXZh_lQ7ZPTeoTZSHXhDE4HGY6e9InPx)

**P.S.** Peter, America is stepping into its 250th year, and this Memorial Day begins that national remembrance. Veterans across generations have given their lives to defend America and all she stands for. And for the Greatest Generation in particular, this may be the last time they're able to join us at this year's National Memorial Day Parade. There's no better send-off we can give them than to make sure they're present to honor their fallen brethren during America's 250th birthday. **[Please join AVC and me today in making this event possible with your generous gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or whatever amount you can give.](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=744ae7e0232447ff8e3b9cf2b9bf611c&_e=dnHIWx1xZ7_nUdrGRmx7FYvN2oRu10dsX4OqEcfme3tq40SlYkDR7MXVpz7-a2C-A-4rAad4I58f22709xGcwhmi_tRkWOK8FRqFydNPTZt6Ml9AZ7Vn9jtJWLvOLyt2rH8XftXSlyF5RR1lNVqxaSeaVCWLJrVFfaGeZamI_qZxQUVXiZPvyrlXK4GD3qTh033zQuDQu-vUZH19Qaq5Nx91Nz7KYItAmIEUxtpEqZ8ouNPmh0j3LwdhE-yKRoKHdhpSP6pLHRTktCAQ511ZBSuwBur2OX3X9Px_Ayhl1Wo9zj4vUFcgoI8hrFNQ9b_8L2_P8FyqiUGDGELWtFeQetXJ3UYYpq1GloDhf15tZarrpvWBDq2_Ntw6dlPC9-N3up7_Y3_i5ybzrM9kmrSAlqBTKEuY6E8CIKYBF4_8Vv4TdsrKQa0XYh1e-oiz3deuETffDqAxTvhAkTW_VGZGbaZUk6jG20vKtm1lwSX9QXvQAzGG_7J5QfC5SakIm8jZtQzYLe9F8WYXXmanZKKj0aUgH2kT9bTtKSK4Wt58K5euo6LdbL2JBJC-qaomlUU-nD89oo9StLP8IdJ96n8mvsQvRHzbDTe8fzwlNiAZfJOcr8lL9yfL1zJ4XaHiloES4-mYh64IcQ37CrdIGMGoGxNQ7SknEFsocEupus683rmeiXC7NPzA8V7uq5-xomLd)**

clean_body: A historic opportunity to honor veterans this Memorial Day | | | Peter, It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. But I've never been more excited to serve as an Honorary Grand Marshall than I am this year - the year of America's 250th birthday. Peter, this Memorial Day marks the first major national act of remembrance of the veterans who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend America from its enemies and ensure it has remained the Land of the Free for the last 250 years. And this year's National Memorial Day Parade will be a special edition - the biggest parade yet - where national memory meets living history as veterans from every military generation celebrate America and honor the service and sacrifice of their fallen brethren. Peter, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous patriots like you. - The 2026 National Memorial Day Parade is a once-in-a-lifetime moment where we need to do all we can to honor our country and our veterans. Bigger crowds. More veterans. More young people learning what courage, service, and sacrifice really mean. Historically, AVC has been able to do this at a fraction of what other major public events cost to put on each year. In fact, the entire parade costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! That means that every dollar that's contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event. But I want to reiterate that American Veterans Center does not take ANY federal funding for this event. It is funded entirely by private citizens like you who want to honor those who have sacrificed so much for our country. | | Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the parade, but also supports great programs like: Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to today before we lose these stories forever. Hold our annual American Veterans Conference, which allows young cadets a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, Vietnam, and our modern wars. Produce veteran-focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom. But of course, the National Memorial Day Parade is AVC's flagship event honoring America's veterans. And this year's parade will be the largest in their history of hosting the parade. If you've been looking for an opportunity to give back to those who have given so much for our freedom, this is one of the best possible ways you could do it. And for a special group of veterans, this might be one of the last chances we get. You see, with each year that goes by, we lose more and more of our World War II veterans, and for the few we have remaining, traveling to Washington, D.C., for the parade is difficult. | | | | | WWII veterans were a key part of the first parade when it was revived in 2005, as they have been every year since for the last 20 years. But this may be the last year we have the opportunity to have these brave men and women with us for the parade. This year is a rare moment when living witnesses to World War II and America 's 250**th birthday overlap. - And no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you. On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more. Peter, thank you for your support of America 's heroes. Sincerely, Gary Sinise Honarary Grand MarshalNational Memorial Day Parade P.S. Peter, America is stepping into its 250th year, and this Memorial Day begins that national remembrance. Veterans across generations have given their lives to defend America and all she stands for. And for the Greatest Generation in particular, this may be the last time they're able to join us at this year's National Memorial Day Parade. There's no better send-off we can give them than to make sure they're present to honor their fallen brethren during America's 250th birthday. The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center 1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201
