| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | Every time you open your phone... body:
We need the resources to compete with Greg Abbott’s $106 million in the bank.
# [](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)[](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)
Here’s the latest on Gina’s campaign for Governor of Texas, Peter:
**FIRST:** Gina won the Democratic primary and secured the nomination last month, officially putting us head-to-head with Greg Abbott this November. Since then, Gina has been traveling across the state to meet with voters and let them know why Greg Abbott is not fit to continue running Texas.
**THEN:** A new poll showed Gina leading Abbott by _**19 points among Latino voters**_ — the very voters Republicans can’t afford to lose in a statewide race.
**NOW:** With millions ready to go into attack ads, Abbott is trying to drown out Texas voters and move crucial races in his party’s favor.
Greg Abbott isn’t just any governor. He’s one of the GOP’s most powerful leaders, and Texas is the cornerstone of their political strategy. If Texas is in play, everything changes.
**That’s exactly why Abbott is sitting on a $106 million war chest and why Republican outside groups are already flooding races with their money.**
We don’t have to match Abbott dollar-for-dollar. But we do need the resources to compete everywhere. On TV, online, and on the ground, so voters hear Gina’s message, not just Abbott’s version.
**That’s why we set a goal to raise $100,000 by April 30 to have the resources to inform voters and fight back.**

That’s exactly why Abbott is sitting on a $106 million war chest and why Republican outside groups are already flooding races with their money. We don’t have to match Abbott dollar-for-dollar. But we do need the resources to compete everywhere. On TV, online, and on the ground, so voters hear Gina’s message, not just Abbott’s version. That’s why we set a goal to raise $100,000 by April 30 to have the resources to inform voters and fight back. Thank you. Team Gina Email us: mailto:info@ginafortexas.com

name: Roy Cooper (via Cooper Victory Fund)
subject: We are the exception to the rule
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund and authorized by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
We can't take it for granted
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6532?source=&t=1&akid=1180%2E124072%2EVCw5hr)
| | | |
---
**Good news, Peter:** North Carolina is the #1 most likely Senate seat to flip, according to The Hill.
**Bad news:** It's also the only battleground state President Donald Trump has won three times.
**But here's the thing:** We're also the only state to defy him by electing a Democrat statewide every single time. We are the exception to the rule, but we can't take that for granted.
**If we're going to flip this seat, it's going to take all of us.**[**Chip in $5 today to help me defy the odds.**](https://act.roycooper.com/go/9841?amount=5&t=8&akid=1180%2E124072%2EVCw5hr)
| | [CHIP IN $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/9841?amount=5&t=9&akid=1180%2E124072%2EVCw5hr)
---
Thank you,

Roy Cooper
| |
* * *
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8188?t=2&akid=1180%2E124072%2EVCw5hr)
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**_[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6533?source=&t=3&akid=1180%2E124072%2EVCw5hr)_**

---

---

---
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=7&akid=1180%2E124072%2EVCw5hr)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund and authorized by Cooper for North Carolina.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.
_

body:
Our chance to restore our Democratic Senate majority is looking better and better every day. But we urgently need your help to take advantage of this opportunity every day between now and November.
#
These aren’t the Senate races you might expect, Peter.
**_The New York Times_ just identified SIX U.S. Senate races that Democrats have a chance at winning in 2026. [If you’re ready to help us flip the Senate this year, please make a donation right away. Otherwise keep reading...](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103058?refcode=em20260426-10357&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10357%2E614464%2ERbHiwZ)**

**TEXAS** Democratic nominee James Talarico has already brought polls within SINGLE DIGITS. Now, we’re just waiting on the GOP primary runoff election to determine his opponent.
---

**MICHIGAN** This month, one of the largest national GOP groups announced that they would start spending UNPRECEDENTED amounts of money to prop up the Republican candidate in this race – confirming that they’re VERY worried heading into the midterms this year.
---

**MAINE** Incumbent Susan Collins is THE MOST UNPOPULAR Republican U.S. Senator running for re-election in 2026. And with strong Democratic candidates running against her, pundits say this might be one of our best opportunities to flip a seat this November.
---

**IOWA** After disgraced GOP U.S. Senator Joni Ernst announced her retirement earlier this cycle, pundits upgraded Iowa’s U.S. Senate race TOWARD Democrats – signaling another key chance to flip a seat from red to blue.
---

**LOUISIANA** A fierce and messy GOP primary this year could offer an unexpected opening for Democrats to flip a seat in deep red Louisiana.
---

**FLORIDA** With a strong Democratic candidate and an unpopular GOP incumbent who struggles to fundraise, pundits across the country are hoping Florida will be another chance to expand the map for Democrats this year.
---
Our chance to restore our Democratic Senate majority is looking better and better every day, Peter. But we urgently need your help to take advantage of this opportunity every day between now and November.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/45502f6c-b8fb-4331-9ec1-705fd2ec19fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I have a weakness for seemingly irrelevant indicators. Nearly fifteen years ago, [I wrote in ](https://substack.com/redirect/12cb146b-fb90-4749-b181-76a99060f4b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[Forbes](https://substack.com/redirect/12cb146b-fb90-4749-b181-76a99060f4b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ that the race to build the world's tallest skyscraper was a more robust indicator of forthcoming chaos than most traditional measures that economists track](https://substack.com/redirect/12cb146b-fb90-4749-b181-76a99060f4b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). As luck would have, the **world 's tallest skyscraper neatly captures overconfidence** **and hubris** , loose money conditions that encourages excess liquidity to find a home, and ubiquitous speculative tendencies as "build it and they will come" logic runs roughshod over more conservative thinking.
Let's revisit some skyscraper history. Weeks after the Burj Dubai was deemed the world's tallest freestanding structure, the bursting of a credit bubble began which led to the Global Financial Crisis. Before the Internet bubble peaked, Taipei 101 (home of the hardware side of the tech boom) claimed the title of the world's tallest tower, displacing the Petronas Towers, a position the Malaysian skyscrapers took in 1997, as the Asian Tigers imploded and spurred the Asian Financial Crisis. In the 1970s, the Willis Tower (aka the Sears Tower) redefined the Chicago skyline and stagflation followed. And perhaps the most iconic skyscraper to telegraph a financial crisis was the Empire State Building, which took the crown of the world's tallest in 1931 as the Great Depression took hold.
* * *
## [Skyscrapers Are A Great Bubble Indicator](https://substack.com/redirect/26a6be42-a4b0-4e54-bda7-3d421da9c5f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| March 10, 2011
[](https://substack.com/redirect/26a6be42-a4b0-4e54-bda7-3d421da9c5f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[One of the first skyscrapers was designed and built by Bradford Lee Gilbert in 1887. It was designed to solve a problem of extremely limited space resulting from the ownership of an awkwardly shaped plot of land on Broadway in New York City. Gilbert chose to maximize the value (and potential occupancy) of the small plot by building vertically. His 160-f...](https://substack.com/redirect/26a6be42-a4b0-4e54-bda7-3d421da9c5f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/26a6be42-a4b0-4e54-bda7-3d421da9c5f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
I love unconventional indicators because they capture something official data often miss...they capture real time human behavior while traditional information is usually backward-looking. Today I'd argue that pizza -- specifically, **frozen pizza** -- is telling us something equally important about the American consumer. And very few people seem to be listening.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..WehNd_PmfM98VTvL2kQAEy__SQI5Pzs_jvBJ40Z39Qs?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=195541809)
Consider the journey of a middle-class household over the past eighteen months. Call them the Andersons. In early 2025, with inflation cooling and the labor market still firm, Saturday night was date night at the neighborhood bistro -- tasting menu, wine pairing, the works. Then [inflation reaccelerated](https://substack.com/redirect/879cd6e9-975f-4a87-be73-651ae51fc800?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), driven by a [21% month-over-month spike in gasoline prices](https://substack.com/redirect/bd0d94ad-f109-4ef3-95fa-f9a207d82022?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) tied to the Iran war. Budgets were strained, and something had to give.
So the Andersons traded down. Saturday night moved from fancy to frugal; fine dining was replaced with [Cava](https://substack.com/redirect/13b197c9-7aa7-4e63-864c-45249ac090b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), then [Sweetgreen](https://substack.com/redirect/2971273b-850a-48fb-a45c-512d3176f078?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But it didn't stop there. [Analysts now report restaurant traffic is down more than 2% year-to-date, with fast casual faring worse due to "inflation fatigue."](https://substack.com/redirect/903f29e1-4557-4bd9-869d-ba877b745bde?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Consultants note diners are still showing up; [they're just ordering fewer items per visit](https://substack.com/redirect/8d8b3cc5-d8cf-46a3-a42a-f4a286b06381?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Next came takeout...and then delivery. Eventually, and here's where the story gets interesting, the Andersons end up in the freezer aisle.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/bfbe60b1-b90b-4228-bbbe-5f6d8c1c53a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Frozen pizza is a $7.4 billion U.S. category, and while some legacy brands are struggling, [private-label frozen pizza grew 4% last year to $1.1 billion](https://substack.com/redirect/c2d8b4f2-ccf9-43aa-b54b-41e1cc719b60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), making it the third-largest player in the category. The frozen pizza market is expected to grow at a 5.3% CAGR through 2030, with private label as the key driver of the category's growth towards a $10 billion market ([Technavio](https://substack.com/redirect/79d959b7-072c-400b-9549-a28a11d9ea38?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)). Similar dynamics are at work globally ([Fortune Business Insights](https://substack.com/redirect/754978e1-2ea1-4f7c-8027-5cfba1dc9233?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)).
As you're likely aware, I have skin in this game. At Goodwell Foods, we're seeing steadily rising demand for private label frozen pizza from some of America's largest retailers. After launching our operations in December 2024, we've made investments (including a liquid nitrogen tunnel) to meaningfully expand our capacity ([Concord Monitor](https://substack.com/redirect/4553778c-114f-49b8-bd9f-14aa90d4230a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)). I'm not in the business of investing capital like that on a hunch or hope. We invested because the consumer keeps showing up at the private-label shelf, and the retailers are eager to keep those shelves stocked.
* * *
## [Zigging While Others Zag: Investing to Cool a Hot Market](https://substack.com/redirect/c8c64aa1-64d5-4f0c-a229-2d6cca85060f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| June 14, 2025
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c8c64aa1-64d5-4f0c-a229-2d6cca85060f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[For many business leaders, the cross currents of a global trade war, military conflict in the Middle East, shifting geopolitical pressures, and disruption from new technologies have created a paralyzing level of uncertainty. Does it make sense to invest when artificial intelligence may replace your entire industry? What if tariffs create chaos in your s...](https://substack.com/redirect/c8c64aa1-64d5-4f0c-a229-2d6cca85060f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/c8c64aa1-64d5-4f0c-a229-2d6cca85060f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Layer _private label_ onto _single-serve_ and you get what may be the purest trade-down vehicle in American grocery: a personal-sized, store-brand pie that costs a fraction of a DoorDash order and perhaps a twentieth of a casual-dining entree. **Single-serve frozen pizza** is among the fastest-growing sub-segments, driven by smaller households, GLP-1 driven portion control goals, and "[convenience meal solutions](https://substack.com/redirect/79d959b7-072c-400b-9549-a28a11d9ea38?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
Further, there is an increasing expectation among consumers that everyone can have whatever "flavor" they want. It's not surprising for a family of four to have one vote for pepperoni, one for cheese, one for veggies, and one for a meat trio. Single serve allows everyone to get what they want, and this is driving [15% higher repeat-purchase rates](https://substack.com/redirect/79d959b7-072c-400b-9549-a28a11d9ea38?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), when compared to multi-serve pizza.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/a6d957b6-bd75-4415-bcb2-3af6ad6fda3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In a K-shaped economy where the top decile still dines at [Eleven Madison Park](https://substack.com/redirect/d2666c58-aff7-4bcc-afb1-79010e850728?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) while the bottom leg of the K counts dollars at checkout, **single-serve private-label frozen pizza is where the squeeze shows up most cleanly.** The logic is straightforward. When oil spikes, food and transport costs cascade through the system. When gasoline runs ~25% higher year-on-year, every trip to the restaurant is itself squeezing budgets. And when consumer confidence sags, discretionary dining is among the first casualties. Pizza is a beneficiary, and private label and frozen catch strong tailwinds.
In fact, the Conference Board notes that 2026 spending is shifting toward _" cheap thrills and necessary services"_ and away from expensive or highly discretionary purchases. A hot pizza from your own oven is, in my admittedly biased eyes, both a cheap thrill and a necessary service...which is exactly why pizza deserves a seat at the FOMC's table.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..WehNd_PmfM98VTvL2kQAEy__SQI5Pzs_jvBJ40Z39Qs?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=195541809)
The Federal Reserve builds policy using data that includes aggregated, lagged, and backward-looking series to understand inflation and employment conditions. But the cascade from tasting menu to store-brand single-serve frozen pizza captures real-time household behavior with far more integrity than a consumer sentiment survey. It is, in the language of professional economists, a _**revealed**_ rather than a _**stated** _preference. Frozen pizza is where rising gas, sticky inflation, and eroding confidence meet actual consumer decisions.
* * *
## [Pizza Power and The Economy](https://substack.com/redirect/dcfb4155-3681-42a0-a6b8-9d9c5fa98382?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| December 15, 2025
[](https://substack.com/redirect/dcfb4155-3681-42a0-a6b8-9d9c5fa98382?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Earlier this year, I wrote a short piece about pizza's power to predict major domestic and geopolitical events, noting that unusually large late night pizza order delivered to the Pentagon, White House, or even Congress had been correlated with major geopolitical developments emerging from Washington DC. After all, during an emerging or ongoing crisis, ...](https://substack.com/redirect/dcfb4155-3681-42a0-a6b8-9d9c5fa98382?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/dcfb4155-3681-42a0-a6b8-9d9c5fa98382?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
So, in my humble opinion, it's time for the Fed to consume more pizza (data). When private-label single-serve frozen pizza sales accelerate, consumers aren't simply eating more carbs. They're telling you something about household cash flow, perceived risk, and how they feel about the future. By moving a rung or two down the trade-down ladder, they're revealing what their budgets are dictating. And historically, when a lot of people move down that ladder at once, the macroeconomy tends to follow. By many measures, the lower leg of the K-shaped economy is already in a recession. Frozen pizza seems to be confirming this reality.
Skyscrapers suggest (over)confidence and potential irrational exuberance. Frozen pizza points to strained budgets and declining confidence. Somewhere in between sits the business cycle. Right now, the freezer aisle is whispering something economic data may eventually reveal in the months ahead.
We should probably listen.

**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/3fca11be-d894-4d99-b21b-65941f726653?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/bbfae178-9e6f-4ad3-9032-f9525e704245?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/8752d21f-0ff3-45a9-ad2f-8cf3324a5f77?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

As luck would have, the world 's tallest skyscraper neatly captures overconfidence and hubris , loose money conditions that encourages excess liquidity to find a home, and ubiquitous speculative tendencies as "build it and they will come" logic runs roughshod over more conservative thinking. Let's revisit some skyscraper history. Weeks after the Burj Dubai was deemed the world's tallest freestanding structure, the bursting of a credit bubble began which led to the Global Financial Crisis. Before the Internet bubble peaked, Taipei 101 (home of the hardware side of the tech boom) claimed the title of the world's tallest tower, displacing the Petronas Towers, a position the Malaysian skyscrapers took in 1997, as the Asian Tigers imploded and spurred the Asian Financial Crisis. In the 1970s, the Willis Tower (aka the Sears Tower) redefined the Chicago skyline and stagflation followed. And perhaps the most iconic skyscraper to telegraph a financial crisis was the Empire State Building, which took the crown of the world's tallest in 1931 as the Great Depression took hold. | | | | ---|---|--- | March 10, 2011 | I love unconventional indicators because they capture something official data often miss...they capture real time human behavior while traditional information is usually backward-looking. Today I'd argue that pizza -- specifically, frozen pizza -- is telling us something equally important about the American consumer. And very few people seem to be listening. Consider the journey of a middle-class household over the past eighteen months. Call them the Andersons. In early 2025, with inflation cooling and the labor market still firm, Saturday night was date night at the neighborhood bistro -- tasting menu, wine pairing, the works. Then , driven by a tied to the Iran war. Budgets were strained, and something had to give. So the Andersons traded down. Saturday night moved from fancy to frugal; fine dining was replaced with , then . But it didn't stop there. Consultants note diners are still showing up; . Next came takeout...and then delivery. Eventually, and here's where the story gets interesting, the Andersons end up in the freezer aisle. Frozen pizza is a $7.4 billion U.S. category, and while some legacy brands are struggling, , making it the third-largest player in the category. The frozen pizza market is expected to grow at a 5.3% CAGR through 2030, with private label as the key driver of the category's growth towards a $10 billion market (). Similar dynamics are at work globally (). As you're likely aware, I have skin in this game. At Goodwell Foods, we're seeing steadily rising demand for private label frozen pizza from some of America's largest retailers. After launching our operations in December 2024, we've made investments (including a liquid nitrogen tunnel) to meaningfully expand our capacity (). I'm not in the business of investing capital like that on a hunch or hope. We invested because the consumer keeps showing up at the private-label shelf, and the retailers are eager to keep those shelves stocked. | | | | ---|---|--- | June 14, 2025 | Layer private label onto single-serve and you get what may be the purest trade-down vehicle in American grocery: a personal-sized, store-brand pie that costs a fraction of a DoorDash order and perhaps a twentieth of a casual-dining entree. Single-serve frozen pizza is among the fastest-growing sub-segments, driven by smaller households, GLP-1 driven portion control goals, and "." Further, there is an increasing expectation among consumers that everyone can have whatever "flavor" they want. It's not surprising for a family of four to have one vote for pepperoni, one for cheese, one for veggies, and one for a meat trio. Single serve allows everyone to get what they want, and this is driving , when compared to multi-serve pizza. In a K-shaped economy where the top decile still dines at while the bottom leg of the K counts dollars at checkout, single-serve private-label frozen pizza is where the squeeze shows up most cleanly. The logic is straightforward. When oil spikes, food and transport costs cascade through the system. When gasoline runs ~25% higher year-on-year, every trip to the restaurant is itself squeezing budgets. And when consumer confidence sags, discretionary dining is among the first casualties. Pizza is a beneficiary, and private label and frozen catch strong tailwinds. In fact, the Conference Board notes that 2026 spending is shifting toward " cheap thrills and necessary services" and away from expensive or highly discretionary purchases. A hot pizza from your own oven is, in my admittedly biased eyes, both a cheap thrill and a necessary service...which is exactly why pizza deserves a seat at the FOMC's table. The Federal Reserve builds policy using data that includes aggregated, lagged, and backward-looking series to understand inflation and employment conditions. But the cascade from tasting menu to store-brand single-serve frozen pizza captures real-time household behavior with far more integrity than a consumer sentiment survey. It is, in the language of professional economists, a revealed rather than a stated preference. Frozen pizza is where rising gas, sticky inflation, and eroding confidence meet actual consumer decisions. | | | | ---|---|--- | December 15, 2025 | So, in my humble opinion, it's time for the Fed to consume more pizza (data). When private-label single-serve frozen pizza sales accelerate, consumers aren't simply eating more carbs. They're telling you something about household cash flow, perceived risk, and how they feel about the future. By moving a rung or two down the trade-down ladder, they're revealing what their budgets are dictating. And historically, when a lot of people move down that ladder at once, the macroeconomy tends to follow. By many measures, the lower leg of the K-shaped economy is already in a recession. Frozen pizza seems to be confirming this reality. Skyscrapers suggest (over)confidence and potential irrational exuberance. Frozen pizza points to strained budgets and declining confidence. Somewhere in between sits the business cycle. Right now, the freezer aisle is whispering something economic data may eventually reveal in the months ahead. We should probably listen. | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and **. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Kelan Publishing, LLC PO BOX 932, Lincoln, NH 03251 ? ?

_**It’s Adam Schwarze; former Navy SEAL and Marine Corps veteran running for the U.S. Senate in Minnesota.**_

If elected, I'd be the **eighth Navy SEAL to serve in Congress,** and the second in the United States Senate.

This year feels as though it’s shaping up to be one for the record books, but what records I’m not sure yet. Minnesota has all of the hallmarks of a state that is ready for new, fresh leadership. But whether or not we’re going to be able to get the job done remains to be seen.

Peter,
MAGA was just catastrophically blown out in a deep red Louisiana special election. The 2026 Blue Tsunami is REAL & ROLLING over blood red deep South.
We come bearing good news from the Bayou:
Louisiana is now IN PLAY.
In a deep red MAGA country that voted for Trump by 13 points a Democrat didn't just win: she won big --defeating a diehard MAGA Republican w/ Trump's "total and complete" endorsement by 24 points. A 37 pt over-performance (I got chills typing that).
The White House had to issue a statement that stated, "Don't Be a Panican. We're Winning"
My name is Jamie Davis, I'm the Democrat running for US Senate in Louisiana. My family worked this Delta land when they were slaves and today my son and I farm the very same fields together but now we own them.
[Democrats have been making a desperate last stand here and now we've broken through. We can elect Louisiana's first Black Senator since Reconstruction. Please, rush a donation of $50, $25, or even just $5 to help us take the fight to MAGA's home turf.](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001am2VYKm9sCmVpD4ZNzO0fNyc3ytME1XnIDtL-aMPy8zrceECgnP94TSkOVpq4ljxXZ4OKabTyGaTF6CEkYygtRqyA6fhmqlXe13r4DAgAuNgmz_3l4UmBo51ERMJTon8tTtTcYxn_jGxRgZWK9N-cQ-_sxwxvy7oT35QAKJMjqhvyYXhpa9iRVgmfRC7XuxH7VszUBiTkImpaY4JOBZ1iMbML_t6A09lBy9hHljN1rI=&c=HF6wZu_r6mAanzsXEhKLOQll3NHMH49FL1IYxXI_z8Q-h2Z0FWkjbw==&ch=qgNldknItC6bWoYOE9JQvIrY6gKM8OjEVYwhsoK3uMlgaSzPrOwTdA==)

clean_body: 2026 Blue Tsunami is REAL & ROLLING over blood red deep South. | | | | | == Peter, MAGA was just catastrophically blown out in a deep red Louisiana special election. The 2026 Blue Tsunami is REAL & ROLLING over blood red deep South. We come bearing good news from the Bayou: Louisiana is now IN PLAY. In a deep red MAGA country that voted for Trump by 13 points a Democrat didn't just win: she won big --defeating a diehard MAGA Republican w/ Trump's "total and complete" endorsement by 24 points. A 37 pt over-performance (I got chills typing that). The White House had to issue a statement that stated, "Don't Be a Panican. We're Winning" My name is Jamie Davis, I'm the Democrat running for US Senate in Louisiana. My family worked this Delta land when they were slaves and today my son and I farm the very same fields together but now we own them. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | == | Thank you for heeding Louisiana's call. subject: Anything we can say?

body:
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=6324%2E582294%2Efusku4)
Is there anything we can say to **[convince you to support Chris Pappas today?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260426_2.gpiDST&amount=5&refcode=em20260426-2&t=2&akid=6324%2E582294%2Efusku4)**
What if we told you that New Hampshire could decide the Senate majority – and **whether Trump has a rubber stamp for his agenda or a check on his power?**
What if we told you Chris is TIED with his GOP opponent John Sununu – and **political experts no longer consider this a safe blue seat?**
What if we told you Sununu is backed by corporate special interests, right-wing super PACs, and Donald Trump – while **Chris is relying on people like you to chip in?**
**_And what if we told you that falling even a few dollars short right now could be the difference between winning and losing in a race this close?_**
**[Please, chip in $5 or anything you can today to help Chris Pappas surge ahead.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260426_2.gpiDST&amount=5&refcode=em20260426-2&t=3&akid=6324%2E582294%2Efusku4)**

Team Pappas

name: Katie Porter
subject: “Evil, mean, and nasty”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=41222%2E3422442%2EfGztTZ)
Peter, I wanted to share this letter with you:
[](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260426_2_ak.Znhqyg&refcode=em20260426-41222&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=41222%2E3422442%2EfGztTZ)
_I couldn’t agree more, Tyler._
Since taking office, Trump hasn’t made life cheaper or easier—he’s done the opposite.
Costs are up, families are stretched thin, greedy corporations are still squeezing people, **and working Californians are still left behind.**
**Now, I’m up against two Republican candidates who are running to bring that same Trump agenda to California.**
Unlike them, I’m running to _actually_ lower costs—less expensive housing, free child care, and a single-payer health care system.
Our June primary is just five weeks away, and if we’re going to break into the top two that move onto the general election, we need a surge of support right now. We can’t afford to let Trump’s agenda win in California.
**[If you’re able, I’d be honored if you chipped in $5 to my campaign today. Together, we can make sure this kind of “evil, mean, and nasty” politics Tyler described has no place in our state.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260426_2_ak.Znhqyg&refcode=em20260426-41222&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=4&akid=41222%2E3422442%2EfGztTZ)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260426_2_ak.Znhqyg&refcode=em20260426-41222&t=23&akid=41222%2E3422442%2EfGztTZ)
Thank you,
Katie

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20260426-41222&amounts=&t=24&refcode2=41222_3422442_fGztTZ&akid=41222%2E3422442%2EfGztTZ)**

* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4g-em-update-info-footer?source=41222)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=41222&t=26&akid=41222%2E3422442%2EfGztTZ)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026
PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616

body:
# [](https://act.electjon.com/go/4629?t=1&akid=2185%2E5691347%2E_Qn2GL)

Peter,
We’re reaching out to ask if you can contribute $5 to Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign tonight.
Not tomorrow, not next week, but **tonight.** Here’s why:
Donald Trump’s second term has done unfathomable damage to our country and the lives of millions of people. Trump and his MAGA allies are hoping that by overwhelming us with their disastrous agenda, we’ll be too exhausted and demoralized to fight back. _But we won’t be._
This year, we have a real chance to regain Congressional power and restore checks and balances in Washington. But Jon is MAGA’s #1 target. Trump and Republicans know that if they can defeat Jon, the chances of a Democratic Senate majority are finished.
That’s why we’re asking for your help **tonight.** The more contributions that we receive now, the earlier we’ll be able to mobilize people to volunteer, build the resources we’ll need to knock doors across Georgia, and ultimately get people to the polls.
[**If you’re ready to fight for our democracy and send Trump the biggest message possible this November, please consider making a $5 contribution to Jon’s reelection campaign tonight:**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-april26.1MDdBw&refcode=em260426-2-f-b&t=2&akid=2185%2E5691347%2E_Qn2GL)

subject: $1 trillion in cuts to Medicaid by 2034
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=em03132026 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=em03132026]
Peter ,
Last week, RFK Jr. sat in front of Congress defending the Trump administration’s healthcare agenda while Democratic lawmakers grilled him on what their budget would actually do to people who rely on Medicaid, Medicare, and HHS programs.
RFK Jr., of course, refused to tell the truth, but here’s what we know: Trump's agenda could cut $1 trillion from Medicaid by 2034, slash another $15.8 billion from the Department of Health and Human Services, and put 5 to 10 million people at risk of losing Medicaid coverage by 2028.
This is maybe one of the best examples of why it’s ESSENTIAL Democrats take back Congress this November.
A Republican House will champion these cuts, but a Democratic House will stop them.
That is why our number one focus from now until November is supporting the strongest candidates with real opportunities to flip red districts blue and take back the House.
Who holds the majority after November will determine whether Trump’s agenda gets rubber-stamped or challenged every step of the way.
So if you’re able to, chip in today to help us keep building the resources to win these races.
Your donation goes toward the work that actually flips seats – reaching voters who don’t always turn out, having real conversations, and making sure the people who decide these elections show up in November. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpac2026?refcode=em04262026]

name: James Talarico
subject: My mom was fired while she was in the hospital
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter —
On Christmas Day 1990, my younger sister Madeleine was born 10 weeks premature. The doctors told us that, if she survived, she would never walk or talk. Since our mother was in the hospital for so long and didn’t have access to family leave, her boss fired her over the phone.
There she was: no job, mounting medical bills, and a baby fighting for survival. Quite a Christmas.
My sister was in the NICU for eight weeks. My dad took on more work to keep our family afloat and my mom went to the NICU every night to be with Madeleine.
Thankfully, my sister got better. She was eventually released from the hospital. She learned to walk and talk. She became an accountant, and three years ago she became a mom herself to my beautiful niece Jane.
But things have gotten worse for workers in this country.
Billionaire mega-donors and their puppet politicians want you to believe that if we raise the minimum wage, support paid family leave, or allow workers to unionize, it’ll kill jobs and crush the economy. But the evidence doesn’t back that up.
Workers and small businesses keep our economy running. But right now, the deck is stacked against them. Giant corporations get tax write-offs when their CEOs fly on private jets.
It’s why we have to beat them. And it’s why billionaires and corporate donors are going to spend millions of dollars trying to defeat our movement.
They know our movement taking back Texas is the single greatest threat to their power and their profit.
**[So please, if you can, make a contribution today to help us take on the billionaires who are trying to buy this race and install another puppet politician in Washington.](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q2.t_pReT&refcode=em260412-1-ak&t=1&akid=568%2E1347274%2EuE-12w)**

We don’t have to choose between supporting workers and supporting businesses. Doing both is how we ensure hard work is rewarded and every American has the opportunity to earn their way into the middle class — like my mom.
James

clean_body: Peter — On Christmas Day 1990, my younger sister Madeleine was born 10 weeks premature. The doctors told us that, if she survived, she would never walk or talk. Since our mother was in the hospital for so long and didn’t have access to family leave, her boss fired her over the phone. There she was: no job, mounting medical bills, and a baby fighting for survival. Quite a Christmas. My sister was in the NICU for eight weeks. My dad took on more work to keep our family afloat and my mom went to the NICU every night to be with Madeleine. Thankfully, my sister got better. She was eventually released from the hospital. She learned to walk and talk. She became an accountant, and three years ago she became a mom herself to my beautiful niece Jane. But things have gotten worse for workers in this country. Billionaire mega-donors and their puppet politicians want you to believe that if we raise the minimum wage, support paid family leave, or allow workers to unionize, it’ll kill jobs and crush the economy. But the evidence doesn’t back that up. Workers and small businesses keep our economy running. But right now, the deck is stacked against them. Giant corporations get tax write-offs when their CEOs fly on private jets. It’s why we have to beat them. And it’s why billionaires and corporate donors are going to spend millions of dollars trying to defeat our movement. They know our movement taking back Texas is the single greatest threat to their power and their profit. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly. | | ** | | | | | | | ** We don’t have to choose between supporting workers and supporting businesses. Doing both is how we ensure hard work is rewarded and every American has the opportunity to earn their way into the middle class — like my mom. James Paid for by Talarico for Texas Talarico for Texas PO Box 14508 Austin, TX 78761
